# Changelog

All notable changes to Agent Bridge are documented here. This project adheres
loosely to [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/) and tracks
version bumps via the `VERSION` file.

## [0.17.0-beta9] ‚Äî 2026-07-03 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ token-updater lease auto-rotation completes (default-off) + macOS/reliability hardening)

**v0.17.0-beta9 completes the token-updater lease auto-rotation feature** (#21895) ‚Äî all four sub-PRs are now on main: the config surface + setup wizard landed in beta8 (#2245), and this cut lands the lease client (checkout / heartbeat / checkin), the swap-or-defer decision + daemon-tick that drives due-check / rotation on the daemon loop, and the picker-sweep integration (#2252). It ships **default-OFF**: nothing rotates until an operator enables it per host, and reactive-429 rotation remains separately gated behind a 2-host canary. Alongside the marquee, this beta hardens three reliability surfaces ‚Äî a **macOS post-upgrade daemon-recovery safety net** (#2203, re-verified with real launchctl in a disposable macOS VM), **durable idle-nudge re-spool** (#2179), and an **iso-v2 onboarding-layer reconcile** (#2193) ‚Äî plus the **security-cycle-A git-guard** hardening (#2159 / #2163) and the autonomous-operation reminder hooks already landed on main. This is a **mainline beta (prerelease)**: the **`v0.16.x` LTS line stays the `lts` / Latest head** and production installs tracking `lts` are unaffected; a **`v0.16.22` LTS fast-follow** backports the qualifying reliability fixes only (#2179, #2203), and the token-updater lease auto-rotation is deferred to a separate, deliberately-reviewed **`v0.16.23` LTS follow-up** (tracked in #2255) ‚Äî it does not cherry-pick cleanly onto the LTS line and warrants its own conflict-resolution + review rather than a deadline-rushed port. Each underlying fix carried its own codex / patch-dev review; the assembled cut is green on CI with the four cut PRs re-verified CLEAN + head-matched at merge.

### Token-updater lease auto-rotation (#21895 ‚Äî marquee, default-OFF)

- **auth / daemon: token-updater lease auto-rotation ‚Äî sub-PRs 2‚Äì4 (#2252).** The lease client (`claude-token lease` checkout / heartbeat / checkin), the swap-or-defer decision + the daemon tick that drives due-check / rotation on the daemon loop, and the picker-sweep integration. Completes the feature begun in beta8 by #2245 (config surface + setup wizard). **Default-OFF** ‚Äî no behavior change until an operator enables it per host; the reactive-429 rotation path is separately gated behind a 2-host canary and is not enabled by this cut.

### Reliability / platform hardening

- **upgrade: reliably restore the daemon on macOS after `upgrade --restart-daemon` (#2085 / #2203).** Adds post-restart liveness confirmation (a running pid **and** a fresh heartbeat, not just a loaded launchd job) plus a recovery net; on an out-of-band launchd split (the daemon supervised out-of-band of launchd, `bridge_daemon_launchd_restart` rc=2) it **refuses** to fork a competing plain-bash daemon, avoiding the KeepAlive thrash. No-op on the healthy path. Re-verified end-to-end with **real launchctl** in a disposable tart macOS VM (both the drift-recovery and rc=2 out-of-band-split-gate scenarios file-corroborated).
- **daemon: durable idle-nudge re-spool on a post-grace queued-drop (#2179).** A daemon idle-nudge that drops at the post-grace boundary is durably re-spooled instead of being silently lost. Submit primitives are byte-unchanged; no key-send, no task mutation.
- **upgrade: reconcile iso-owned onboarding-layer `SESSION-TYPE.md` copies on migrate (#2193).** Follow-up to #2084 ‚Äî iso-v2 agent-UID-owned onboarding copies are reconciled as the agent user after migrate, clearing the post-upgrade false "agent profile drift" warn. Best-effort, one-way pending‚Üícomplete, shared-mode byte-unchanged.

### Security ‚Äî git-guard cycle A

- **guard: close the #2159 variable-VALUE-hidden git escape via const-tracking (#2241).**
- **guard: #2163 tight config / auth-token indirection backstop (#2242).**

### Operating hooks / agent lifecycle / misc (landed on main since beta8)

- teams-renderer quoteResult (quantity + sub-material spec) + devReq section-label styling ‚Üí 0.1.11 (#2251).
- operating reminders: autonomous inbox-progress with the confirm-first gate intact (#2247), a long-running-task ack reminder folded into the per-turn hook (#2246), and a Claude-only per-turn parallel-dispatch nudge (#2244).
- watchdog: treat an agent-ROOT managed block as present for home-subdir agents (#2018 blast radius / #2243).
- start: non-fatal node-version gate for agents whose plugins declare `engines.node` (#1191 / #2238).
- cron: opt-in `--reconcile` to adopt new default cadences over same-family drift (#2090 / #2237).
- status: exempt idle codex-engine static agents from `stale=crit` (#2100 / #2235).
- smoke: pin lifeline gating envs + pre-assert a real tick to kill the #2207 T7 full-suite flake (#2239 / #2240).
- librarian: guard agent memory / shared captures from the operating-rules SSOT (#2236).

## [0.17.0-beta8] ‚Äî 2026-07-01 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ never-die wave ‚Äî Îç∞Î™¨ ÏÝàÎåÄ Ïïà Ï£ΩÏùå defense-in-depth + ÎèôÏÝÅ‚ÜíÏÝïÏÝÅ converter FR#2061 in-beta QA)

**v0.17.0-beta8 ‚Äî the daemon "never-die" hardening wave, the operator's #1 priority after the 06-30 M4 fleet-down incident**, on top of beta7's rotation *core* fix. The 06-30 RCA (#2209 umbrella) found the daemon could go silently DOWN and stay down across five distinct failure classes: a hung restart failed open, launchd unloaded+disabled drift had no self-heal, the token lifeline died with the daemon (fleet quota-cascade), an interrupted upgrade left version skew + partial state, and a `--no-restart-daemon` reconcile could boot the daemon out. This beta lands the **six-fix defense-in-depth wave** that closes each of those, bundled with the **first-class dynamic‚Üístatic agent converter (FR#2061 + #2216)** ‚Äî shipped for **in-beta QA** per operator direction (live-QA #20517 moves to the beta channel) ‚Äî plus the auto-rotate roadmap (#2217, 5 steps) and standalone reliability fixes already landed on main. This is a **mainline beta (prerelease)**: the **`v0.16.20` LTS stays the `lts` / Latest head** and production installs tracking `lts` are unaffected; a **`v0.16.21` LTS fast-follow will backport the never-die wave only** (features stay mainline). The bundled integration (never-die + converter + main) passed a full-branch codex `implement-ok` on the combination surface and green CI; each underlying fix carried its own codex / patch-dev review.

### Daemon never-die wave (#2209 ‚Äî operator #1, 06-30 fleet-down RCA)

- **daemon: escalate the silence-watchdog on a hung restart instead of failing open (#2208 / Track A #2224).** A restart that hangs (rc124 ‚Üí 300s cooldown) no longer silently swallows without escalation.
- **daemon: self-heal non-operator launchd disabled-drift via per-path markers (#2205 / Track B #2225).** Runtime detection + self-heal for the unloaded+disabled drift class that previously had no recovery path.
- **daemon: out-of-process token lifeline ‚Äî recover-due + sync survive daemon-down (#2207 / Track C #2227).** The token rotation / recover-due / sync lifeline no longer dies with the daemon, closing the fleet quota-cascade.
- **upgrade: restore reconcile-induced daemon bootout under `--no-restart-daemon` (#2210 / Track D #2223).** A layout-v2 reconcile bootout no longer leaves the daemon silently DOWN when `--no-restart-daemon` is set.
- **upgrade: atomic apply-in-progress marker + idempotent resume (#2211 / Track E #2228).** `upgrade --apply` is now atomic / resumable ‚Äî an interruption mid-run no longer leaves version skew + partial state.
- **auth: fail closed on a malformed date-anchored weekly reset stamp (#2222 / Track F).** A malformed weekly-reset stamp fails closed instead of mis-parsing into a wrong window.
- Fast-follow: the **PR-B2 Part2 daemon `active-dead-recover` smoke harness** completion is tracked separately (**#2233**, non-blocker; the WIP `b8dd699e` is inherited-on-main from #2176, a harness gap not a functional regression).

### Agent lifecycle ‚Äî dynamic‚Üístatic converter (FR#2061 + #2216, in-beta QA)

- **agent: first-class `agent convert` verb + config-dir-aware state migration (#2061).** Track A (config-dir migration engine `lib/bridge-agent-convert.sh`), Track B (the `agent convert` verb + ¬ß0.1 last-flip transaction), Track C (`--carry-session` resume-id pin). The live-QA gate (#20517) moves to in-beta testing per operator direction.
- **agent: preserve source model + effort on dynamic‚Üístatic conversion (#2216).** Conversion no longer drops the source model / effort (which had rendered the unavailable `claude-fable-5` default and required a manual re-set).

### Auto-rotate roadmap (#2217) + standalone reliability

- **auto-rotate roadmap #2217 (5 steps):** usage-probe content-age freshness gate (#2214), picker-sweep `--preflight` contract (#2215), hud-tap universality ratchet (#2218), reactive inference/picker `429` ‚Üí preflighted rotation (#2219 ‚Äî **ships flag-OFF**; `BRIDGE_REACTIVE_429_ROTATE_ENABLED` stays a canary gate, cut-independent), cross-layer auto-rotate decision-chain integration smoke (#2221).
- **auth: synchronously converge operator-global creds on `rotate` / `activate --sync` (#2183).**
- **memory: stop the memory-daily backfill self-perpetuating on zero-activity (#2229).**
- **plugin-cache: verify must descend within-marketplace symlinked-dir deps (#2191).**

## [0.17.0-beta7] ‚Äî 2026-06-28 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ #2171 M4 fleet-down rotation regression ÌöåÍ∑Ä ÌîΩÏä§ *core* ‚Äî live-preflight + Îç∞Î™¨ enablement + Ï∏°ÏÝï prefilter + ÌÇ§Ï≤¥Ïù∏ self-heal)

**v0.17.0-beta7 ‚Äî the M4 fleet-down rotation regression is closed at candidate selection time**, on top of beta6's inert `mark-adverse` primitive. The M4 incident (#19460): reactive Claude-token rotation re-picked a stale/limited candidate when the usage probe degraded (403/edge-blocked), and the whole fleet went dark. This beta lands the **core** of the hybrid fix (#2171) ‚Äî four PRs that make the daemon's rotation actually verify a candidate is live before committing it, prefer a measured-fresh candidate, and stop a macOS keychain shadow from silently re-authing a session onto a dead personal token. **Each PR carried a patch-dev `implement-ok` + green Linux CI; PR-D's keychain reconcile went four adversarial review rounds closing a fail-open class at every IO layer.** The remaining **PR-B2 Part2 (daemon `active_dead_recover` for the 401/403 auth-dead marker + blind-nudge suppression) is deferred to a fast-follow** (its authoring fixer died on a transient rate-limit with an unverified harness; the WIP is preserved as draft PR #2176). A **planned `v0.16.19` LTS fast-follow will backport this core** once its release PR lands and verifies green on the LTS line ‚Äî until then the **`v0.16.18` LTS stays the `lts` / Latest head** and production installs tracking `lts` are unaffected; this mainline beta is a **prerelease**. **`Closes #2171`** (core fix; Part2 + LTS backport tracked as fast-follows).

### Auth / credential ‚Äî token auto-rotation (M4 fleet-down core fix, #2171)

- **auth: `rotate` selected-candidate **live preflight** + daemon enablement (#2171 PR-B1 #2172 / PR-B2 Part1 #2175 / #19460).** `cmd_rotate --preflight` live-probes the *selected* rotation candidate before committing the active pointer ‚Äî using the `cmd_check` split (locked snapshot ‚Üí **UNLOCKED** probe ‚Üí locked revalidate ‚Üí commit; never probes under `registry_lock`). Authorizes **only** on a parseable JSON `available`; quota / auth / `403` / timeout / parse-unknown all fail-closed and the ring advances one bounded pass, then revalidates active-id + fingerprint + enabled + still-available before committing (a raced/replaced candidate is deterministically discarded). PR-B1 shipped the capability **opt-in (default-off)**; **PR-B2 Part1 enables it on the daemon's usage-monitor rotate** (`--preflight --preflight-budget 15 --preflight-timeout 6` under a raised 30s ceiling) ‚Äî un-dormanting the live check for the fleet auto-rotation path, with a **global preflight budget** that caps summed ring-probe time and **fails closed** (budget-exhausted unprobed candidates are excluded as `preflight_budget_exhausted`, never committed on stale registry availability; a fully-exhausted pass keeps the existing `skipped:all_tokens_limited` + #1789 envelope). `--preflight` OFF = byte-identical legacy. New smokes `2171-rotate-live-preflight` + `2171-rotate-preflight-budget`.
- **auth: candidate **measured-usage prefilter** + wrong-home cache-split diagnostic (#2171 PR-B3 #2174 / #19460).** `rotation_candidate_availability` (the single eligibility gate) now also skips a candidate whose **own measured `used_percent`** (from the `.usage-cache.json` telemetry, keyed by `_token_signal_digest`) is near-limit (`measured_near_limit`), via a token-free index built **outside** `registry_lock` and injected into the predicate ‚Äî complementary to the live preflight (cheap local pre-filter vs authoritative live check). Additive / fail-OPEN: absent / stale / malformed / no-digest-match ‚Üí existing registry-stamp behavior (no-cache / LTS = no-op). Part 2 adds a wrong-home cache-split **diagnostic** (no-signal only; no alternate-home search). New smoke `2171-prb3-measured-usage-prefilter`.
- **auth: Claude Code **keychain-shadow self-heal** ‚Äî hashed service name reconcile (#2171 PR-D #2173 / #19460).** macOS-only `agb auth claude-token reconcile-keychain` full-enumerates `Claude Code-credentials*` (base **and** the hashed `Claude Code-credentials-<hash>` variant Claude Code v2.1.195 writes) via `dump-keychain`, fingerprint-compares each against the expected active pool token, and reports a stale shadow (a divergent keychain token that makes a session ignore its rotating `.credentials.json` ‚Äî the confirmed sean-m4 RCA). **Default fail-closed diagnostic** (delete nothing, non-zero exit so a health gate notices); `--apply` is the operator-approved cleanup that deletes **only** stale entries. Fingerprint-only ‚Äî the raw secret never reaches stdout / the audit log. The `operator_keychain_credentials_present` sync-global guard now also sees the hashed variant. Four-round review closed a fail-open class: enumeration failure, per-entry read failure, and mixed stale+unreadable sets all now fail closed (a discovered-but-uninspectable entry never reads as clean, never mutates, exits non-zero). New smoke `2171-keychain-shadow-selfheal` (T1‚ÄìT12).

## [0.17.0-beta6] ‚Äî 2026-06-28 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ ÌÜÝÌÅ∞ auto-rotation Í≤∞ÏÝïÎ°ÝÏÝÅ-Î≥µÍµ¨ groundwork + #19146 fixer-escape Í∞ÄÎìú)

**v0.17.0-beta6 ‚Äî the deterministic active-dead *primitive* for token auto-rotation, plus the fixer-escape Bash guard**, on top of beta5. The marquee is the **`claude-token mark-adverse` registry primitive (#2168 / #19460 Fix 1 PR-A)** ‚Äî the **foundation** for deterministic active-dead recovery, NOT the recovery behavior itself yet. It deterministically stamps a real observed `429`/quota or `401`/`403` onto the token row **without a network probe**, so the cascade (`rotation_candidate_availability`) skips a known-dead token *before* the next selection instead of blindly re-picking it: `quota_limited` disables + stamps the reset window (`mark-quota` parity), `auth_failed` adverse-stamps only (**never permanently disabled** ‚Üí the cascade skips it for the bounded adverse-freshness TTL, then fails OPEN), and a token-id + fingerprint guard drops a stale capture from a since-replaced token. **The daemon wiring that calls it ‚Äî the nudge-path `active_dead_recover` (persist adverse ‚Üí rotate `--sync` ‚Üí converge operator-global ‚Üí only then nudge, park on pool-exhaustion) ‚Äî is PR-B and is NOT in this beta**; `mark-adverse` ships here as the inert, fully-tested CLI primitive that PR-B will build on. Bundled with the **#19146 PreToolUse Bash guard (#2157)** ‚Äî a fail-closed DENY of destructive `git` (reset/checkout/clean/branch-D/‚Ä¶) escaping a dispatched-fixer worktree into the operator's primary checkout ‚Äî and a README sync for the v0.16.18 LTS cut (#2166). This is a **mainline beta (prerelease)**: the **`v0.16.18` LTS stays the `lts` / Latest head**; production installs tracking `lts` are unaffected. Each PR carried its own codex / patch-dev review; the full Linux CI gate is green.

### Auth / credential ‚Äî token auto-rotation (deterministic active-dead *primitive*)

- **auth: `claude-token mark-adverse` ‚Äî deterministic active-dead registry primitive, no network probe (#2168 / #19460 Fix 1 PR-A).** `agb auth claude-token mark-adverse <id> --status quota_limited|auth_failed [--reset-at <iso>] [--fingerprint <fp>] [--api-error-status <s>] [--source <text>] [--json]` stamps a real observed failure onto the token row so the rotation cascade skips a known-dead token before the next selection (the M4 fleet-down root shape: rotation re-picked / blind-nudged a dead active). `quota_limited` mirrors `mark-quota` (disable + `limited_until`/`disabled_until`/`next_check_at` reset window so `recover-due` re-enables on reset); `auth_failed` adverse-stamps only (`last_check_status=auth_failed` + `last_checked_at` + a bounded `next_check_at`) and **never permanently disables**, so the candidate is skipped for the adverse-freshness window then fails OPEN. Token-id + fingerprint guarded (a stale capture from a since-replaced token is skipped as `token_replaced`, never written); fingerprint-only logging. Routed through the `bridge-auth.sh` wrapper for daemon/automation callers. New smoke `19460-mark-adverse` (6 cases incl. wrapper plumbing + cascade-skip integration). **Groundwork only** ‚Äî the daemon `active_dead_recover` that consumes it is PR-B (deferred).

### Security

- **hooks: PreToolUse Bash guard ‚Äî fail-closed DENY of destructive git escaping a fixer worktree into the operator's primary checkout (#19146 / #2157).** A dispatched-fixer session is confined to its own `.claude/worktrees/...` worktree, but nothing structurally stopped a `git reset` / `checkout` / `switch` / `restore` / `clean` / `branch -D` / `worktree remove` / `reflog expire` / `gc --prune` aimed at the operator's primary checkout ‚Äî via `-C`, `--git-dir`, an env wrapper, a `VAR=` prefix, or a `cd` that realpath-escapes the worktree. `hooks/tool-policy.py` now **fail-closed DENIES** that shape (only the bare `git <verb>` inside the session's own worktree is permitted); operator sessions (cwd at the repo root) are structurally exempt, and the hook fails OPEN on its own crash so it stays defense-in-depth.

### Docs

- **docs: README top-block sync for the v0.16.18 LTS cut (#2166).** Current stable / LTS ‚Üí v0.16.18, recommended-target + leap path bumped, CHANGELOG [0.16.18] mirrored to main so the README anchor resolves.

## [0.17.0-beta5] ‚Äî 2026-06-27 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ ÎèôÏÝÅ ÌÜÝÌÅ∞ auto-rotation enable + #17927 P2 + A2A/teams/discord/plugin Î¨∂Ïùå)

**v0.17.0-beta5 ‚Äî the #18849 dynamic-token auto-rotation feature is now ENABLE-able on the v0.17 line**, on top of beta4. `v0.16.17` shipped the rotation machinery (Part 1 / Part 1b) **gated default-OFF / INERT**, but the only documented opt-in ‚Äî `config set-env BRIDGE_CLAUDE_GLOBAL_AUTH_SYNC=1` ‚Äî was a **dead path** (the key was never on the `set-env` allowlist), so the gate could never actually be turned on. beta5 closes that gap (**#2155**) and lands the **Part 2** daemon threshold-gate + cascade, making seamless mid-session token rotation a real, operator-enableable capability ‚Äî bundled with the #17927 P2 statusLine usage feed and an A2A / teams / discord / plugin reliability set. This is a **mainline beta (prerelease)**: the **`v0.16.17` LTS stays the `lts` / Latest head** and production installs tracking `lts` are unaffected ‚Äî this is the v0.17 feature line. Each PR carried its own internal codex review; the full Linux CI gate is green.

### Auth / credential ‚Äî dynamic token auto-rotation (#18849, now enable-able)

- **‚òÖ auth/config: a sanctioned, headless-safe enable path for global auth-sync ‚Äî the dead opt-in is closed (#19140 / #2155).** Adds `auth claude-token global-auth-sync enable|disable|status [--json]` (mirrors the keychain-free verb): a **persisted, audited, daemon-inherited** runtime-config bool, so the gate is now `effective = persisted OR (BRIDGE_CLAUDE_GLOBAL_AUTH_SYNC == "1")` and the daemon follows the **effective** state. `status --json` reports `enabled / persisted_enabled / env_override_enabled / effective_source ‚àà {runtime-config, env, both, default}`; `disable` clears the persisted bool but **warns** when an env override still wins (never a false full-disable). The previously-missing symmetric **`config unset-env KEY`** verb lands behind the same `resolve_config_caller` trust gate as `set-env` (denylist-first ‚Üí allowlist-required, sibling-preserving, idempotent, distinct audit; exit codes mirror `set-env`). `BRIDGE_CLAUDE_GLOBAL_AUTH_SYNC` is added to the env allowlist as a strict **`flag_one`** literal (canonical `"1"`-only ‚Äî `0`/`true`/empty/whitespace/control rejected), and the shared-UID caller-writable deny message is **platform-aware** (drops the iso-v2 migration suggestion off Linux). The admin-verb hook allowlist admits **only** `global-auth-sync` via a standalone validator ‚Äî the token-bearing `add/activate/sync/rotate/receive` validators and the credential read-carveout are **byte-unchanged**. This is what makes #18849 actually turn-on-able.
- **auth: daemon threshold-gate + cascade rotation ‚Äî #18849 Part 2 (#2147, #2148).** The Stage-2 work `v0.16.17` deferred: the daemon now **gates rotation on token attribution** (#2147) and **cascades to the next-available token with warn-on-exhaustion** when a token is spent (#2148) ‚Äî the consumer side that makes the Part 1 seamless-rotation primitive useful across the fleet.
- **auth: identity-sync for global auth rotation ‚Äî Part 1b / 1b-v2 (#2144, #2150 closes #2145).** Converges the displayed identity with the verified account after a rotation; #2150 adds an operator-provided `account_email` identity source so convergence no longer blocks on a `user:profile`-scoped token (the prior INERT no-op path).
- **claude-token: daemon-core statusLine usage feed ‚Äî #17927 P2 (#2132).** Feeds the per-token usage/limit signal (E5/E6/E8/E10) into the daemon-core statusLine so quota state is observable.

(The Part 1 seamless-rotation writer (#2143) shipped in `v0.16.17` INERT and is carried forward on this line. The `v0.16.17` keychain-free auth-hijack hardening (#2137 / #2138 / #2141) is likewise present on the v0.17 mainline.)

### Channels

- **teams: advance the conversation reference only after confirmed delivery (#2134).** The bot's stored conversation reference (used to proactively reach a chat) is now advanced **only after a send is confirmed delivered**, so an unconfirmed / failed send no longer poisons the reference ‚Äî a reliability follow-on to the #2112 reply delivery-proof.
- **discord/thread-session: one-time thread-created awareness signal to the main session (#14577 / #2133).** When a Discord thread (mapped to an agent sub-session) is first created, a single awareness signal is delivered to the main session so it knows the thread/sub-session exists ‚Äî emitted exactly once, not on every message.
- **channels: suppress singleton channel plugins on a disposable `claude -p` ‚Äî telegram poller self-theft (#17957 / #2128).** A disposable `claude -p` invocation no longer spins up the singleton channel plugins (e.g. the telegram poller), which had let the throwaway process **steal updates** from the real long-running channel session.
- **plugins: `marketplace.json` ‚Üî `plugin.json` version sync + a version-sync guard (#18177 / #2135).** The beta4 packaging slip (`marketplace.json` pinned teams `0.1.1` while `plugin.json` was `0.1.8`) made `agb plugins seed` a no-op; `marketplace.json` is bumped to `0.1.8` to match and a new **version-sync guard** prevents the two from drifting apart again.

### A2A / reliability

- **a2a: re-exec the receiver after detach to fix the macOS NAT64 `getaddrinfo` segfault / receiver crash-loop (#2140 / #2146, fleet-down).** `serve --detach` double-forked **without** `exec`, so the reconcile loop's `resolve_bind` ‚Üí `getaddrinfo` ran in a fork-polluted grandchild ‚Üí `SIGSEGV` on macOS NAT64 (a receiver crash-loop that took the A2A leg down on an affected host). The launcher now binds + proves the listener synchronously, then **re-execs a fresh interpreter** that adopts the proven listener instead of re-binding; the untrusted-remote surface (HMAC / source-addr / allowlist / dedupe) is unchanged and the fd adoption is fail-closed. (Same fix that shipped as the `v0.16.17` LTS backport.)
- **picker-sweep: don't misclassify the codex 'Update available' picker as a cwd-confirm (#2117 / #2136).** The picker-sweep no longer treats the codex "Update available" picker as a cwd-confirm prompt, so it is dismissed correctly instead of being answered as the wrong picker kind.

### CI / docs

- **ci: add an `lts-line-ancestry-check` gate for `release/0.16-*` drift (#2153).** A new CI gate verifies `release/0.16-*` branch ancestry so an un-verified force-push or bad merge that breaks the LTS-line ancestry is caught at PR time (the recurring branch-hygiene class).
- **docs(CLAUDE): require reading the actual codex completion note as a separate step before acting (#2154).** Codifies the hard rule that a reviewer's completion note must be read from its real `completion_note_file:` path ‚Äî never inferred or pattern-matched ‚Äî before recording or acting on the verdict.

## [0.17.0-beta4] ‚Äî 2026-06-26 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ devStatus card renderer + claude-token clock-recovery + resolver crash-loop backports)

**v0.17.0-beta4 ‚Äî the Teams devStatus Í∞úÎ∞úÌòÑÌô© Adaptive Card renderer, plus the v0.17 backports of the claude-token clock-based recovery (#17927 P0) and the resolver crash-loop fix (#2106), on top of beta3.** Three PRs, each with a codex `implement-ok` on green Linux CI.

### Channels

- **teams: devStatus Adaptive Card renderer ‚Äî the Í∞úÎ∞úÌòÑÌô© card (#17992, #2129).** A third `cardintent` kind alongside `quoteResult` + `devReqAutofill`: one Container per dev-product (a `ÏÝúÌíà ¬∑ ÏÉÅÌÉú` Accent header + a status badge colored from the ÏÉÅÌÉú row value ‚Äî ÏßÑÌñâ Ï§ë‚ÜíAccent / Î≥¥Î•ò‚ÜíWarning / Ï∂úÏãú ÏôÑÎ£å‚ÜíGood / ÎìúÎ°≠‚ÜíAttention ‚Äî + an 8-field FactSet rendered verbatim, literal `‚Äî` preserved). The card-level `openDevStatusDetail` action is a renderer-supplied domain-pinned `Action.OpenUrl` (`/d/?screen=dev-status`; the cardintent's `payload.url` is ignored ‚Üí zero open-redirect); `Action.Submit` is never emitted. ¬ß10 is inherited unchanged and the quoteResult + devReqAutofill paths are byte-for-byte unchanged. teams plugin **0.1.7 ‚Üí 0.1.8**. The card is **dormant** until the bot is reseeded to 0.1.8 (a pre-0.1.8 bot falls back to the `fallbackMarkdown` table ‚Äî no regression).

### Reliability / credential (v0.17 backports)

- **claude-token: parse the named-tz weekly reset + clock-based recovery (#17927 P0, #2125 ‚Üí #2127).** v0.17 backport of the operator-mandated fix: `parse_reset_at` now parses the real weekly-429 string `resets Jul 1 at 12pm (Asia/Seoul)` (`at` separator + abbreviated month + named timezone via `zoneinfo`, graceful fallback) and stamps `limited_until`; recovery becomes clock-authoritative (a quota-limited token re-enables once its reset stamp passes, without requiring the unreliable `claude -p` probe; a still-future-capped token stays disabled). Fixes the headless-server weekly-quota tokens that never auto-recovered.
- **resolver: align resume config-dir with launch for shared agents (#2106, #2122 ‚Üí #2124).** v0.17 backport of the CRITICAL fleet-host-down fix: a shared admin agent no longer infinite-crash-loops on `claude --resume` (the resume resolver now returns the same per-agent `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` that launch pins, scoped to non-iso shared+agent_home agents; iso agents return earlier and are unaffected) + an honest quarantine log.

## [0.17.0-beta3] ‚Äî 2026-06-25 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ Teams reply delivery-proof + devReqAutofill renderer + rooms migration)

**v0.17.0-beta3 ‚Äî a Teams reply reliability fix (a production silent non-delivery), the Í∞úÎ∞úÏùòÎ¢∞ draft Adaptive Card renderer, and the #2079 rooms admin migration robustness, on top of beta2.4.** Three PRs, each with a codex Phase-4 `implement-ok` on green Linux CI.

### Channels

- **teams: the `reply` tool now surfaces the send outcome + emits an audit row (#2112, #2119).** The reply text-only path returned `sent: <chatId>` as soon as `sendActivity` awaited, discarding the Bot Framework `ResourceResponse.id` ‚Äî so a reply the SDK accepted but never delivered read as success (a production customer agent saw 14 silent non-deliveries). A new pure, unit-tested `outbound-result.ts` classifier turns the send outcome into a three-way result: **confirmed** (non-empty id) ‚Üí `sent: ‚Ä¶ message_id=‚Ä¶` (backward-compatible), **unconfirmed** (awaited OK, empty id) ‚Üí `ok:false` + `teams_reply_unconfirmed:` (never a bare `sent:`), **failed** (threw) ‚Üí `teams_reply_failed:`. A single-line `teams_outbound_reply` audit row (with newline+length sanitization on every interpolated field) makes outbound delivery observable. teams plugin **0.1.6 ‚Üí 0.1.7**.
- **teams: devReqAutofill Adaptive Card renderer ‚Äî the Í∞úÎ∞úÏùòÎ¢∞ draft card (#17471, #2116).** A second `cardintent` kind alongside `quoteResult`: a per-project grouped draft (an empty-rows section opens a per-project `emphasis` Container, content sections nest, a trailing `‚öÝ Î≥¥ÏôÑ` section becomes a warning Container) with renderer-supplied domain-pinned `Action.OpenUrl` deeplinks for `confirmDevReq` / `editDevReq` (the action payload's entity-id is never placed in the url ‚Üí zero open-redirect surface). `Action.Submit` is never emitted (Phase-2 trusted-handler gate). ¬ß10 is inherited unchanged and the quoteResult path is byte-for-byte unchanged. **Dormant** until crm-dev's `preview_card` emit lands. teams plugin **0.1.5 ‚Üí 0.1.6**.

### A2A / rooms

- **rooms: robust `room_members.admin` migration + local-admin backfill + leader rebroadcast (#2079, #2113 ‚Üí #2114).** Backport to the v0.17 line of the rooms admin-migration hardening: `_migrate_schema` PRAGMA-prechecks each column and swallows only the duplicate-column race (a lock / readonly / disk error now re-raises instead of silently leaving the column missing); the reconcile roster-epoch step backfills this node's admin bits from the local configured admin id (local-node rows only, empty config leaves `-1`, never infers a remote admin) and, as the leader, atomically bumps the epoch + enqueues a durable roster rebroadcast.

## [0.17.0-beta2.4] ‚Äî 2026-06-25 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ Teams quoteResult live-feedback fixes)

**v0.17.0-beta2.4 ‚Äî two operator live-feedback fixes to the (now lit) Teams `quoteResult` card, on top of beta2.3.** Renderer-only; codex Phase-4 `implement-ok` (findings 0) on green CI.

### Channels

- **teams: add the RFQ FactSet row + suppress the duplicate prose on card success (#2111).** The per-RFQ stack gains an `RFQ` FactSet row (matched from the server's existing `RFQ` row; missing ‚Üí em dash). On **successful** card render the visible message text is now suppressed (`text: ''`) so the card no longer renders alongside the legacy 10-column markdown table ‚Äî the card is the content, the prose was a duplicate. This also **strengthens ¬ß10**: the success path now carries zero visible text (no leak surface), while the failure path keeps the ¬ß10-scanned text fallback and the no-fence path is byte-for-byte unchanged. teams plugin **0.1.4 ‚Üí 0.1.5**.

## [0.17.0-beta2.3] ‚Äî 2026-06-25 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ Teams quoteResult light-up-ready)

**v0.17.0-beta2.3 ‚Äî the Teams `quoteResult` Adaptive Card is light-up-ready: a 6-field per-RFQ layout + a renderer-supplied deeplink, on top of beta2.2.** Still **dark-launched / dormant** (`AC_QUOTE_RESULT_CARD` defaults OFF) ‚Äî a beta2.3 install sees **zero behavior change** until the operator opts in; light-up is a separate, gated step (renderer reseed + server flag + sign-off). Three renderer PRs redesign the quoteResult card and extend the ¬ß10 fail-closed surface to the visible card prose; each carried a full codex Phase-4 pair-review (`implement-ok`, findings 0) on top of green Linux CI.

### Channels

- **teams: ¬ß10 fail-closed scan of the visible card prose + Korean/literal forbidden list (#2104).** `renderOutbound` previously scanned only the rendered card bytes ‚Äî a forbidden cost term in the human-visible prose accompanying a card (outside the `cardintent` fence) was sent unscanned. It now also scans the visible text and **hard-replaces** it with a fixed ¬ß10-clean fallback on a hit (both the success and the failure paths), and the forbidden list is extended with the Korean/literal contract terms (`ÏÝúÏãúÍ∞Ä`/ÏõêÍ∞Ä/ÎßàÏßÑ/Í≥µÌóåÏù¥Ïùµ/ÏòÅÏóÖÏù¥Ïùµ/ÎÑ§Í≥ÝÏú®/ÌöåÏàòÏú®/ÏûëÏóÖÏû•Î™Ö/ÏûÑÎ•Ý/Í≥ÝÍ∞ùÏöîÏ≤≠Í∞Ä/manufacturingCostSuggested/standardCost). The no-fence path stays byte-for-byte unchanged.
- **teams: quoteResult LIST ‚Üí 6-field per-RFQ stack + Phase-1a contract primitives (#2107).** The quoteResult LIST is redesigned to the operator's #17138 6-field, mobile-safe **per-RFQ stack** (`Í≥ÝÍ∞ù ¬∑ ÏÝúÌíà` header + `Ïö©Îüâ` / `Îû©ÎÑòÎ≤Ñ` + `ÎÇ¥Ïö©Î¨º Í≤¨ÏÝÅ` / `Í∞ÄÍ≥µÎπÑ Í≤¨ÏÝÅ`), **superseding** the dormant beta2.2 3-col `Í≤¨ÏÝÅ ÏÜåÍ≥Ñ` / `ÏÇ∞Ï∂úÏÉÅÌÉú` layout. Adds the Phase-1a contract primitives: **PriceCell string-only** (raw decimals rejected ‚Äî zero path for a raw cost number), **`Action.OpenUrl` domain-pinned**, and **`Action.Submit` rejected** in Phase 1a. AC v1.2 only (no `Table` / `targetWidth`, ColumnSet ‚â§3).
- **teams: renderer-supplied quoteResult deeplink url (#2108).** The `[ÏÝÑÏ≤¥ Í≤¨ÏÝÅÍ≤∞Í≥º Î≥¥Í∏∞]` deeplink URL is now **supplied by the renderer** (`https://<allowed-host>/d/?screen=rfq-list`, host configurable) and any cardintent-supplied URL is **ignored** ‚Äî eliminating the open-redirect surface (smuggled / off-domain / `javascript:` URLs all resolve to the pinned destination). The server emits the action id only.
- teams plugin **0.1.2 ‚Üí 0.1.4** (version-triggered reseed loads the new renderer).

## [0.17.0-beta2.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-25 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ Teams Adaptive Card layout)

**v0.17.0-beta2.2 ‚Äî the Teams `quoteResult` Adaptive Card renders its multi-RFQ LIST as a compact, mobile-safe ColumnSet table, on top of beta2.1.** The card feature stays **dark-launched / dormant** (`AC_QUOTE_RESULT_CARD` defaults OFF) ‚Äî a beta2.2 install sees zero behavior change until a later opt-in light-up. This cut is renderer/layout only: the additive `renderOutbound` seam, fence parse/strip, fail-closed CardIntent validation, and the ¬ß10 forbidden-cost-key golden are unchanged. Carried a full codex pair-review convergence (r1‚Üír3 `implement-ok`) on top of green Linux CI.

### Channels

- **teams: quoteResult LIST ‚Üí compact AC 1.2 ColumnSet table + col2 = `Í≤¨ÏÝÅ ÏÜåÍ≥Ñ`, bump 0.1.2 ‚Üí 0.1.3 (#2101).** The multi-RFQ LIST now renders as a scannable ColumnSet "table" ‚Äî a header row + one row per RFQ (`Í≥ÝÍ∞ù¬∑ÏÝúÌíà | Í≤¨ÏÝÅ ÏÜåÍ≥Ñ | ÏÉÅÌÉú`, ‚â§3 columns) ‚Äî instead of a per-RFQ FactSet stack, so multiple quotes scan record-by-record. AC **1.2** only (no `Table` / `targetWidth`; ColumnSet wraps gracefully at a narrow mobile width per the #15157 mobile-LCD envelope ‚Äî AC 1.5 `Table` was rejected for mobile). Column 2 matches the **stable `Í≤¨ÏÝÅ ÏÜåÍ≥Ñ`** quote-subtotal label (both spaced + unspaced spellings), not the role-scoped cost labels and not `ÌôïÏÝïÍ∞Ä` (which live RFQs leave unset ‚Üí an all-em-dash column); column 3 matches the stable `ÏÇ∞Ï∂úÏÉÅÌÉú` only (a generic `ÏÉÅÌÉú` row no longer leaks). `valueState`‚ÜíÎ¨∏Íµ¨¬∑ÏÉâ preserved (calculating‚Üí`(Í≥ÑÏÇ∞Ï§ë)`, masked‚Üí`‚óè‚óè‚óè`, notRequested‚Üí`(Ìï¥ÎãπÏóÜÏùå)`, missing‚Üíem dash) with no raw-value leak through the new column path; the detail layout (named-section FactSets) is unchanged. The plugin version bump makes a fleet reseed **version-triggered** (clean refresh on restart). **No runtime behavior change** ‚Äî ships dormant. (The forbidden-key golden remains the contract-derived placeholder pending the vendored `forbidden_cost_keys.gen.json` hash-pin.)

## [0.17.0-beta2.1] ‚Äî 2026-06-25 (mainline ¬∑ beta ¬∑ restart-safety hotfix)

**v0.17.0-beta2.1 ‚Äî a fleet restart-safety hotfix on top of beta2.** beta2's Teams Adaptive Card renderer added `bun-types` as a type-only `devDependency` for its typecheck. The plugin-cache seed treats every nested `package.json` ‚Äî including `node_modules/bun-types/package.json` ‚Äî as a **channel-required** contract file, but the node_modules copy skipped `bun-types`, so the `teams` plugin **failed to link into the cache** (`linked-failed`, criticality=channel-required). The practical blast radius (confirmed in cm-prod soak) is broader than a blocked re-seed: a **channel-required `linked-failed` teams plugin bricks any bot (re)start** ‚Äî the bot exits immediately after launch. `always_on` bots survive only while never restarted (`--no-restart`); a daemon crash / upgrade / kill would brick them. This release removes the hazard.

### Channels / packaging

- **teams: drop the `bun-types` type-only devDependency + bump 0.1.1 ‚Üí 0.1.2 (#2097).** `bun-types` is removed from `devDependencies` (bun provides `bun:test` natively at runtime; it was only needed for `tsc`), so `node_modules` no longer carries it and the seed contract has nothing to miss ‚Äî the channel-required `teams` plugin links clean (`linked-verified`) and bots start normally. A minimal `bun:test` ambient shim keeps `bun run typecheck` passing without the package. The plugin version bump makes a fleet reseed **version-triggered** (clean refresh) rather than force-only ‚Äî the same-version staleness that masked the regression. **No runtime/renderer change**: the Adaptive Card renderer is byte-unchanged and remains **dark-launched / dormant** (`AC_QUOTE_RESULT_CARD` defaults OFF). The deeper plugin-cache seed contract bug ‚Äî which can recur for any plugin declaring a type-only or symlinked dependency ‚Äî is tracked separately ([#2098](https://github.com/seanssoh/agent-bridge-public/issues/2098)).

## [0.17.0-beta2] ‚Äî 2026-06-24 (mainline ¬∑ beta)

**v0.17.0-beta2 ‚Äî a Teams Adaptive Card renderer (dark-launched / dormant) on top of beta1, plus the v0.16.16 stabilization fixes that landed on `main` after beta1, folded into the v0.17 line.** Like beta1 this is a live-test vehicle, not a promotion ‚Äî the `v0.16.x` LTS line continues independently and is **not** this prerelease. The Adaptive Card feature ships **dormant**: the CRM core defaults its `AC_QUOTE_RESULT_CARD` flag OFF, so the renderer is inert until a later opt-in light-up ‚Äî a beta2 install sees **zero behavior change** until then. Each PR carried its own internal codex review; this cut adds a full orchestrator Phase-4 pair-review on the new feature (which caught and fixed three fail-closed/spoofing bugs before merge) on top of green Linux CI.

### Channels

- **Teams Adaptive Card `quoteResult` renderer ‚Äî `cardintent` fenced pass-through, Model B (#2096).** The `@agent-bridge/teams` plugin now extracts a `cardintent` fenced block from the agent's outbound reply text and renders it as a Microsoft Adaptive Card v1.2 attachment (FactSet / ColumnSet ‚â§3 columns, no `Table`/`targetWidth`, `valueState`‚ÜíÎ¨∏Íµ¨¬∑ÏÉâ mapping) alongside the text. **Additive + fail-closed**: with no fence the existing reply is byte-for-byte unchanged; any parse / validation / ¬ß10 / render failure degrades to graceful text-only (never throws into the send); and the raw fenced JSON is always stripped from what the user sees. CardIntent validation is fully fail-closed ‚Äî string-typed closed enums for `actionId` / `valueState`, and a payload may not override the validated `actionId` ‚Äî and a ¬ß10 forbidden-cost-key golden scans the rendered card bytes. Ships **dark-launched / dormant**. (The forbidden-key list is a contract-derived placeholder pending the vendored `forbidden_cost_keys.gen.json` hash-pin.)

### Folded v0.16.16 stabilization (from `main`, post-beta1)

The seven v0.16.16-line fixes that landed on `main` after the beta1 cut, folded into the v0.17 line (each merged to `main` with its own green CI + codex / patch review; the `ci-select` required-set was union-merged exactly, with zero dangling smoke tokens):

- **#2091 (#1990)** ‚Äî hooks: per-writer unique temp for the concurrent settings-render race (ends the `plugin_mcp_liveness_restart_failed` flap).
- **#2089 (#1653)** ‚Äî upgrade: clear a spurious conflict sidecar that holds no recovery.
- **#2088 (#1789)** ‚Äî daemon: pool-level cooldown for saturated claude-token rotation.
- **#2086 (#1650)** ‚Äî ms365: always send `offline_access` on the refresh-token grant.
- **#2083 (#16309)** ‚Äî config: allowlist `BRIDGE_QUEUE_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` as a tunable env knob.
- **#2092** ‚Äî ci: restore green after #2089 (raw-pathlib noqa + heredoc baseline row).
- **#2094** ‚Äî docs: the LTS branch is protected; force-pushes are CI-gated.

## [0.17.0-beta1] ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (mainline ¬∑ beta)

**v0.17.0-beta1 ‚Äî the first cut of the v0.17 feature line (beta ‚Äî features still landing).** This forks the mainline from `v0.16.16-rc2` and carries the `v0.16.16-rc3` hardening already on `main`; the **v0.16.x LTS line continues independently** (the `lts` / Latest head is unchanged and is NOT this prerelease). As a beta it is a live-test vehicle, not a promotion ‚Äî expect further v0.17 features to land on top before a stable v0.17.0. First-batch features (each carried its own internal codex review at park time; this cut is the integration gate ‚Äî full Linux CI + codex integration review):

### Channels

- **bridge-official Discord plugin ‚Äî vendored, generalized thread-as-session (#2060).** A first-party Discord channel plugin under `plugins/discord/` (server + thread-session dispatcher/guard/egress, access + configure skills, docs). A Discord thread maps to an agent sub-session with workdir binding; the `thread_session_guard` is a PreToolUse guard that fails-closed on reads of the channel transport credentials (`.discord/.env` / `.telegram/.env`), with a self-test proving the deny survives tmp carve-outs and path-normalization evasion. Generalizes the thread-as-session pattern so the same machinery serves other thread-capable channels.

### Reliability / fallback

- **Provider-health outage oracle ‚Äî Anthropic-outage detection for Codex fallback (P1a, #2066; master-gated OFF).** A daemon-owned 1-prober/N-readers oracle (`bridge-provider-health.py` + `lib/bridge-provider-health.sh` accessors + a gated `cmd_sync_cycle` tick + the `bridge-usage-probe.py` outage classifier) that detects a real Anthropic outage ‚Äî scoped-on-first-report, DNS-guarded (never blame Anthropic for a local network failure), N-of-M static-agent fleet quorum, recovery hysteresis, and 429/auth explicitly NOT outage-class. **Steady state = zero probes**: the whole tick is a hard NO-OP unless `BRIDGE_FALLBACK_ENABLED` is on AND the oracle has pending work, so a production install pays nothing. Nothing consumes the DOWN state yet ‚Äî this is the detection foundation for the Anthropic-outage ‚Üí Codex fallback that P1b (cron) and P3 (live) will build on. Daemon-state writes are O_EXCL-serialized; the stale-lock break is meta-lock-serialized so it is race-free even on the portable no-fcntl path (no lost report under concurrent eviction).

### A2A

- **agent‚Üínode discovery (whois) + `send --peer auto` + a `known_agents` roster column (#2025).** `agb a2a whois <agent>` resolves which node(s) an agent lives on by consulting the rooms registry; ambiguity (the same agent name on >1 node) is never collapsed ‚Äî every candidate is listed, never guessed. `agb a2a send` with `--peer auto` (or omitted) auto-resolves the node from `--to` via the same whois lookup and fails closed (with the candidate list) on an ambiguous target or an incomplete room roster; an explicit `--peer` is still honored verbatim with no whois. `a2a peers list` (plain + `--json`) gains a `known_agents` node‚Üíagents column so an operator can see the node‚Üîagent mapping at a glance. No secret material leaks into `whois` / `peers` output.

## [0.16.22] ‚Äî 2026-07-03 (LTS)

**v0.16.22 ‚Äî a reliability fast-follow on the LTS line** (the `lts` / Latest head, supersedes `v0.16.21`). It backports two qualifying reliability fixes from the `v0.17.0-beta9` mainline cut ‚Äî nothing else.

### Reliability ‚Äî macOS post-upgrade daemon recovery + durable idle-nudge re-spool (LTS backport)

- **upgrade: reliably restore the daemon on macOS after `upgrade --restart-daemon` (#2085 / #2203).** Adds post-restart liveness confirmation (a running pid **and** a fresh heartbeat, not just a loaded launchd job) plus a recovery net; on an out-of-band launchd split (the daemon supervised out-of-band of launchd, `bridge_daemon_launchd_restart` rc=2) it **refuses** to fork a competing plain-bash daemon, avoiding KeepAlive thrash. No-op on the healthy path. An **upgrade/restart-breakage** class fix ‚Äî qualifies for LTS backport. Re-verified end-to-end with **real `launchctl`** in a disposable tart macOS VM (both the drift-recovery and rc=2 out-of-band-split-gate scenarios file-corroborated).
- **daemon: durable idle-nudge re-spool on a post-grace queued-drop (#2179).** A daemon idle-nudge that drops at the post-grace boundary is durably re-spooled via the existing pending-attention path instead of being silently lost (idempotent: at most one spooled copy, re-derived against the live queue, dropped once the task is claimed). Fixes the ~30-min-latency fleet-wide "idle-agent nudges lost ‚Üí time-sensitive tasks unclaimed" regression. Submit primitives are byte-unchanged; no key-send, no task mutation.

**Scope: reliability only.** The token-updater lease auto-rotation (mainline `v0.17.0-beta9`, default-OFF) is **deferred to a separate `v0.16.23` LTS follow-up** (tracked in #2255) ‚Äî it does not cherry-pick cleanly onto the LTS line and warrants its own conflict-resolution + review rather than a deadline-rushed port. The v0.17 feature roadmap (auto-rotate, dynamic‚Üístatic converter, ¬ß10 lighting) stays mainline; features are not LTS-backported. Both backported fixes carried a codex / patch-dev `implement-ok` on mainline, and the ported commits' net effect was proven line-for-line identical to the reviewed mainline change (lts-line-ancestry gate).

> Note: this is the mainline-mirrored copy of the v0.16.22 LTS entry (short heading); the canonical release entry lives on the `release/0.16-lts` line. The v0.16.22 release itself (VERSION + tag) shipped from `release/0.16-lts` (merge `eb3ead3b`), not `main`.

## [0.16.21] ‚Äî 2026-07-01 (LTS)

**v0.16.21 ‚Äî the daemon "never-die" hardening wave is backported to the LTS line**, the new `lts` / Latest head (supersedes `v0.16.20`). After the 06-30 M4 fleet-down incident (#2209 RCA), the daemon could go silently DOWN and stay down across five distinct failure classes: a hung restart failed open, launchd unloaded+disabled drift had no self-heal, the token lifeline died with the daemon (fleet quota-cascade), an interrupted upgrade left version skew + partial state, and a `--no-restart-daemon` reconcile could boot the daemon out. This backports the same six-fix defense-in-depth wave shipped on `v0.17.0-beta8` (mainline) ‚Äî a **fleet-host-down regression** class that qualifies for LTS backport. **Scope: never-die only** ‚Äî the v0.17 auto-rotate roadmap, the dynamic‚Üístatic converter, and ¬ß10 lighting stay mainline (features are not LTS-backported). The F#2222 test was scoped to its **#2204 date-anchored assertions** (the LTS line has no #17927 bare-clock branch, so those main-only assertions were omitted); the `parse_reset_at` fail-closed fix + all six fixes re-verified on the v0.16 line. Each fix carried a codex / patch-dev `implement-ok` on mainline.

### Daemon never-die wave (#2209 ‚Äî 06-30 fleet-down RCA, backport)

- **daemon: escalate the silence-watchdog on a hung restart instead of failing open (#2208 / #2224).** A restart that hangs (rc124 ‚Üí 300s cooldown) no longer silently swallows without escalation.
- **daemon: self-heal non-operator launchd disabled-drift via per-path markers (#2205 / #2225).** Runtime detection + self-heal for the unloaded+disabled drift class.
- **daemon: out-of-process token lifeline ‚Äî recover-due + sync survive daemon-down (#2207 / #2227).** The token rotation / recover-due / sync lifeline no longer dies with the daemon.
- **upgrade: restore reconcile-induced daemon bootout under `--no-restart-daemon` (#2210 / #2223).** A layout-v2 reconcile bootout no longer leaves the daemon silently DOWN.
- **upgrade: atomic apply-in-progress marker + idempotent resume (#2211 / #2228).** `upgrade --apply` is now atomic / resumable ‚Äî an interruption mid-run no longer leaves version skew + partial state.
- **auth: fail closed on a malformed date-anchored weekly reset stamp (#2222).** `parse_reset_at`'s date-anchored branch fails closed (`""`) on malformed input like the bare-clock branch. Backport test scoped to the #2204 date-anchored set.

> Note: this is the mainline-mirrored copy of the v0.16.21 LTS entry (short heading); the canonical release entry lives on the `release/0.16-lts` line. The v0.16.21 release itself (VERSION + tag) shipped from `release/0.16-lts` (merge `d8d14842`), not `main`.

## [0.16.20] ‚Äî 2026-06-29 (LTS)

**v0.16.20 ‚Äî the #2182 fleet-critical plugin-cache fresh-build race is closed on the LTS line**, the new `lts` / Latest head (supersedes `v0.16.19`). On a `v0.16.19` upgrade that bumped a channel plugin's version (teams `0.1.1‚Üí0.1.2`), an agent that did **not** already have that cached version fresh-built its per-agent plugin cache on launch ‚Äî and a race partially copied the `@azure` dependency (8-11/12, non-deterministic). The required-contract check then found `node_modules/@azure/core-client/package.json` missing, aborted the launch with `criticality=channel-required`, and the verify-retry loop **reused the partial cache** instead of rebuilding it ‚Üí the agent was left `stopped` (a **permanent wedge**). This is a straight cherry-pick of the `main` fix (PR #2184); the smoke (T1-T6) and the called helpers were re-verified green on the v0.16 line.

### Plugins ‚Äî per-agent cache build (fleet-down hotfix, #2182)

- **plugins: atomic temp-build + winner-guarded retire for the per-agent plugin cache (#2182 / PR #2184 backport).** `bridge-dev-plugin-cache.py` no longer assembles a fresh cache **in place** (which exposed a half-built `node_modules` to a concurrent agent that then skipped it). `_atomic_build_cache_version` builds the whole tree into a pid-private temp (`.<ver>.tmp.<pid>`, same-filesystem), verifies the required contract on the temp, and only then publishes with `os.rename` ‚Äî so the live path only ever transitions to a **complete** cache (the temp is cleaned on success, concurrent-winner, and failure). An **incomplete existing** cache is no longer reused: `_retire_cache_if_incomplete` re-verifies immediately before acting (never deleting a complete cache another agent has since published), retires only the exact version path via a single `os.rename` to a pid-private sibling, then rebuilds atomically ‚Äî breaking the permanent verify-retry wedge. The required-contract is deliberately **not** narrowed (that would hide genuine incomplete copies); the fix guarantees a *complete* copy instead. macOS bash 5.3 + Linux bash 5.2 smoke `2182-plugin-cache-atomic-build` (T1-T6) green; carried a `main` codex `implement-ok` + green Linux CI, byte-identical (shared patch-id) to the `main` fix.

> Note: this is the mainline-mirrored copy of the v0.16.20 LTS entry (short heading); the canonical release entry lives on the `release/0.16-lts` line. The v0.16.20 release itself (VERSION + tag) shipped from `release/0.16-lts` (merge `25b8f70d`), not `main`.

## [0.16.19] ‚Äî 2026-06-28 (LTS)

**v0.16.19 ‚Äî the #2171 / #19460 M4 fleet-down rotation regression is closed on the LTS line**, the new `lts` / Latest head (supersedes `v0.16.18`). The M4 incident: reactive Claude-token rotation re-picked a stale/limited candidate when the usage probe degraded (403 / edge-blocked), and the whole fleet went dark. This backports the **core** of the hybrid fix (the same four PRs shipped on `v0.17.0-beta7`) ‚Äî the daemon now live-probes a rotation candidate before committing it, prefers a measured-fresh candidate, and stops a macOS keychain shadow from silently re-authing onto a dead personal token. **The remaining PR-B2 Part2 (daemon `active_dead_recover` for the 401/403 auth-dead marker + blind-nudge suppression) is a tracked fast-follow.** Each PR carried a patch-dev `implement-ok` + green Linux CI on mainline; this LTS backport re-verified all four M4 smokes green on the v0.16 line, plus a small classifier backport (below) the cherry-picks needed.

### Auth / credential ‚Äî token auto-rotation (M4 fleet-down core fix, #2171)

- **auth: `rotate` selected-candidate live preflight + daemon enablement (#2171 PR-B1 / PR-B2 Part1 / #19460).** `cmd_rotate --preflight` live-probes the *selected* candidate before committing the active pointer (locked snapshot ‚Üí UNLOCKED probe ‚Üí locked revalidate ‚Üí commit; never under `registry_lock`); authorizes only on a parseable JSON `available`, fail-closed on quota / auth / 403 / timeout / parse-unknown, bounded one ring pass, revalidate-at-commit. PR-B2 Part1 enables it on the daemon's usage-monitor rotate with a **global preflight budget** (caps summed ring-probe time, fail-closed on exhaustion ‚Üí existing `skipped:all_tokens_limited` + #1789 envelope). `--preflight` OFF = byte-identical legacy.
- **auth: candidate measured-usage prefilter + wrong-home cache-split diagnostic (#2171 PR-B3 / #19460).** The single eligibility gate also skips a candidate whose own measured `used_percent` (keyed by `_token_signal_digest`) is near-limit (`measured_near_limit`), via a token-free index built outside `registry_lock`. Additive / fail-open (absent / stale / malformed / no-match ‚Üí existing registry-stamp behavior). Part 2 cache-split diagnostic only.
- **auth: Claude Code keychain-shadow self-heal ‚Äî hashed service name reconcile (#2171 PR-D / #19460).** macOS-only `reconcile-keychain` full-enumerates `Claude Code-credentials*` (base + hashed variant), fingerprint-compares against the active pool token, reports a stale shadow. Default fail-closed diagnostic; `--apply` is operator-gated. Four-round review closed a fail-open class (enumeration / per-entry read / mixed unreadable all fail closed). Fingerprint-only.
- **auth: backport `classify_token_kind` / `active_registry_token_kind` to the v0.16 line (#18696 / #2141 helpers).** The PR-B1 live-preflight gates OAT-native probing on the token kind; the classifier shipped on mainline (#2141) but was not on the v0.16 line, so the cherry-picked preflight needed it. Two self-contained prefix-classifier helpers + their kind constants, no new dependencies.

> Note: this is the mainline-mirrored copy of the v0.16.19 LTS entry (short heading); the canonical release entry lives on the `release/0.16-lts` line. The v0.16.19 release itself (VERSION + tag) shipped from `release/0.16-lts` (merge `d7c7f799`), not `main`.

## [0.16.18] ‚Äî 2026-06-27 (LTS)

**v0.16.18 ‚Äî token auto-rotation becomes *enable-able* on the v0.16 LTS line (the fleet-delivery cut).** The operator fleet is pinned to the `lts` channel, so this backport is the **only path that delivers seamless token rotation to the fleet at all** ‚Äî v0.16.17 carried the Stage-1 machinery but the actual enable path was dead (INERT, no persisted operator switch) and Part 2 (the daemon-driven rotation) was absent. This cut closes that deployability gap: it backports the **`global-auth-sync` enable verb (#19140)** ‚Äî a first-class, persisted, headless-safe operator switch for the operator-global seamless credential propagation (so a rotated token reaches a dynamic-vanilla Claude agent without a restart) ‚Äî plus **#18849 Part 2 (daemon threshold-gate + cascade)** so the daemon actually rotates an `auto_rotate`-enabled token before exhaustion. **It is backported DEFAULT-OFF for fresh installs via two independent gates: the daemon registry rotation (threshold/cascade) runs only for a token whose per-token `auto_rotate` is enabled (off by default), and the operator-global seamless propagation is additionally gated on `global-auth-sync` ‚Äî so a fresh fleet that has enabled neither sees exactly zero rotation and zero live token-behavior change. (An install that had already enabled `auto_rotate` will see registry rotation on that pre-existing opt-in; `global-auth-sync enable` governs only whether a rotated token seamlessly propagates to the operator-global creds.)** Bundled with the **#19146 PreToolUse Bash guard** (security class ‚Äî structurally blocks a dispatched-fixer from escaping its worktree into the operator's primary checkout with a destructive git command). This supersedes `v0.16.17` as the v0.16.x line head; the `lts` / `stable` / Latest markers advance here, and because the new rotation is default-OFF the upgrade is safe for every existing fleet install. Each bundled lane carried its own codex / patch-dev adversarial review, sits as a verified descendant of `v0.16.17`, and the full Linux CI gate is green.

### Auth ‚Äî fleet token rotation (Stage 2, backported DEFAULT-OFF)

- **auth: first-class `global-auth-sync enable|disable|status` verb ‚Äî close the dead enable path (#19140 / #2155 backport, marquee).** v0.16.17 shipped the Stage-1 seamless-rotation writer but left it reachable only through the `BRIDGE_CLAUDE_GLOBAL_AUTH_SYNC` env gate + an `auto_rotate` config flag ‚Äî there was no operator-facing, persisted way to turn it on, so on the LTS line the feature was effectively un-deployable. `agb auth claude-token global-auth-sync enable|disable|status` now does a persisted, atomic gate write (headless-safe, no longer dependent on a live `set-env` allowlist), so an operator can actually opt a fleet into the seamless propagation of a rotated credential. This is the operator-facing switch for the operator-global seamless credential propagation (so a rotated token reaches a dynamic-vanilla Claude agent without a restart); the daemon registry rotation itself is gated separately, per-token, on `auto_rotate`.
- **auth/daemon: Part 2 ‚Äî daemon threshold-gate + cascade-to-next-available + warn-on-exhaustion (#18849 Part 2 / #2147 / #2148 / #17927-P2 backport).** Backports the daemon-driven half of the rotation design: the daemon proactively rotates the active credential once usage crosses the **95% threshold** (so the swap happens before a hard exhaustion stalls the session), **cascades to the next available token** when the current one is spent, and surfaces a **warn-on-exhaustion** signal when the pool has nothing left to rotate to. **Backported DEFAULT-OFF for fresh installs** ‚Äî the daemon attempts a rotation only for a token whose per-token registry `auto_rotate` is enabled (off by default), so a fresh fleet sees no threshold gating, no cascade, and no behavior change. (`global-auth-sync enable` is the separate gate governing whether a rotated token seamlessly propagates to the operator-global creds ‚Äî not whether the rotation itself runs.) Pairs the Part-1 dirfd/`flock` writer hardening already on the LTS line.
- **auth: operator-email identity source for Part 1b ‚Äî converge LTS identity-sync from probe-inert to operator-email (#2150 / Part 1b-v2).** The Stage-1 identity-sync follow-up converged the displayed identity with the verified account, but on the LTS line it was a probe-only no-op pending a `user:profile`-scoped token. This switches the LTS identity source to the **operator-email identity**, so a rotation converges the displayed identity without depending on the un-backported profile scope. Still gated under the same default-OFF rotation switch.

### Security

- **hooks: PreToolUse Bash guard ‚Äî fail-closed DENY of destructive git escaping a fixer worktree into the operator's primary checkout (#19146 / #2157 backport).** A dispatched-fixer session is meant to be confined to its own `.claude/worktrees/...` worktree, but nothing structurally stopped a `git reset` / `checkout` / `switch` / `restore` / `clean` / `branch -D` / `worktree remove` / `reflog expire` / `gc --prune` aimed at the operator's primary checkout ‚Äî via `-C`, `--git-dir`, an env wrapper, a `VAR=` prefix, or a `cd` that realpath-escapes the worktree ‚Äî which is how WIP loss / branch-state collisions have happened. `hooks/tool-policy.py` now **fail-closed DENIES** that shape (allowlist: only the bare `git <verb>` inside the session's own worktree is permitted); operator sessions (cwd at the repo root, not under `.claude/worktrees`) are structurally exempt, and the hook fails OPEN on its own crash so it stays defense-in-depth. Structural prevention of the incident class.

### Notes

- **Default-OFF is load-bearing for this cut.** The marquee capability ships reachable but inert behind two independent gates: the daemon registry rotation is gated per-token on `auto_rotate` (off by default), and the operator-global seamless propagation is additionally gated on `global-auth-sync`. Upgrading to v0.16.18 changes **no** fresh fleet's token behavior; an install that had already enabled `auto_rotate` keeps rotating on that pre-existing opt-in, and `global-auth-sync enable` governs only the seamless propagation of a rotated token. Conservative / production installs tracking the `lts` channel with `auto_rotate` off advance to this head with zero rotation-behavior delta.
- **Known follow-up #2164 (non-blocking, scoped to the post-opt-in path).** Once `global-auth-sync` is enabled, a daemon rotation does not yet *synchronously* converge the operator-global creds ‚Äî a quality gap in the seamless-propagation timing for opted-in installs, not a default-off risk for fresh / un-enabled fleets.
- Ancestry verified as a descendant of `v0.16.17`; each lane (#2155 / #2160 verb, #2147 / #2148 / #2162 Part 2, #2150, #2157 / #2161 guard) passed its codex / patch-dev gate, and the full Linux CI gate is green on the assembled head.

## [0.16.17] ‚Äî 2026-06-27 (LTS)

**v0.16.17 ‚Äî promotion of the `v0.16.17-rc1` security line to an official LTS release, bundled with the Stage-1 auth-rotation + A2A fleet-reliability fixes that landed during the rc1 soak.** This makes the **#2137 keychain-free auth-hijack root-fix** official on the v0.16.x LTS line (soaked as rc1 since 2026-06-26), and folds in a fleet-down A2A receiver crash-fix (#2146) plus the **Stage-1 (Part 1) seamless token-rotation machinery (#18849)**, which ships **gated default-OFF / INERT** so it is safe to land on the LTS line without changing live behavior. Part 2 of the auth-rotation work (daemon threshold-gate + cascade) is **Stage 2 and is NOT in this cut**. This supersedes `v0.16.17-rc1` as the v0.16.x line head; conservative / production installs tracking the `lts` channel advance here. Each bundled PR carried its own codex / patch-dev adversarial review; the full Linux CI gate is green.

### Security

- **auth: scope-guard the keychain-free backfill + fail-closed `keychain-free enable` ‚Äî promoted to official (#2137 / #2138).** The single-fix security patch that shipped as `v0.16.17-rc1` is promoted to the official `v0.16.17` LTS release after the sean-mac soak + joint-test. Every broad-scope keychain-free write (`sync` / `backfill-settings` / daemon backfill) now consults `bridge_auth_apikeyhelper_allowed_for` and **excludes the admin / attached-interactive agent** from the managed `apiKeyHelper` (so a live admin can't be silently moved onto API-key billing ‚Üí an "Invalid API key" break) while still syncing its OAuth credential; the admin opts in only via an explicit `--agents <admin>`. `--help` no longer mutates, unknown flags fail closed, and a sanctioned `claude-token keychain-free enable|disable|status` verb does an atomic gate write with a fail-closed enable-time preflight + a settings-write audit row. Acceptance smoke `2137-keychain-free-scope-guard` (A1‚ÄìA8); `1855-keychain-free-backfill` no-regression. (No change from the rc1 base ‚Äî promotion only.)

### A2A / fleet reliability

- **a2a: re-exec the receiver after detach to fix the macOS NAT64 `getaddrinfo` segfault / receiver crash-loop (#2146 / #2140, fleet-down).** `serve --detach` double-forked **without** `exec`, so the reconcile loop's `resolve_bind` ‚Üí `getaddrinfo` ran in a fork-polluted grandchild ‚Üí `SIGSEGV` on macOS NAT64 (a receiver crash-loop that took the A2A leg down on an affected host). The launcher now binds + proves the listener synchronously, then **re-execs a fresh interpreter** (`--already-detached` + `--inherited-listen-fd`) that adopts the proven listener instead of re-binding ‚Äî only interpreter provenance changed; the per-tick bind re-prove still runs every reconcile. The untrusted-remote surface (HMAC / source-addr / allowlist / dedupe) is unchanged and the fd adoption is fail-closed. New smoke `2140-handoffd-detach-reexec` (passes here, fails against the pre-fix base ‚Äî a real discriminator). (patch-dev A2A-bar adversarial review: implement-ok.)

### Auth (Stage 1 ‚Äî gated default-OFF / INERT)

- **auth: gated file-based seamless token rotation for dynamic-vanilla Claude ‚Äî Stage 1 / Part 1 (#18849 / #2143).** Lands the machinery for a seamless mid-session credential rotation (rotate-while-running ‚Üí one prompt picks up the new token, no restart) for a dynamic-vanilla Claude agent on the operator-global `~/.claude.json`. **Double-gated default-OFF** (requires BOTH `auto_rotate` AND `BRIDGE_CLAUDE_GLOBAL_AUTH_SYNC`); the writer is a dirfd-pinned no-follow + `flock`'d PATCH (preserves `refreshToken` / unknown keys), root fail-closed, invalid-JSON / no-oauth fail-closed, fingerprint-only output, identity detection-only. Three rounds of codex security review each caught + closed a real TOCTOU (`.lock`-through-symlink, parent-swap-after-lock, lock-dir‚âÝwrite-dir) ‚Üí a single pinned `dir_fd` threaded through the whole read‚Üícheck‚Üíwrite; smoke 14/14. Real auth is unaffected while the gate is off.
- **auth: identity-sync for global auth rotation ‚Äî Stage 1 / Part 1b (#18849 / #2144).** The identity-sync follow-up that converges the displayed identity with the verified account after a rotation, shipped **INERT** (operator-approved dark-launch, default-OFF). Convergence is gated on a `user:profile`-scoped token (tracked by #2145); until that scope is present it is a documented, diagnosable no-op (the `no_scope` reason is surfaced in `doctor` / `global-auth-status`), and the fail-safe path was canary-verified (a `403 no_scope` ‚Üí no write, no corruption). The identity writer reuses the Part 1 dirfd hardening; an r2 token-replace race was closed by recheck + persist + `~/.claude.json` write all under a single held `registry_lock`.
- **Note:** Part 2 of #18849 (daemon threshold-gate + cascade rotation) is **Stage 2 and is not in this cut.**

## [0.16.16] ‚Äî 2026-06-26 (LTS)

**v0.16.16 ‚Äî promotion of the v0.16.16 LTS line (rc1‚Äìrc4, soaked 2026-06-17 ‚Üí 2026-06-25) to an official LTS release, and the LTS endpoint of the v0.16.x line.** Marquee is a **Teams reply silent-non-delivery fix (#2112)** ‚Äî a production customer agent reported replies that the SDK accepted but never delivered while the reply tool reported success ‚Äî bundled with the full rc1‚Äìrc4 reliability/hardening set below. This **supersedes `v0.16.15` as the `lts` / Latest head**; conservative / production installs tracking the `lts` channel advance here. With this LTS endpoint cut, **mainline development moves to `v0.17`**: the v0.17 feature line (bridge-official Discord plugin, Teams Adaptive Cards, and more) merges to `main`, so `main` now carries the latest features while the `lts` channel stays pinned to this line. Each bundled PR carried its own codex / Phase-4 review; the full Linux CI gate is green.

### Channels

- **teams: the `reply` tool surfaces the send outcome + emits an audit row (#2112 / #2120).** The reply path returned `sent: <chatId>` on SDK await alone, discarding the Bot Framework `ResourceResponse.id` ‚Äî an accepted-but-undelivered reply read as success. A pure unit-tested `outbound-result.ts` classifier now returns a three-way result (**confirmed** ‚Üí `sent: ‚Ä¶ message_id=‚Ä¶` / **unconfirmed** (empty id) ‚Üí `ok:false` + `teams_reply_unconfirmed:` / **failed** ‚Üí `teams_reply_failed:`) + a sanitized single-line `teams_outbound_reply` audit row. teams plugin **0.1.1 ‚Üí 0.1.2**.
- **ms365: always send `offline_access` on the refresh-token grant (#1650 / #2086).** Ensures a durable refresh token so the M365 session survives without recurring re-auth.

### A2A / rooms

- **rooms: robust `room_members.admin` migration + local-admin backfill + leader rebroadcast (#2109 / #2113).** `_migrate_schema` PRAGMA-prechecks each column and re-raises lock / readonly / disk errors (only the duplicate-column race is swallowed); the reconcile roster-epoch step backfills this node's admin bits from the local configured admin id (local-node only, empty config ‚Üí `-1`, never a remote admin) and, as the leader, atomically bumps the epoch + enqueues a durable roster rebroadcast.

### Daemon / reliability

- **daemon: pool-level cooldown for saturated claude-token rotation (#1789 D2 / #2088).** Prevents rotation thrash when the token pool is saturated.
- **upgrade: clear a spurious conflict sidecar that holds no recovery (#1653 / #2089).** Stops a phantom conflict marker from blocking an upgrade.
- **hooks: per-writer unique tmp for settings render ‚Äî fix the #1990 liveness/render race (#2091).** Closes a concurrent settings-render corruption window.
- **config: allowlist `BRIDGE_QUEUE_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` as a tunable knob (#16309 / #2083).**

### CI / docs

- **ci: restore main green after #2089 (#2092)** and **docs(release-lines): the LTS branch is protected ‚Äî force-pushes are CI-gated (#2094).**

## [0.16.16-rc3] ‚Äî 2026-06-23 (LTS ¬∑ release candidate)

**v0.16.16-rc3 ‚Äî the third release candidate on the v0.16.16 LTS line: it carries everything in rc2 plus the wave of issues the rc2 soak surfaced (10 issues / 8 tracks, #2040-#2048 + #2051) AND the managed-block data-loss / interrupted-upgrade / daemon-safety / A2A-onboarding fixes plus two operator-mandated A2A cross-server features that landed after the original rc3 assembly (#2062, #2064, #2065, #2070, #2075, #2076, #2078, #68, #2079, #16247).** Like rc1/rc2 this is a soak vehicle, not a promotion ‚Äî `v0.16.15` stays the `lts` / Latest head and this prerelease is **not** marked Latest. The batch is daemon/launchd reliability (the #2040 enabled-but-unloaded recovery + the #2051 admin self-restart split-brain guard + the #2064 interrupted-upgrade daemon re-enable), a **fleet-wide managed-block data-loss guard (#2062 / #1814)**, picker/cron/session correctness, doc-backfill, a root-cause auth fix (the #2048 ms365 cross-process refresh lock that ends the operator's recurring ~3h M365 re-auth), the #2065 false crash-loop-alarm retirement on a launch-cmd change, the A2A self-service-onboarding correctness fixes, a structural live-leak safety guard (#68), and ‚Äî **operator-mandated onto the LTS line for the live multi-server rollout** ‚Äî A2A cross-server admin‚Üîadmin routing authz (#2079) and A2A DNS-hostname peer keying (#16247). Each PR carried its own internal codex review; the rc3 cut is the integration gate (full Linux CI + per-fix codex / patch adversarial review).

### Daemon / launchd reliability

- **Recover an enabled-but-unloaded launchd/systemd daemon (#2040).** A daemon that exited graceful-TERM with its launchd job left **enabled-but-unloaded** (KeepAlive moot) stayed silently down for hours ‚Äî outside the #2030 sleep-wake and #1563 in-tick-wedge paths that rc2 covered. **Part A** hardens the upgrade restore (`_bridge_upgrade_launchd_restart_daemon`): poll-until-not-loaded before `bootstrap` (defeats the async-`bootout` race), retry the transient launchd errors, capture stderr, **verify the job actually loaded**, and on failure emit a loud non-swallowed WARN + exact remediation + a `load_state` in the upgrade summary (systemd parity verifies `is-active` for the daemon service + liveness timer). **Part B** gives the independent `bridge-daemon-liveness` watcher a standing recovery: when the daemon is gone AND the job is **enabled-but-unloaded**, it re-bootstraps it (cooldown-gated + audited) ‚Äî tri-state **fail-closed** on a `disabled` job so it never fights an operator `agb daemon stop`. launchd + `systemctl --user` parity. (cm-prod `patch` adversarial review: implement-ok-for-scope; user-scope systemd confirmed.)
- **Refuse an admin self-restart (split-brain guard) (#2051).** When the agent *issuing* a restart was also the target (an admin restarting its own session), the controller died mid-flight ‚Äî the kill/launch ran inside the process being killed ‚Äî so the kill‚Üíconfirm-dead‚Üílaunch handoff had no surviving supervisor and could leave **two live instances of one identity** (double-claimed tasks, conflicting state writes, the `ensure_singleton ec=1` restart-storm bursts seen in the #2040 RCA). `run_restart` now refuses the caller-identity self-restart (`BRIDGE_AGENT_ID == target`) with a redirect to the configured `restart_peer` (new `declare -Ag BRIDGE_AGENT_RESTART_PEER` roster map) or a "restart manually" fallback, and exits non-zero before any kill. This is scoped strictly to the caller-identity case: restarting a *different* agent is fully unchanged, and the #1853 detached-survival path for the TMUX-session-only self-restart (where `BRIDGE_AGENT_ID` is unset) is preserved. A foot-gun guard, not a security boundary.

### Picker / cron / session

- **picker-sweep cron registers on macOS/non-iso instead of failing every upgrade (#2041, #2042).** The default picker-sweep registration unconditionally used `--kind shell --run-as-agent`, which a non-iso/macOS install can't resolve (iso v2 is Linux-only) ‚Üí silent failure + a `failed` line re-logged on every upgrade. Registration is now platform-aware (reusing the exact `bridge_cron_validate_shell_run_config` gate): non-iso converges to a working text-kind job (idempotent, no perpetual re-fail), iso/Linux keeps shell-kind unchanged, and the cron-create stderr is no longer swallowed.
- **picker auto-resolve no longer flags a roster-claude ACTIVE assistant turn as unknown_stuck (#2043).** The `unknown_stuck` detector escalated an admin claude agent mid-turn (its live "Working" output mistaken for a stuck picker). It now reuses the #1409 active-work signal to exclude a live claude assistant turn; a genuinely-stuck unknown picker (stable, no live banner) still escalates.
- **self/loopback task-create wakes in seconds, not minutes (#2045, #2046).** A `task create --from X --to X` (a Discord thread sub-session enqueuing to its own main session) got no create-time wake push (a `from!=to` guard suppressed it) and no fresh-arrival marker (only the A2A receiver posted it) ‚Üí ~6-minute periodic-nudge latency. The create-time push now fires for loopback (disambiguated from a self-nudge-mid-turn by the existing target-session busy gate ‚Äî a busy target still spools), and the local enqueue posts the fresh-arrival marker.
- **Producer-only sub-sessions skip the consumer inbox-auto-drain Stop hook (#2047).** A producer-only leg (enqueues to its parent, no queue consumption, `agb` transport-guarded) that shared the main's `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` inherited the consumer Stop hooks and looped demanding drains it couldn't perform. `compute_drain_decision` now honors a `BRIDGE_AGENT_LEG` producer marker and skips the drain (a real consumer is never mis-skipped).

### Channels / auth

- **ms365 cross-process refresh_token lock ‚Äî ends the recurring ~3h M365 re-auth (#2048).** Root-caused (joint crm-dev + cm-prod `patch` runtime RCA) as a cross-process `refresh_token` double-consume: the ms365 plugin's in-process `SingleFlight` doesn't serialize across the MCP server, the `get-valid-token` CLI, and an external grant actor, so two processes rotate the same single-use AAD RT ‚Üí `invalid_grant` ‚Üí forced re-auth. The refresh grant is now wrapped in a cross-process lock keyed on `tokenPath(upn)` with **re-read-after-acquire** (skip the redundant grant if another holder already rotated a fresh token), bounded acquisition timeout + stale-lock reclaim, no token material in the lockfile, portable (`bun` `O_EXCL`, not `flock(1)`). Concurrent-refresh smoke proves exactly one grant / zero `invalid_grant`. Pairs with the cosmax-crm hook delegating to `get-valid-token` instead of its own grant.

### Doc-backfill

- **engine-detection trusts roster authority over filesystem codex residue (#2044).** The codex-AGENTS hygiene pass re-flagged 6 roster-declared **claude** agents as `detected=codex` (stale `AGENTS.md` residue), regenerating a hygiene task every pass (4+ recurrences). A roster-authoritative non-codex agent now takes a `hold-quiet` path (no recurring task; residue flagged report-only), idempotent across passes; real codex agents still detected. Distinct from #2016's emission gate.

### Managed-block data-loss + upgrade / A2A onboarding / daemon safety (landed after the original rc3 assembly)

- **Managed-block hardening ‚Äî fleet-wide symlink-clobber data-loss guard + SSOT unify + ghost-ref gate + pointer-block completion (#2062 / #1814 #1815 #1816, DATA-LOSS).** The renderer's `sync_memory_schema_from_template` did `target.write_bytes(template_bytes)`, which **followed a `MEMORY-SCHEMA.md -> canon` symlink and would overwrite the canonical doc with the smaller template fork ‚Äî fleet-wide, in a single apply pass**. `#1814` adds the data-loss fix (refuse to write through a symlink, with a TOCTOU re-check) and unifies COMMON-INSTRUCTIONS + MEMORY-SCHEMA onto their `docs/agent-runtime/*` canon body via a shared `_render_canon_doc` primitive (render-to-`shared/` + per-home symlink, retiring the divergent per-home template fork). `#1815` drops ghost references (the `HEARTBEAT.md` bullet present in 0/N homes, the dead `CHECKLIST.md`) and existence-gates `references/`. `#1816` cuts the remaining `CLAUDE.md` hardcopy down to canon pointers single-sourced from the engine render, version-stamps the `BEGIN` marker so a stale/foreign block is mechanically detectable (back-compat: `MANAGED_START_RE` matches stamped + unstamped), guards an unbalanced (orphaned-`BEGIN`) block (skip + report, never double-prepend), and makes backups changed-only. Three new mutation-proven regression smokes; end-to-end `apply` verified on throwaway homes.
- **Re-enable the daemon job if an upgrade is interrupted between quiesce-disable and restore-enable (#2064 / #2055).** The #2040 liveness watcher fail-closed **skips a *disabled* launchd job** (never fight an operator `agb daemon stop`). But the launchd upgrade quiesce `disable`+`bootout`s the job *before* the reconcile and re-`enable`+`bootstrap`s it *after* ‚Äî so an upgrade **killed** (SIGKILL / power-loss) mid-window leaves the job **disabled+unloaded**, indistinguishable from an operator stop, and the watcher correctly-but-unhelpfully leaves the daemon down until the next manual start.
