A durable `state/upgrade/daemon-quiesce.intent` marker (atomic tmp+mv, records the upgrade pid + platform) brackets the quiesce window and is the discriminator the watcher lacked: marker + **dead** pid ‚Üí interrupted ‚Üí re-enable + recover; marker + **live** pid ‚Üí in flight ‚Üí defer; **no marker** + disabled ‚Üí operator stop ‚Üí **stay down** (#2040 contract preserved). Two self-heal layers (the upgrade EXIT handler for a catchable abort + the marker for the uncatchable crash). Also fixes a pre-existing latent errexit bug: under `set -euo pipefail` the bare reconcile-driver aborted on every non-zero rc, making the #1820 fail-closed `case` unreachable for the benign rc=2 "nothing to do" ‚Äî captured via `|| _reconcile_rc=$?` so rc 0/2 proceed + restore, rc 1/3 fail closed. 13-scenario mutation-proven smoke; #2040/#655/#1905/#1820 smokes stay green.
- **Retire a stale crash/report.env on a launch-cmd change ‚Äî stop the false crash-loop alarm on a healthy agent (#2065 / #2063).** A leftover `runtime/crash/report.env` from a prior launch era kept driving false crash-loop alarms (~200√ó/12h `crash_notified_origin_suppressed` + a spurious "Crash loop detected" urgent) on a **healthy** agent after its launch command changed (dynamic‚Üístatic convert, `reclassify`, `update --set-launch-cmd`). The daemon now, before alarming, compares the recorded launch base against the agent's **current** roster base (`CRASH_LAUNCH_CMD_RAW` via the side-effect-free `bridge_agent_launch_cmd_raw` + a `shlex`-tokenize signature helper, no `eval`): on a positive mismatch it auto-retires the report + emits one `crash_report_retired_stale_launch_cmd` audit; a genuine ongoing crash rewrites the report with the current base so a matching base still alarms. Fail-safe bias ‚Äî empty/unparseable/cross-engine/degenerate signals ‚Üí incomparable ‚Üí **keep alarming** (full `CRASH_*` family sanitized before sourcing, so a truncated report can't inherit a prior iteration's launch cmd). Part 2: `ack-crash` retires a valid report when the agent is healthy + a new `clear-crash` verb. 8-case mutation-proven smoke (incl. cross-iteration-leak teeth).
- **Structural live-leak guard ‚Äî refuse to tick the LIVE runtime from a transient source checkout (#68).** A wave-orchestration fixer / CI checkout isolates *files* (a git worktree) but inherits the controller env, so `BRIDGE_HOME` still points at the operator's live `~/.agent-bridge`; a fixer that ran an individual daemon smoke / `bridge-daemon.sh run` directly then ticked the live runtime from a `/private/tmp` or `.claude/worktrees` scratch tree, and on cleanup of that tree the live daemon's `BRIDGE_SCRIPT_DIR` dangled (the #946 cascade) ‚Äî the operator's daemon went down. A fail-closed guard now refuses a state-mutating daemon verb (`start`/`ensure`/`run`/`restart`/`sync`/`run-cron-worker`) when the source checkout is transient (mktemp / system-tmp / fixer worktree, incl. the managed `~/.agent-bridge/worktrees` root and the macOS `/private/var` canonicalization) AND `BRIDGE_HOME` is a persistent install ‚Äî **before any live dir is created**. Help forms (`<verb> --help`) stay read-only (no refuse, no mkdir); normal dev/prod, upgrade self-respawn, and fully-isolated smokes all pass; explicit escape hatch `BRIDGE_ALLOW_FOREIGN_CHECKOUT=1`. (4-round codex adversarial review + reproduced/verified on a Linux container.)
- **A2A auto-join: self-heal a stale token-bootstrapped local peer + a stale-invite diagnosis instead of an opaque `bad_signature` (#2076 / #2073).** A joiner whose first contact failed while receiver autojoin was off had already written a local leader peer from that now-stale invite; a freshly-minted invite then skipped bootstrap and signed with the stale secret ‚Üí an opaque `401 bad_signature` that read like a crypto/secret-mismatch and stalled onboarding. The joiner now refreshes the stale token-bootstrapped local peer from a new signed invite (bounded to the explicit new-invite path), and unknown/expired/revoked-token receiver rejects collapse to a single `stale_or_unknown_invite` remediation ("ask the room leader for a fresh invite") ‚Äî no opaque `bad_signature`, no oracle (genuine tampering still rejects as `bad_signature`).
- **A2A receiver sources `agent-env.local.sh` before spawning (#2075).** The cross-bridge receiver spawn did not load the agent's local env overrides, so per-agent A2A config set there was missing on the receiver path; it is now sourced before the spawn.
- **Daemon blocked-aging reminders are exempt from the `[unclaimed-task]` admin escalation (#2070 / #2067).** Daemon-generated `[blocked-aging]` reminder rows (created_by=daemon, assigned to the owning agent) aged past the unclaimed threshold and each escalated to admin ‚Üí N blocked tasks produced N√óN auto-generated, non-actionable admin escalations. The unclaimed scanner now skips that daemon reminder class (keyed on BOTH `created_by=='daemon'` AND the `[blocked-aging] task #` title prefix, so a genuine work task with that literal still escalates).
- **CI: deflake `1563-pr7-tick-cadence` H1/H2/H4 (#2078).** The cadence smoke tested gating with `printf ‚Ä¶ | grep -q`; `grep -q` closes the pipe on first match so `printf` dies with SIGPIPE (141) and under `pipefail` the pipeline returns 141 even on a match ‚Üí an intermittent false `[FAIL]` on Linux that turned `main` red on an unrelated merge. Rewritten to pure-bash matching (no pipe ‚Üí no SIGPIPE), deterministic on every runner.

### A2A cross-server routing + DNS-hostname keying (operator-mandated onto the LTS line for the live multi-server rollout)

- **Receiver-enforced cross-server admin‚Üîadmin A2A routing authz (#2079).** On a multi-server mesh, each server's admin agent (e.g. `patch`) must be callable cross-server **only** by another server's admin ‚Äî a non-admin remote agent must not reach an admin endpoint on a different node. The receiver now enforces a **symmetric admin‚Üîadmin** room-delivery predicate: if either endpoint is a configured admin, BOTH must be. The admin signal is a **tri-state `bridge_admin`** bit derived from each node's *configured admin id* (`BRIDGE_ADMIN_AGENT_ID`, **not** `name=='patch'`), with `unknown` never silently downgraded to non-admin; every admin-authz reject collapses to a **single generic 403** (reason audit-only ‚Äî no oracle), and the sender-side UX guard refuses a bare ambiguous `--to` before any POST. A fail-open was closed in the same change: the receiver **launch** paths (the systemd handoffd unit, `agb a2a daemon start`, `bridge-handoff-daemon.sh start`) did not export the admin id, so a local admin classified `unknown` and `non-admin@remote ‚Üí admin@local` failed OPEN; the admin id is now **resolved from the on-disk roster on every launch path** before serving via a zero-execution **pure-parse** of the roster assignment (a bash-eval pivot was rejected as an RCE surface). (patch-dev adversarial review: implement-ok after the fail-open + RCE-avoidance rounds.)
- **A2A DNS-hostname peer keying (#16247).** A 4th peer-identity leg ‚Äî a peer/`listen` may key on a fixed DNS **`hostname`** (e.g. a Cloudflare-managed `<handle>.cosmaxcf.com`) that resolves via the host resolver to that node's CURRENT IP, so the live fleet can move off raw-IP keying and auto-track IP changes. Precedence **`node_id` > `tailscale_name` > `hostname` > literal `address`**; transport-agnostic (works on `tailscale` / `cloudflare-warp-mesh` / `trusted-routed` alike). Like the Tailscale identity legs it is an **identity, never a trusted address**: fail-closed (an unresolvable / empty-resolve / malformed ‚Äî incl. a dot-only ‚Äî name hard-errors, never falling back to `address`), **bounded** (a daemon-thread `getaddrinfo` + join-timeout so untrusted inbound can't block the receiver), and cached ~45s (a transient DNS blip inside the freshness window is absorbed; expired+unresolvable fails closed; a short negative cache stops resolver hammering before each 403). The receiver **source-address check** (all four inbound sites) now resolves the authenticated peer's hostname to its **full** current A/AAAA set and accepts **membership** (multi-homed / round-robin safe; IPv4-mapped IPv6 normalized) rather than a single-string compare. (Design codex-confirmed; patch-dev adversarial review caught + closed a dot-only-fallback bug ‚Üí implement-ok.)

## [0.16.16-rc2] ‚Äî 2026-06-21 (LTS ¬∑ release candidate)

**v0.16.16-rc2 ‚Äî the second release candidate on the v0.16.16 LTS line: it carries everything in rc1 plus the full batch of issues/PRs that arrived during the rc1 soak window (35 PRs), re-cut to also fold in 5 more that landed before any install adopted the tag (40 PRs total).** Like rc1 this is a soak vehicle, not a promotion ‚Äî the `v0.16.15` tag stays the `lts` / Latest head and this prerelease is **not** marked Latest. The batch is dominated by daemon/queue reliability (the #1973 three-track stall-recovery set, the #1991 blocked-prompt safety floor, and the #2036 nudge-cadence fix), A2A room self-service onboarding, channel managed-send hardening (teams/discord/telegram), upgrade-safety guards for operator-customized files, agent onboarding/restart lifecycle, and the `agb status` fast-by-default perf work. Each PR carried its own internal codex review at park time; the rc2 cut is the integration gate. None of this batch is a daemon-down regression on top of rc1 ‚Äî it is the accumulated hardening the rc-soak window was meant to collect. **Re-cut note (2026-06-21):** since the original `v0.16.16-rc2` tag had zero adopters (soak never started), it was rebuilt rather than deferring these to a later cut ‚Äî folding the #2030 sleep-aware wedge supervisor, #2029 cron-child relay guard, #2021 plugin MCP-log liveness signal, #2035 ms365 token-freshness, and the #2036 nudge-cadence fix.

### Daemon & queue reliability

- **Idle re-nudge fires every cycle again ‚Äî heavy maintenance steps are cadence-gated (#2036, re-cut marquee).** The daemon's `cmd_sync_cycle` is a single serial loop; `interval=5s` is only the inter-cycle sleep, but the cycle *work* had bloated to ~2 minutes (`daemon_tick_slow` rows of 106-151s), starving `nudge_agents` inside it so an idle session's re-nudge arrived minutes late instead of within seconds. The heavy un-gated steps (`bridge_sync` ‚Äî now de-duplicated from two full syncs per cycle to one, the L1 roster reload, `mcp_orphan_cleanup_early`, `cron_staging_apply`, `claude_token_recovery`, `daily_backup`, `context_pressure_scan`) are now each cadence-gated via a fail-open `bridge_daemon_pass_due`, while `nudge_scan` / `nudge_agents` / `discord_relay` and every other delivery/wake/escalation path stay every-cycle. The gate is fully fail-open (a corrupt/unreadable/future cadence stamp runs the step now, never skips-forever, never aborts the daemon under `set -e`). The deeper Option-3 (decouple nudge dispatch into its own event-driven path) is the v0.17 direction.
- **Sleep-aware daemon wedge supervisor ‚Äî no false self-abort on wake-from-sleep (#2030, re-cut).** On a host that sleeps (laptop / non-always-on Mac), the #1563 wedge supervisor measured in-tick "no progress" with wall-clock time; system sleep froze the heartbeat while the wall clock jumped forward, so on wake it read a huge no-progress age and self-aborted (`exit 99`) though nothing hung ‚Äî a permanent outage if no restarter was loaded. The deadline is now suspend-aware (a single huge age-jump is treated as resume and re-anchored; an accumulation of poll-interval gaps ‚Äî a genuine hang ‚Äî still wedges), with a loud WARN when the abort path can't confirm a restarter.
- **Accept the Claude engine MCP-log as a second plugin-liveness signal (#2021, re-cut; credit @sankyul / #1770).** When the descendant-process probe is inconclusive/flaky for a plugin channel, the daemon now also consults the *current session's* engine MCP log for `Successfully connected` + `Channel notifications registered` before declaring the channel dead ‚Äî cutting false-dead restart churn. The process probe stays primary (a conclusive-dead is never overridden); a stale prior-session log can't yield a false-alive (current-session-scoped, fail-closed to the probe when the session id can't be resolved).
- **Queue-gateway stall recovery ‚Äî bounded drain, capped backoff, liveness-timer re-nudge (#1973, Tracks A/B/C).** Three layered fixes for the gateway-stall class. Track A (#1978) bounds the per-request drain and makes a degraded claim/done report honestly instead of faking success. Track B (#1979) replaces the fixed-interval re-nudge with a capped exponential backoff so a stuck agent no longer triggers a nudge storm. Track C (#1980) installs a liveness timer + gateway-stall detection that fires a single one-shot recovery re-nudge when the drain wedges. Track C consumes Track A's status-json signal and Track B's backoff seam ‚Äî they land in A‚ÜíB‚ÜíC order.
- **Blocked-prompt safety floor ‚Äî a blocked agent can never silently wedge (#1991, #1992, marquee).** A long-running agent whose input pane is occupied by a blocking prompt used to go silently deaf. The new safety floor (detect-only core in #1992) guarantees an independent operator escalation that never depends on the wedged agent itself, and a patch-owned **agentic blocked-prompt resolver** rides on top of it, **canary-gated** (off by default; opt-in per install). Extended to detect codex panes as well (#2007/#1991, detect-only).
- **Blocked-aging reminder re-fires visibly on cadence (#1986).** The blocked-task aging reminder refreshed in place silently instead of re-surfacing on its cadence; it now re-fires visibly, and the resurface no longer clobbers a reminder a human has already claimed (claimed-alert protection, with a T7 symmetry test).
- **Idle-claim reclaim honors the task lease (#1970).** An idle reclaim now respects the task lease and a worker `update` renews it, closing the #14837 live-repro race (regression oracle added).
- **Warn on unsupervised / non-canonical source-root daemon start (#1955).** A daemon started from a non-canonical source root (or unsupervised) now emits a startup warning instead of running silently from the wrong tree.

### A2A & cross-bridge

- **Discoverable room self-service onboarding + actionable `room_autojoin_disabled` 403 (#2024 A, #2027).** A fresh peer can join a discoverable room via an opt-in `BRIDGE_A2A_ROOM_AUTOJOIN` gate; with the gate off the receiver returns a **posture-only** 403 (`code: room_autojoin_disabled`) that returns *before* any token/room lookup, so it never becomes a token/room-existence oracle, and an unknown-peer invalid token stays peer-facing-opaque with precise audit. `BRIDGE_A2A_ROOM_AUTOJOIN` was also added to the `config set-env` allowlist (strict `flag_one`, only `"1"`) so the documented operator toggle actually works.
- **Require a valid sender id ‚Äî no OS-username fallback (#1933, data-integrity).** The A2A receiver no longer falls back to the OS username when a sender id is absent; an unidentified sender is rejected rather than silently attributed.
- **Task-oriented A2A quick reference (#2025).** A new task-oriented quick-reference doc for the A2A surface (room join, whois, manual setup).

### Channels

- **ms365 token-freshness hardening (#2035, re-cut).** Two defensive fixes to `plugins/ms365/server.ts`: (1) a read-time normalize so a legacy ms-format (13-digit millisecond) `expires_at` left by an old plugin version no longer reads as valid-for-56,000-years ‚Üí never-refresh (`expires_at > 1e12` ‚Üí seconds), applied on `getAccessToken` + `get_valid_token`; (2) an optional `min_remaining_seconds` / `force` parameter on `get_valid_token` so a proactive-refresh cron can pre-renew with margin **without** changing the global 300s reactive threshold (which would pull every interactive call's rotation cadence in). Param-absent behavior is byte-identical to before; negative margins are rejected + clamped so a token is never served past expiry.
- **Teams notify-kind ‚Äî managed-send adapter + notify_kind/target inference (#1996/#2006).** A `_adapter_teams` managed-send path (shells the bundled `bun plugins/teams/server.ts send-managed`, body passed as argv not shell), `TEAMS_STATE_DIR` resolved in bash as the workdir SSOT and threaded via `--teams-state-dir`, teams branches in notify_kind/target inference (allowFrom[0] with explicit-map precedence preserved), and a fail-closed bridge-notify guard. The notify-target helper is heredoc-free (footgun #11 clean).
- **Discord/Telegram managed-send reads the workdir SSOT state dir (#2005/#2010).** The discord/telegram managed-send paths resolve their state dir from the workdir SSOT (Python never re-derives the path), generalizing the #2006 `--plugin-state-dir` shape.
- **Telegram setup/verify hardening.** Drops the mcp-list 409, adds a bun preflight, and makes `verify` truthful (no false-green).

### Upgrade safety

- **`detect_engine` ignores the engine placeholder (#1930, #1928).** The upgrade engine detector no longer mis-reads the `<Claude Code CLI | Codex CLI>` template placeholder as a real engine.
- **Preserve operator-customized agent workdir CLAUDE.md (#1931, data-loss).** The upgrade no longer overwrites an operator-customized CLAUDE.md in the agent workdir.
- **Never hijack the operator-global settings.json + repair an existing hijack (#1981, #1985).** A settings render whose workdir resolved to the operator HOME could write managed hooks into the operator-global `settings.json`; that path is now guarded, and the upgrade detects + backup-gates repair of an *existing* hijack (reversible).

### Hooks, settings & layout

- **Runner-injected PreToolUse guard for disposable cron children (#2029, re-cut).** A disposable cron child ran `claude -p --permission-mode bypassPermissions` with `cwd` = the agent workdir (where channel transport creds live) and no tool restrictions, so a prompt-injected/over-eager child could read a channel token and post to a human channel directly (observed: a duplicate operator-channel delivery), or drop `BRIDGE_CRON_RUN_ID` from the queue CLI's env to mutate out-of-run tasks. The runner now injects a PreToolUse `Bash` deny hook (which fires even under `bypassPermissions`) into cron children only: it denies reading channel-credential `.env` paths + direct chat-transport/managed-send calls (Tier 1), and enforces in-run queue scope via the hook's own non-strippable inherited `BRIDGE_CRON_RUN_ID` (Tier 2), with carve-outs for legitimate in-run `cron:<run_id>` ops, `task create` delegation, and body-quoted verbs. Honest residual: a string-matching hook is not airtight under macOS "shared" isolation (no UID drop) ‚Äî it mitigates the realistic prompt-injection / naive-command class; airtight containment needs OS-level isolation.
- **Usage-tap composes with an operator statusLine renderer (#1961, display-only).** The usage-tap statusLine now composes with (rather than replaces) an operator's own statusLine renderer, renderer-agnostic. The broad propagation work is deferred to v0.17 (#1964).
- **First-party codex hook pretrust (#2007).** A hook-changing upgrade no longer wedges a managed codex agent on the first-trust prompt ‚Äî first-party codex hooks are pretrusted.
- **Engine-gate codex `AGENTS.md` emission for claude agents (#2016).** A claude agent no longer gets a Codex-contract `AGENTS.md` emitted into its home (3rd recurrence of the cross-engine doc leak).
- **iso: gate `ab-*` group creation to Linux + clean up inert macOS groups.** The linux-user isolation `ab-*` group create is gated to Linux and the inert macOS groups are cleaned up.
- **Seed an absolute `autoMemoryDirectory` (no tilde) (#2014).** The auto-memory directory is seeded as an absolute path so a literal `~` is never written into settings.

### Onboarding & agent runtime

- **Durable onboarding-completion authority + atomic 3-layer writer (#2004).** Onboarding completion state has a single durable authority written atomically across the three layers, so a restart mid-onboarding no longer loses or double-fires completion.
- **Deterministic, idempotent post-restart handoff wake (#2003).** A post-restart handoff wake is now deterministic and idempotent (no double-wake, no missed wake).
- **Gate the onboarding nudge on a genuine first launch (#2002).** The onboarding nudge fires only on a genuine first launch, not on every restart.
- **Watchdog checks the managed CLAUDE.md block against the identity home (#2018).** The managed-block watchdog matches against the identity home, fixing a false-positive for a custom-workdir claude agent.

### Status performance

- **`agb status` is fast by default; expensive analytics behind `--full` (#2000).** The default human dashboard defers the expensive analytics (orphan-dir fs-walk, context-pressure fp-rate, config-drift, nudge-recheck, upgrade-conflict) behind a `--full` (alias `--analytics`) flag and prints a "analytics deferred ‚Ä¶ run with --full" hint; the `--json` machine path is handled separately so no consumer silently loses fields.
- **Orphan-walk prune is classification-preserving (#1966).** The orphan-dir keep-set walk prunes `node_modules` / `.git` / transcripts (the dominant status cost) while preserving the registered-agent classification.

### Roster

- **Declare `BRIDGE_AGENT_SKILLS` associative ‚Äî stop silently dropping every configured skill (#2020).** `agent-roster.sh` declared every per-agent map with `declare -Ag` *except* `BRIDGE_AGENT_SKILLS`, so `agent create --skills` / `materialize-fields --skills` wrote into an undeclared name ‚Üí a non-numeric subscript collapsed to index `0` ‚Üí a broken indexed array ‚Üí `bridge_var_is_assoc` false ‚Üí `bridge_agent_skills_csv` empty ‚Üí **no configured skill materialized for any agent**, silently. Now declared associative alongside the sibling maps (before `agent-roster.local.sh` is sourced).

### Misc fixes

- **Doc-backfill resolves a dynamic agent's engine from the registry (#1956).** No longer guesses; reads the registry.
- **Orphan classifier case-folds on case-insensitive volumes (#1982).** The registered-agent classifier match is case-folded so a case-insensitive volume no longer mis-classifies a registered agent as orphan.
- **`forget-session` quarantines transcripts so the resolver fallback starts fresh (#1968).** A forget-session now quarantines the agent's transcripts (HIGH-RISK session-resume surface) so the resolver fallback starts clean.
- **Wiki-ingest Lane B targets by liveness + honest enqueue status (#1983).** No silent drop; the enqueue status is truthful.
- **Codify the system-escalation chain as a global contract (#1998).** `COMMON-INSTRUCTIONS.md` documents the system-escalation chain as a cross-agent contract.
- **macOS cron memory-pressure check no longer false-defers on chronic swap (#1929).** A chronically-swapping macOS host no longer perpetually defers crons.

## [0.16.16-rc1] ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (LTS ¬∑ release candidate)

**v0.16.16-rc1 ‚Äî the first release cut under the new RC-soak rule: a release candidate that soaks on the macbook + cm-prod for a few days before it is promoted, as-is, to the official `v0.16.16` LTS.** This RC batches the remaining **stability-unrelated** cm-prod v0.16.13 post-upgrade RCA findings (the reliability / iso-perm / UX residue after the F1 + F8 daemon-stability P1 pair already shipped in v0.16.14 / v0.16.15): F3, F5, F7, F6, and F4. None of these is a daemon-down regression ‚Äî they are the deferred batch the v0.16.15 notes promised would go through the RC path. The `v0.16.15` tag stays the `lts` / Latest head until this RC is promoted; this prerelease is **not** marked Latest.

### Reliability / iso-permission (cm-prod RCA batch)

- **Upgrade warns (instead of silently breaking) on unmodeled Claude crons (#1943, F3).** The #1880 cron model-gate fail-closes an enabled Claude cron that resolves no model when interactive `settings.json` pins one ‚Äî correct, but on upgrade it surfaced as a silent break with no migration or warning. `bridge-upgrade.sh` now appends a best-effort WARN to `[upgrade-complete]` (via a new read-only `lib/upgrade-helpers/cron-unmodeled-claude-warn.py` detector that imports the canonical `resolve_cron_child_model_effort` + `interactive_settings_model_for_request` so it fires on exactly the #1880 condition and can never drift). **WARN only** ‚Äî it never auto-pins a model (auto-pinning the interactive model re-introduces the #1880 coupling; a cheaper model is a usage decision the operator owns) and can never fail the upgrade (`|| true` + internal try/except).
- **Iso bootstrap memory-index rebuild graceful-skips an unreadable iso tree (#1947, F5).** The bootstrap memory-index rebuild hard-failed (`rc=2`) when it hit a `0700` iso-owned tree it could not read, aborting the step instead of skipping the unreadable subtree. It now degrades to a graceful skip so a single unreadable iso home no longer breaks the rebuild.
- **Iso `agent restart` renders settings as the iso UID, not the controller (#1945, F7).** An iso `agent restart` rendered `settings.effective.json` as the controller UID into an iso-owned home ‚Üí `PermissionError`. The settings-render now escalates to the agent's iso UID (fail-loud if it genuinely cannot escalate ‚Äî never a silent controller-direct re-deny), and the deferral guard is gated **before** the render escalation so a decided-to-defer case returns cleanly. New smoke `1945-iso-restart-settings-iso-uid` gates the render; the existing `1145-option1-deferral-guard` gates the deferral decision (hardened non-vacuous after a regression that the broadened ci-select surfaced).
- **Daemon latches unclaimed-task escalation once per `(agent, task)` (#1944, F6 ‚Äî Part 1).** The daemon's unclaimed-task escalation already had a per-task marker cooldown, but it re-alerted the same stuck queued task once per cooldown window (default `1800s`) rather than latching once for the queued `(agent, task)` ‚Äî so a perpetually-unclaimed task kept re-alerting on a timer (the nudge-churn symptom). The per-task escalation marker is now a once-latch keyed by `(agent, task)` (cooldown default `1800 ‚Üí 0`): it suppresses a repeat alert for the same assignee but **re-arms** on a handoff to a new assignee and self-heals a legacy single-line marker, so a legitimately-reassigned (possibly wedged) task is never permanently silenced. The deeper nudge-submit-loop reshaping (per-agent `submit_lost` backoff + wedged-session stop) is intentionally **deferred** ‚Äî F6's root cause was already removed by the F8 queue-gateway fix in v0.16.15.

### Docs / UX

- **Headless shared-UID config-deny points at the supported flow (#1946, F4).** A headless shared-UID admin (daemon and agent on the same UID) has no config-mutation path because #1738 correctly fail-closes a self-owned binding store and there is no operator TTY ‚Äî and the deny message offered no next step. This is **by design**, not a bug to code around (a daemon-mediated path on a shared UID is security theater ‚Äî the daemon cannot prove non-forgeability when it shares the agent's UID). The deny `reason=` now points at the two supported flows ‚Äî an attended operator TTY (`--from <admin>`) for one-time changes, or linux-user isolation (iso-v2) for unattended headless config ‚Äî and `OPERATIONS.md` ¬ß"Iso v2 agent troubleshooting" documents the rationale. **Authorization-neutral**: only the deny message changed; the `#1738` deny teeth (`allowed=False` + the `agent-binding-store-writable` prefix the smoke matches) are unchanged.

## [0.16.15] ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (LTS)

**v0.16.15 LTS ‚Äî stability hotfix: a single poison request can no longer silently kill the whole fleet's queue gateway (#1949, cm-prod F8).** Completes the **F1/F8 daemon-stability P1 pair** from the cm-prod v0.16.13 post-upgrade RCA (F1 ‚Äî the systemd-`--user` enable-linger 27h daemon-down ‚Äî shipped in v0.16.14; F8 ships here). Cut directly to official (not the new RC-soak path) given the live daemon-stability impact on installs already upgraded to v0.16.13 / v0.16.14. Supersedes `v0.16.14` as the `lts` / Latest head; prior v0.16.x LTS designations remain valid.

- **A poison queue request no longer aborts `serve-once` ‚Üí fleet-wide silent queue death (#1949, F8).** `bridge-queue-gateway.py`'s `cmd_serve_once` ran each request's `handle_request` with no per-request guard, and `run_queue` did `subprocess.run(cwd=<client-recorded cwd>)`. An iso agent that ran `agb` from inside a `0700` iso-owned dir (e.g. `.teams/attachments/<id>`) recorded a cwd the controller-run gateway could not `chdir` into ‚Üí `PermissionError` aborted the entire batch, and the request ‚Äî already promoted to `<id>.working.json` ‚Äî re-crashed every tick (a permanent loop). Every other agent's queued request then timed out forever (the real root of the #1944 nudge-churn; on cm-prod a single attachment-cwd request killed the fleet queue ~26h while the daemon reported healthy). Fix: `run_queue` falls back to a controller-safe cwd when the recorded cwd is missing/inaccessible (queue ops key off `BRIDGE_*` env + the queue DB, not cwd), and `cmd_serve_once` wraps each request in a per-request try/except that dead-letters the poison out of the drain (`.poison` rename + error response) and CONTINUES the batch. New smoke `1949-gateway-poison-request` gates both.

The remaining cm-prod findings ‚Äî F3 (#1943 cron model-gate), F5 (#1947 iso index-rebuild graceful-skip), F7 (#1945 iso restart settings perm), F4 (#1946 headless config path), and F6 (#1944 nudge backoff ‚Äî root now removed by F8) ‚Äî are **stability-unrelated** (reliability / iso-perm / UX) and are deferred to the next version, which will go through the new RC-soak release rule.

## [0.16.14] ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (LTS)

**v0.16.14 LTS ‚Äî hotfix: the systemd `--user` daemon now survives a login-session churn (enable-linger).** A sudo-self systemd-user install bound the always-on reconcile daemon **and** its liveness-backstop timer to the invoking login session without enabling lingering, so a single session churn (e.g. a `--replace` agent restart) made systemd leave `default.target` / `timers.target` and stop both ‚Äî a silent, indefinite outage (observed on a production host: ~27h reconcile-daemon down + Claude token auto-rotation frozen ‚Üí fleet quota-stall, because the liveness timer shares the daemon's `systemd --user` failure domain rather than being an independent backstop). The daemon installer now runs `loginctl enable-linger` whenever it **applies** the unit ‚Äî not only on `--enable`, so `agb upgrade` (which re-applies the unit *without* `--enable`) reaches existing installs too ‚Äî with a `sudo` fallback and a loud warning + exact remediation (`loginctl enable-linger <user>`) when it cannot. Found via the cm-prod v0.16.13 post-upgrade RCA (finding F1; patch-dev code-side confirmation #8756).

### Fast-follow (cm-prod RCA, tracked, locally mitigated)

- The companion cm-prod findings are tracked as fast-follow fixes (cm-prod has locally mitigated each): the **#1880 cron model-gate** breaking unmodeled Claude crons on upgrade without a migration or warning (F3 ‚Äî local: per-job model pins); an **iso bootstrap memory-index rebuild** hard-failing (`rc=2`) on a `0700` iso tree instead of graceful-skip (F5); a **headless shared-UID admin** lacking any config-mutation path because `#1738` correctly fail-closes a self-owned binding store and there is no operator TTY (F4 ‚Äî needs a daemon-mediated trusted path); **daemon nudge-retry churn** repeatedly nudging idle/wedged agents without per-agent backoff/dedupe (F6); and an **iso `agent restart` settings-render** `PermissionError` writing `settings.effective.json` as the controller UID into an iso-owned home (F7). The daemon self-heal that republishes `#1738` config-caller bindings for live resident panes was verified working (the F4 redefinition).

## [0.16.13] ‚Äî 2026-06-17 (LTS)

**v0.16.13 LTS ‚Äî a security + reliability hardening bundle on top of v0.16.12's upgrade-respawn fixes: config-mutation trust derived from an unspoofable pane binding (#1738), a tmux-socket single-point-of-failure that could down a whole agent fleet (#1932/#1936), and a hook-path footgun that bricked a production farm (#1934).** Promotes the `v0.16.13-rc2` prerelease to the blessed LTS tag for the v0.16 line after a three-way re-test (macbook isolated + a real OrbStack Linux iso-v2 `agb upgrade` + cm-prod production-host isolated verification). The conservative `lts` channel now resolves to v0.16.13. (The "(new in RC2)" sub-heading below is kept verbatim as the record of what landed between the v0.16.12 and v0.16.13 LTS tags.)

### Security & reliability (new in RC2)

- **Config-set / set-env trust derives from an unspoofable pane binding, not env (#1738, #341 root).** `agb config set` / `config set-env` previously trusted `BRIDGE_AGENT_ID` / `--from` / `BRIDGE_CALLER_SOURCE` env to decide admin identity ‚Äî forgeable by any same-UID caller. Trust now derives from a controller-published per-agent pane-pid binding that `bridge-config.py` matches against its own process ancestry (absolute `/bin/ps` + `/proc`); env no longer authorizes. Hardened over a multi-round security gate: an ownership-based store-writability fail-close (a caller-owned binding store can never prove non-forgeability), the tmux-liveness probe pinned to a clean **allowlist** env (no `DYLD_*`/`LD_PRELOAD`/`TMUX_TMPDIR` injection) with a kernel-boundary **pane-owner-UID** check, a fail-safe daemon prune (a transient tmux outage prunes nothing, never the whole fleet), and a defense-in-depth hook gate that denies a config mutation hidden behind `eval`/`bash -c`/`env`/`command`/`exec`/`nice` or an unresolved `$var`.
- **A smoke run can no longer down the live agent fleet (#1932).** Every bridge agent shares the host default tmux socket (a single point of failure), and the destructive smoke harness did not isolate it ‚Äî a smoke run from inside an agent's tmux pane could tear that socket down and take **every** agent with it (observed twice on a live host). A new `lib/bridge-smoke-tmux-isolation.sh` guard severs an inherited `$TMUX` and forces a private socket (fail-closed if it would resolve to the shared/default one), sourced by the smoke lib + orchestrators + the tmux-touching smokes.
- **1738 liveness teeth use the probe's socket (#1936).** Follow-up to #1932 + #1738: the smoke's liveness test session is created on the same default socket the wrapper's liveness probe queries, restoring the ownership-gate teeth without reintroducing a fleet-down risk or an agent-controlled socket seam.
- **Hook command paths never resolve under a transient `/tmp` (#1934).** A settings render with a non-canonical / temp `BRIDGE_HOME` wrote `/tmp`-rooted hook command paths into LIVE agent settings, the `_template` scaffold, and Codex `.codex/hooks.json`; when the OS reaped `/tmp`, every hook vanished ‚Üí `UserPromptSubmit` fail-closed-deaf + `PreToolUse *` tool-deadlock, bricking a production farm. Hook paths now resolve to a stable canonical dir for both engines, a missing hook **file** self-heals (session-start + daemon reconcile re-render, with a per-agent storm-guard) instead of bricking the agent, and the `state/isolated-plugin-grants/` shared dir is re-asserted to a durable `controller:ab-shared 0710` (never the writing agent's private group) so a grant write can't lock out another agent's launcher.

### Upgrade safety

- **Fail-safe picker-sweep migration (#1916).** The upgrade-init shell-kind picker-sweep migration deleted the legacy text-kind cron *before* creating the shell-kind one; when the create raced the daemon-restart window (observed on a v0.16.12 `--channel lts` upgrade), the host was left with **zero** picker-sweep crons. Reordered to **recreate-first / verify-before-delete**: the shell-kind cron is registered (and re-confirmed present) before the legacy row is deleted by id; on a failed/unverified create the legacy row is left in place + WARN, so there is never a window with neither.
- **De-ancestor the live-root CLAUDE.md to an operator stub (#1817).** The upgrade's `--ref` dry-run now hashes the requested ref's contract bytes (not the working tree), so a preview faithfully reflects what that ref would deploy; an operator-customized live-root CLAUDE.md is never overwritten.
- **Flag existing engine-mismatched instruction docs (report-only) (#1906).** Follow-up to #1896: the doc-backfill sweep now detects + flags an existing engine-mismatched instruction doc (a stale Codex-contract `AGENTS.md` left in a correct `claude` agent's home) in the `[hygiene]` surface ‚Äî report-only, never auto-removed (operator does a reversible backup-rename).

### Agent runtime

- **Hard-ban AskUserQuestion for all bridge Claude agents (#1923).** The interactive multiple-choice `AskUserQuestion` picker blocks the input pane ‚Üí a long-running agent goes silently deaf to A2A / daemon nudges / other agents while it is open. #1569 bounded it for hook-managed agents, but dynamic-vanilla agents (#1890) never got the intercept. A dedicated dependency-free PreToolUse deny hook (guaranteed under `--dangerously-skip-permissions`) now bans the tool for **every** bridge Claude agent ‚Äî static, dynamic-managed, and dynamic-vanilla ‚Äî routed into the vanilla `settings.local.json` overlay, plus a scoped `permissions.deny` as defense-in-depth. The operator-global `~/.claude` is never touched (the operator's own interactive sessions keep the tool).
- **Detect feedback-survey + permission/overwrite/context modals as blockers (#1181, Layer 1).** A long-running Claude agent that has the feedback-survey modal (or a per-session permission grant / overwrite-confirm / context-pressure modal) drawn into its input pane silently stops consuming nudges while `agb status` shows it healthy. The blocker detector now recognizes these modals, the status snapshot / `agent show` / wake surfaces report `wake_reason=modal_<state>` via a single shared predicate, and the daemon nudge-drop audit tags the modal class. Detection/status only ‚Äî no submit-path. `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY=1` is documented as the recommended noise-reduction env.

### CI / test-harness de-flaking

- **daemon.sh loop-restart cold-boot ‚Äî two facets closed (#1699).** Two chronic required-smoke flakes that forced reruns on otherwise-green PRs: (1) an accept-readiness TOCTOU ‚Äî the readiness poll returned the instant the socket/pid files appeared, before the gateway listener was actually accepting; the poll now requires the same accept-proof the daemon itself requires. (2) a singleton-lock test-isolation race ‚Äî the loop-restart test shared the daemon singleton flock with the preceding lifecycle test, and `kill`+`wait` did not guarantee flock release before the next spawn; the spawn now bounded-waits for the flock to free, and the diagnostic surfaces the daemon's `launchagent.log` exit record (which made the empty-`daemon_log` signature legible). Test-harness only.
- **Canon shared-doc symlinks resolve correctly in layout-v2 agent homes (#1813).** `bridge-docs.py` now computes the canon shared-doc link target from the actual agent home to `shared/` for both v1 and v2 layouts, accepts already-correct absolute/relative links by realpath, scopes broken-link cleanup to bridge-managed shared-doc names, and the upgrade doc-sync path now targets v2 `<data_root>/agents/<agent>/home`. The related smoke was also hardened to avoid a SIGPIPE/pipefail + heredoc-ban false failure.
- **#1738 config-caller foreign-store smoke is non-vacuous (#1939).** The iso positive-control helper chmod'd the seeded binding files with a caller-shell glob that could not expand after the store was chowned to a foreign owner + `0711` ‚Äî so the positive assertion false-failed on passwordless-sudo hosts and was green-by-skip elsewhere. The glob now expands inside a root shell so the binding files land `nobody:0644` (controller-readable, the real iso store shape). Found by the cm-prod production-host verification; test-harness only.

### Soak / acceptance (verified before the cut)

- **macbook isolated:** the #1738 config-caller-binding, #1934 hook-path-canonical-fence, and #1934 hook-file-self-heal smokes pass on macOS (homebrew bash 5.3.9); the #1932 fleet-down tmux-socket guard was confirmed live.
- **OrbStack real-Linux VM (Ubuntu noble, iso-v2):** a real `agb upgrade --ref v0.16.13-rc2 --apply` from `v0.16.13-rc1` applied clean (rc=0, 0 conflicts, the systemd-aware #1905 quiesce fired, daemon restarted, 12 agents considered/migrated ‚Äî 2 eligible agents restarted and 10 skipped (9 inactive, 1 manual-stop)); post-upgrade health warn=0 crit=0; the three rc2 smokes pass on Linux.
- **cm-prod production-host isolated verification (real-Linux iso-v2, live customer Teams bots):** the full bundle's smokes pass on the cm-prod host (#1934 both facets, #1932 fleet stayed up across 6+ smokes, #1923, #1916, #1181) and the #1738 trust logic was confirmed correct (all 11 security DENY teeth). The live customer-fleet upgrade was intentionally NOT run ‚Äî cm-prod is stable-only and adopts this LTS via the `lts` channel. That verification surfaced the #1939 smoke-harness fix folded into this cut.

## [0.16.12] ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (LTS)

**v0.16.12 LTS ‚Äî upgrade respawn-race fix (systemd + launchd), plus iso-v2 transcript harvest, resume hardening, and usage edge-classification.** The marquee change closes an **upgrade-breakage footgun that hit every systemd-managed install** (and its macOS launchd analog): the #1820 layout-v2 reconcile quiesce stopped the daemon only at the script level, so the init system respawned it inside the quiesce window ‚Üí the fail-closed reconcile fence saw a live pid ‚Üí `rc=3` and a half-applied upgrade. The quiesce/restart is now init-system-aware on both systemd and launchd, validated on the cm-prod production host (the #1905 host-acceptance passed 5/5 ‚Äî the exact footgun that required a manual unit-stop on v0.16.11 now upgrades untouched).

### Upgrade respawn-race ‚Äî the marquee fix

- **systemd: #1820 reconcile quiesce is now systemd-aware (#1905).** On a systemd sudo-self install, `agb upgrade` aborted `rc=3` at the #1820 reconcile gate and half-applied, because the script-level `bridge-daemon.sh stop` is not systemd-aware ‚Äî `agent-bridge-daemon.service` (`Restart=`) + `agent-bridge-daemon-liveness.timer` respawned the daemon inside the quiesce window. The upgrader now stops the liveness timer then the service via `systemctl --user` (and restores them on restart), mirroring the existing A2A-receiver systemd-aware pattern. A real-host catch from cm-prod: `systemctl --user` needs `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` (unset in the upgrader env, Linger=no), so the fix also establishes it before every `systemctl --user` call ‚Äî without which the stop would silently no-op (swallowed by `|| true`) and the bug would recur. Graceful fallback + loud WARN when no user bus is reachable but the unit is installed.
- **launchd: the macOS analog (#655).** The same respawn race on macOS: a `KeepAlive=true` LaunchAgent respawns the daemon during the quiesce window. The upgrader now `launchctl disable`‚Üí`bootout`s the job before the stop and `enable`‚Üí`bootstrap`‚Üí`kickstart`s it on restore, gated behind a launchd-active check and mutually exclusive with the systemd path (a host is systemd or launchd, never both). Non-macOS / plain-bash installs are byte-for-byte unchanged.

### iso-v2 + resume + usage

- **iso transcript harvest crosses the iso boundary via run-as-iso (#1894).** The controller-UID memory harvester couldn't read iso agents' `<iso-home>/.claude/projects` (runtime-created `2700`, iso-owned; the prepare-isolation ACL the comment promised is absent), so every iso agent fell to `--skipped-permission` permanently. The bounded transcript scan now runs as the iso UID via the existing sudoers `bash` allowlist and marshals results back to the controller, which keeps owning all queue-DB / aggregate writes. The prepare-isolation path is untouched (preserves the `index.sqlite` `0600` carve-out + publisher ordering).
- **resume config-dir resolver realpath-hardened against symlinked-to-operator-HOME (#1893, #1889 fast-follow).** Two non-blocking hardening items: the dynamic config-dir resolver now realpath-compares the operator-HOME exclusion (so an agent `.claude` symlinked to the operator's global falls through to scaffold), and the quarantine-archive refuses to move a transcript that realpaths into the operator's own projects root. The shipped #1890 dynamic-vanilla-Claude early-returns are preserved.
- **usage probe classifies CDN-edge blocks vs account quota (#1824).** A usage-probe failure that is a CDN-edge block (Cloudflare 429/403 with no anthropic origin headers) is now classified `edge-blocked` and never fabricates a synthetic 100% near-limit cache (which would poison rotation); a genuine origin `account-rate-limit` still fires the near-limit signal. Adds per-token probe backoff + alert isolation, and `Retry-After` now honors both the delta-seconds and RFC 7231 HTTP-date forms.

### Soak / acceptance (verified before the cut)

- **Local OrbStack real-Linux VM-verify** (Ubuntu noble, iso-v2): `oss-preflight` PASS, all five lane smokes + the ci-select shard self-test + the upgrade fail-closed smoke PASS on Linux; no macOS-only-smoke-not-Linux-portable regression.
- **cm-prod production acceptance (systemd iso, 8 live customer Teams bots):** the #1905 host-acceptance passed 5/5 with decisive evidence the XDG fix fired (single-pass reconcile + daemon PID replacement); customer bots unaffected.
- **macOS launchd acceptance:** the #655 launchd quiesce/restart validated on a real macOS launchd rig (disable‚Üíbootout brings the daemon down and keeps it down vs. a 4s respawn without the fix; enable‚Üíbootstrap‚Üíkickstart restores it). The full destructive `agb upgrade` end-to-end is tracked as a v0.16.13 CI/VM follow-up; the fix is fail-closed (worst case = the documented pre-existing `rc=3`, never new breakage).
- **CI:** the full required smoke battery (3-way sharded) was green on Linux for every constituent lane PR; the assembled tree was additionally verified by the OrbStack real-Linux VM-verify above (oss-preflight + all five lane smokes + the ci-select shard self-test). The chronic #1699 `daemon.sh` cold-boot flake recurred a few times across the session and cleared on re-run each time (deterministic-fixture hardening is tracked for v0.16.13).

## [0.16.11] ‚Äî 2026-06-15 (LTS)

**v0.16.11 LTS ‚Äî dynamic agents become vanilla-equivalent, plus iso-v2 create-path / doc-backfill hardening.** Promotes `v0.16.11-rc1` to the blessed LTS tag for the v0.16 line after a real-Linux soak. The marquee change is operational convergence for **dynamic agents**: a dynamic Claude or Codex agent now runs like vanilla Claude Code / Codex CLI against the operator's global config (settings, model, session history, installed plugins, MCP, credentials) with native resume (`claude -c` / `codex resume --last`), and the bridge layers only its comms/queue hooks via project-local settings ‚Äî superseding the private-config-dir isolation that was destroying operator sessions. The full item list is in the `0.16.11-rc1` entry below.

### Soak (what was verified before promotion)

- **Local OrbStack real-Linux (Ubuntu noble, iso-v2 active) soak.** Upgrade from `0.16.4` ‚Üí `0.16.11-rc1` applied cleanly (246 files, 12 agents migrated, eligible agents restarted, one expected `settings.json` upgrade-conflict backed up for review); the install came up healthy on rc1 (daemon running, 0 health warnings). The six release-critical smokes ran on real Linux (where macOS skips the iso paths) and passed: `1891` (iso create-path), `1892` (doc-backfill fail-closed), `1890` (dynamic vanilla Claude), `1899` (dynamic vanilla Codex), `1900-refresh-noop`, `1899-v2-secret-env-repin`. The dynamic-vanilla Claude launch builder was confirmed live to emit a vanilla `claude` command with no `--resume <id>`, no private `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`, and no model/effort override, with the session-detect/persist chokepoints gated off for the dynamic class.
- **CI.** The full required smoke battery (3-way sharded) was green on Linux for every constituent PR and for the rc1 release PR.
- **Pending production acceptance.** Downstream cm-prod (real-Linux iso-v2 production) consumes this LTS via the `lts` channel and will run the contracted post-upgrade acceptance (later-created iso agents' `memory/` auto-normalized to iso-owned `2770`, `agent-meta.env` auto-created, roster-first engine detection, and reconciliation of the one historical mis-detected entrypoint). Note: live `isolate --reapply` was not exercised on the soak machine because that install is in a pre-existing markerless layout state (no `layout-version` marker); the iso create-path change is covered by the `1891` functional smoke + CI + the cm-prod post-upgrade acceptance above.

## [0.16.11-rc1] ‚Äî 2026-06-15

**Release candidate for dynamic vanilla agents + iso-v2 create-path / doc-backfill hardening, prepared for VM/fleet soak before LTS promotion.** Rolls up everything merged on `main` since the v0.16.10 LTS tag. Headline: operational convergence for **dynamic agents** ‚Äî a dynamic Claude or Codex agent now runs like vanilla Claude Code / Codex CLI against the operator's global config (settings, model, session history, installed plugins, MCP, credentials) with native resume, and the bridge layers only its comms/queue hooks via project-local settings, superseding the prior private-config-dir isolation that was destroying operator sessions. Intended to be validated by a VM/fleet soak (incl. cm-prod real-Linux iso-v2 acceptance) before promotion to the v0.16.11 LTS tag. **Not yet soak-verified.**

### Dynamic agents ‚Äî vanilla-equivalent (operator-directed)

- **Dynamic Claude agents run as vanilla Claude Code + bridge comms only (#1900, #1890).** A dynamic Claude agent now inherits the operator/user global `~/.claude` (settings, model, session history, installed plugins, MCP, credentials), resumes via native `claude -c`, and gets no private `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`. Bridge comms/queue hooks are layered via project-local `<workdir>/.claude/settings.local.json` (operator keys preserved, `.git/info/exclude` guard, refuses to modify a tracked file), and `hooks/prompt-guard.py` no-ops without a bridge session so an operator's later vanilla session in the same workdir is unaffected. This supersedes #1889's private-config isolation for dynamic Claude ‚Äî safety is now "no destructive session machinery", not isolation; the bridge no longer detects, persists, or quarantines a session id for dynamic Claude. Static/admin Claude agents are unchanged.
- **Dynamic Codex agents run as vanilla Codex CLI + bridge comms only (#1901, #1899).** The Codex sibling: a dynamic Codex agent inherits the operator-global Codex durable root (`~/.codex`), resumes via native `codex resume --last` (cwd-filtered), gets no per-agent durable root, and layers comms via project-local `<workdir>/.codex/hooks.json` with read-only hook-trust detection (operator-visible report; never a blanket trust bypass). Bridge session detect/persist is a no-op for this class, and the v2 secret-env launch path is fail-closed re-pinned to the operator durable root. Static/admin Codex agents are unchanged.

### Operator-session safety + iso-v2 hardening

- **Dynamic-agent resume detection + quarantine scoped to the agent's own config dir (#1889).** The original operator-session-hijack fix: a fresh dynamic agent launched in an operator workdir could detect, resume, and quarantine (move) the operator's vanilla Claude session transcript. Detection + quarantine are scoped to the agent's own config dir; the operator transcript is never selected or moved. (For dynamic agents this is now subsumed by the vanilla-equivalent behavior above; it remains the static/admin contract.)
- **iso-v2 create-path completeness: iso-owned `memory/` + always-create `agent-meta.env` (#1895, #1891).** Later-created iso agents could finish provisioning with `memory/` left controller-owned `2700` (the iso UID then can't read its own memory ‚Üí daily harvest fails) and `agent-meta.env` silently absent. The create path and reapply/reconcile now normalize the iso-owned `memory/` trees (dirs `2770` group `ab-agent-<a>`, files `0660`, `index.sqlite` kept controller `0600`), including existing stale subtrees, and `agent-meta.env` absence is now a visible failure rather than a warn-only fallback.
- **doc-backfill / watchdog engine detection is fail-closed on the roster (#1896, #1892).** When `agent-meta.env` was absent the daemon's codex `AGENTS.md` backfill could mis-detect the engine and materialize a codex entrypoint on a claude-roster agent. Engine resolution is now roster-first; absence is never inferred as codex; a roster/metadata disagreement holds and warns (surfaced in the operator hygiene task) instead of materializing the wrong template.
- **Controller scanners graceful-skip the iso boundary consistently (#1878, #1820 rc4).** A common iso-boundary graceful-skip across the reconcile/watchdog controller scanners (reading the runtime-home effective settings for hooks/plugins rather than the data-tree mirror), closing the last of the #1820 layout-v2 reconcile iso-permission edges.

### Other fixes

- **watchdog: `unsupported_engine_contract` reclassified as info, not a HIGH drift problem (#1884, #1872).** Antigravity / unknown engines with otherwise-valid links no longer page; broken links for the same engine still do.
- **cron children get an explicit model instead of inheriting interactive `settings.json` (#1885, #1880).** Cron-dispatched children no longer derive their model from interactive settings; precedence is per-job ‚Üí cron-default ‚Üí roster ‚Üí documented fallback, with a conditional fail-closed only when an inheritable model is present.
- **channel restart guidance + live-MCP readiness; route plugin-enable away from a symlink-write (#1886, #1881).** `agent show` / diagnostics gain a live-MCP connectivity signal for probeable channel plugins, and operators are routed to bridge-native restart/repair rather than a `settings.effective.json` symlink write.
- **picker-sweep registered as a shell-kind cron (#1888).**

### CI / dev-infra

- **unit/static smoke battery sharded across a 3-way matrix + aggregate gate (#1898, #1897).** The required-smoke battery had crossed the 25-minute job timeout and was intermittently false-red; it now runs as `unit/static smoke shard N/3` in parallel (~6‚Äì10 min each) behind an aggregate gate named exactly `unit/static smoke` that preserves the branch-protection check and fails if any shard fails. No user-facing change.

### Soak

- Release candidate prepared for VM/fleet soak. The soak must exercise the iso-sensitive lanes (#1878, #1895, #1896) and the dynamic-vanilla behavior (#1900, #1901), including cm-prod real-Linux iso-v2 acceptance, before any v0.16.11 LTS promotion. Not yet soak-verified at rc1.

## [0.16.10] ‚Äî 2026-06-13 (LTS)

**v0.16.10 LTS ‚Äî a large reliability + migration train, hardened through a fleet soak across sean-mac (macOS shared-mode) and cm-prod (real-Linux iso-v2 production).** This consolidates the rc1‚Üírc3 candidates into the blessed LTS tag for the v0.16.x line. It closes the dynamic-agent restart footgun, makes operator plugin/setting provisioning survive recreate, and lands a gated layout-v2 four-writer migration with a fail-closed v1‚Üív2 reconciliation that stops legacy installs from forking agent memory. Every code lane cleared the queue pair-review gate; the two data-loss/iso-sensitive lanes (#1857 provisioning preserve, #1820 layout-v2 migration) additionally cleared the independent `patch` adversarial gate across four rounds, and the reconcile iso-permission handling (#1820 rc3) was re-verified on a real Linux-UID host.

### Highlights (consolidated rc1‚Äìrc3)

- **Dynamic agent lifecycle:** `agent restart <dynamic>` now relaunches instead of deregistering + quarantining the transcript (#1852); self-restart survives the kill‚Üírelaunch (#1853); per-agent `enabledPlugins`/`statusLine`/plugin-manifest/skills are preserved across recreate/`--replace` with a bridge-owned provenance ledger and fail-closed token validation (#1857).
- **layout-v2 migration & drift-0:** the cron memory writer, PreCompact fallback, per-agent settings render, and doc-sync target all resolved to the v1 `agents/<a>` tree and forked agent memory daily on post-v0.8 installs; all four now resolve v2-first, and a same-release reconciliation runs as one fail-closed, daemon-down, scope-locked migration with both-side backups, content-hash conflict policy (prefix/superset merge, divergent‚Üíarchive+marker+queue-task, idempotent re-apply), atomic workdir-symlink retarget, foreign-symlink-ancestor fencing, and a post-apply invariant (#1820).
- **iso-v2 reconcile correctness (rc3):** the controller-run reconcile graceful-skips iso agent-private 0600 memory it cannot read and records a structured, auditable `isolation_v2_migration` section in `last-apply.json` (#1820 rc3); cron workers refuse to auto-execute interactive-gated or irreversible production-mutation tasks (rc3, extends #1792).
- **Observability (rc2):** the reconcile always writes a structured result at one canonical path (`state/migration/layout-v2-reconcile/last-apply.json`); a successful migration clears the stale `last-error.json` it resolves.
- **Security:** the #1838 tool-policy lane closed a spoofed-admin peer-write bypass (heredoc-body exclusion gated on loose admin instead of strict trusted-admin), caught by CI before merge.

### Soak verdict

- **sean-mac (macOS shared-mode):** clean, drift 0 ‚Äî 8 agents settings-v2-normalized, memory preserved (no strand/fork), v1 tree kept as rollback evidence.
- **cm-prod (first real Linux iso-v2 production host, 7 customer bots):** v1‚Üív2 migration applied with **zero data loss and zero drift**; reconcile result structured/auditable. A controller-shell stale-supplementary-group condition (a 15-day login shell missing groups added after its start ‚Äî KNOWN_ISSUES ¬ß28 / #1836) produced cosmetic `Errno13` reconcile warnings on iso homes; refreshing the controller's group set (fresh login / `newgrp`) clears them with zero data impact. An automatic fresh-group preflight that removes the manual step ships as a follow-up hardening in the next v0.16.x patch (does not affect migration correctness).

### Notes

- LTS line head for v0.16.x. Production / conservative installs should pin the `lts` channel.
- Follow-up hardening tracked post-tag: controller fresh-group preflight for `agb upgrade`/watchdog (removes the stale-shell manual step) and a watchdog data-tree-mirror settings-source correction (cosmetic anomaly-agent false-positive).

## [0.16.10-rc3] ‚Äî 2026-06-13

**Third release candidate ‚Äî closes two blockers the cm-prod (real Linux iso-v2 production) rc2 soak surfaced before promotion to LTS.** rc2 applied functionally with zero data loss, but a deeper cm-prod audit corrected the initial "clean" verdict: the layout-v2 reconcile had an iso-permission gap and the upgrade had been auto-executed by a cron worker that an interactive admin had deliberately gated. Both are now fixed and re-verified (#1876 cleared the `patch` adversarial iso-lane gate on a real Linux-UID container). No change to the gate-2-approved reconcile fencing/fail-closed data logic.

### Fixed

- **layout-v2 reconcile no longer controller-direct-reads iso agent-private memory; the iso pass is now structured & auditable (#1820 rc3).** The reconcile runs as the controller, which cannot read an iso agent's agent-private `MEMORY.md` (owner `agent-bridge-<a>`, mode 0600) ‚Äî producing `Errno13`/permission-denied backup warnings on every iso agent, with no structured `isolation_v2_migration` section in `last-apply.json`. Agent-private 0600 memory is owned and managed by the agent and is **not** controller-reconcilable (same class as the iso-home skips in #1635/#1827): the reconcile now graceful-skips it (recording a structured `isolation_v2_migration: [{agent, os_user, action: skipped-iso-private, ‚Ä¶}]` entry) before any controller read/backup, and the structured section is always present in the result. Verified on a real Linux-UID container: zero Errno13, byte-unchanged iso memory, shared-mode agents still reconcile normally.
- **cron workers refuse to auto-execute interactive-gated or irreversible production-mutation tasks (rc3).** A `librarian-watchdog` cron worker saw a deliberately-held (`status=blocked`) `agb upgrade` A2A handoff in the shared inbox and auto-executed the irreversible production upgrade with no human in the loop. The cron-dispatch scope fence (#1792) now also binds the worker to refuse auto-execution of irreversible/prod-mutation tasks (`agb upgrade`, release/tag, fleet/roster mutation, destructive migration) and any task gated by an interactive admin ‚Äî deferring to interactive handling when uncertain.

### Soak

- rc1 clean on sean-mac (macOS shared-mode, drift 0); rc2 cm-prod edge soak (real Linux iso) applied with zero data loss but surfaced the two blockers above ‚Äî the soak did its job. rc3 re-verifies on cm-prod before the v0.16.10 LTS tag.

## [0.16.10-rc2] ‚Äî 2026-06-13

**Second release candidate ‚Äî observability hardening for the layout-v2 reconcile, found during the rc1 fleet soak on sean-mac (drift verified zero).** No change to the migration's data logic (the gate-2-approved fencing / fail-closed reconciliation is byte-identical); these are the two NON-BLOCKING observability bugs from the rc1 soak, fixed so the cm-prod edge soak (the first host where the v1‚Üív2 reconciliation actually moves data) can audit the migration result and verify zero drift.

### Fixed

- **layout-v2 reconcile always writes a structured result at a consistent path (#1820 soak).** On a no-op reconcile the upgrade left a 0-byte file, and the log message named `state/migration/layout-v2-reconcile/last-apply.json` while the code actually wrote `‚Ä¶-reconcile-upgrade.json` (path mismatch). The reconcile now persists a structured JSON (`status: noop|applied`, agent/isolation migration sub-results, copied/preserved/conflicted/skipped/warnings) at one canonical path (`state/migration/layout-v2-reconcile/last-apply.json`) that the writer, the upgrade log message, and the apply-gated smoke all agree on ‚Äî so every fleet host's migration is auditable.
- **Stale `last-error.json` is cleared on a successful migration pass.** A prior failed `isolation-v2-migrate` error file lingered after a later successful run (this run's `isolation_v2_migration` returned `status: ok`), misleading diagnostics. A successful migration now supersedes the specific `state/migration/last-error.json` it resolves (success-scoped; a genuine error is still written/kept).

### Soak

- rc1 verified **clean on sean-mac (drift 0)**: 8 agents settings-v2-normalized, memory preserved (no strand/fork), v1 tree kept as rollback evidence, rotation pool (incl. #1790/#1789) healthy. rc2 carries only the two observability fixes above; next: cm-prod edge soak ‚Üí v0.16.10 LTS.

## [0.16.10-rc1] ‚Äî 2026-06-13

**Release candidate for the next v0.16 LTS ‚Äî a large reliability + migration train that closes the dynamic-agent restart footgun, makes operator plugin/setting provisioning survive recreate, and lands a gated layout-v2 four-writer migration that stops legacy installs from forking agent memory.** This is an `-rc` prerelease for fleet soak (MacBook + cm-prod edge upgrade-with-migration verification) before the v0.16.10 LTS tag. Every code lane went through the queue pair-review gate (agb-dev-codex); the two data-loss/iso-sensitive lanes (#1857 provisioning preserve, #1820 layout-v2 migration) additionally cleared the independent `patch` adversarial gate, which across four rounds caught real defects the internal review missed ‚Äî a fleet-default class-a/class-b taxonomy downgrade (`@../evil` empty-half + traversal), a foreign-symlink **ancestor** write-escape in the reconciler, and an upgrade dispatch that declared success after a reconcile refusal. The `#1838` tool-policy lane was likewise caught by CI before merge ‚Äî a heredoc-body exclusion had opened a spoofed-admin peer-write bypass (#1806 Tooth1). `agb upgrade` is the same path; the layout-v2 migration runs as one gated, fail-closed, daemon-down step with both-side backups and a v1‚Üív2 reconciliation that never strands or double-writes memory.

### Fixed ‚Äî dynamic agent lifecycle

- **`agent restart <dynamic>` relaunches instead of deregistering + quarantining the transcript (#1852).** Restart killed the session, then the relaunch leg resolved against static roles only, the dynamic was fully deregistered (roster + active-env gone), and the abrupt kill left a resume-unsafe transcript that got quarantined. Restart now detects `source=dynamic`, re-materializes the active-env after every post-kill refresh, and relaunches via the same `--replace` path ‚Äî or fails closed before any kill when the recorded metadata can't reconstruct the launch.
- **Self-restart survives (#1853).** `agent restart <self>` from inside the target session killed the process running the restart before the relaunch ran; it now detaches the relaunch so the survivor completes the marker‚Üíkill‚Üírelaunch, preserving #981/#1769 resume; degrades to fail-closed where no detach facility exists.
- **Per-agent provisioning is preserved across dynamic recreate/`--replace` (#1857).** Recreate dropped operator `enabledPlugins`/`statusLine` despite PRESERVED_USER_KEYS, reset `installed_plugins.json`, and lost skill carriers. The `--replace` path now applies the preserve fold, merges (not resets) the plugin manifest, records provenance in a bridge-owned ledger, and converges `channel-declared ‚à™ fleet-default ‚à™ existing` on every launch; malformed fleet-default tokens class-a skip+warn while traversal/reserved fail closed, and a brownfield no-channel agent snapshots before its first wipe.

### Fixed ‚Äî layout-v2 migration & drift

- **Four runtime writers no longer target the v1 agent tree, with a gated v1‚Üív2 reconciliation (#1820).** The cron memory writer, PreCompact HOME/WORKDIR fallback, per-agent settings render, and doc-sync target all resolved to the v1 `agents/<a>` tree, forking agent memory daily on installs past the v0.8 layout cut. All four now resolve v2-first; a same-release reconciliation runs as one fail-closed, daemon-down, scope-locked migration with both-side backups, content-hash conflict policy (prefix/superset merge, divergent‚Üíarchive+marker+queue-task, idempotent re-apply), atomic workdir-symlink retarget, and a post-apply invariant ‚Äî fencing every read/copy/adopt/archive against foreign symlink ancestors (incl. broken symlinks) and refusing to write the upgrade-complete marker or restart the daemon if reconcile is refused/fails.

### Fixed ‚Äî iso-v2 reliability

- **Queue-gateway exit-code + retry on the iso boundary (#1837, #1834)** ‚Äî exit code now reflects the actual SQLite write outcome (no more false "gateway timed out" thrash), with bounded retry and position-anchored daemon-liveness.
- **Dedicated-agent first-start death + queue-dir ownership (#1836, #1829)** ‚Äî first start is deterministic and `requests/`/`responses/` are owner-correct on success, first-start-death, and rollback.
- **cron, body-file, status, wiki on the iso boundary** ‚Äî iso cron tamper-check no longer false-positives on group-write (#1842) + consecutive-failure escalation surfaces (#1843); `task create --body-file` sudo-as-owner fallback with actionable error (#1835); `agb status` health from daemon-pid liveness not gateway timeout (#1833) + Plugin Liveness wired (#1844); controller-run wiki index-rebuild and monthly/weekly summarize cross the iso boundary cleanly (#1827, #1849).

### Fixed ‚Äî auth, hooks, daemon, cron

- **Pre-#1520 shared admins join the OAT pool (#1855)** ‚Äî an upgrade-time + daemon-hygiene backfill wires the keychain-free `apiKeyHelper` contract for legacy shared admins that were silently keychain-pinned; sync flags `synced_incoherent` when a launch path can't consume the rendered credential.
- **tool-policy cross-agent false-positives (#1822)** ‚Äî quoted-heredoc bodies and top-level globs no longer false-deny admin maintenance; the heredoc-body exclusion gates on the roster-anchored `trusted_admin` predicate so a spoofed-admin peer-write still denies (#1806 preserved).
- **ms365 delegated token keyed by authenticated claim (#1825); cron `--at` naive datetime ‚Üí local tz instead of silent UTC-drop (#1826); reaper keep-audit transition-latched to cut log volume (#1797); smoke daemon-script writes pinned under `BRIDGE_HOME` so an isolated smoke can never stub the live daemon (#1860).**

### Changed

- Templates make background-subagent delegation the default operating pattern (#1821).
- CI: de-flaked the daemon + kappa state-audit smokes (#1856); a residual daemon.sh loop-restart flake under heavy CI load is tracked for a follow-up hardening.

### Release process

- `-rc1` is a **prerelease** for fleet soak. Upgrade order: MacBook (sean-mac) `agb upgrade` **with the layout-v2 migration** (verify zero drift) ‚Üí cm-prod edge ‚Üí if clean, the v0.16.10 **LTS** tag. Problems found in soak ‚Üí fix ‚Üí `-rc2` ‚Üí repeat.

## [0.16.9] ‚Äî 2026-06-12

**Fleet-reliability sweep from a day of live v0.16.8 field reports ‚Äî headlined by the idle-reaper policy fix that was silently killing operator-created agents.** A patch on the **v0.16 LTS line** closing seven issues, every one surfaced by running v0.16.8 on real multi-agent installs the day it shipped. The marquee is a daemon GC fix: the idle dynamic-agent reaper conflated "dynamic" (creation method) with "disposable" and was reaping operator-created long-lived agents ‚Äî including `loop=1` ones ‚Äî confirmed as the root cause of an operator codex pair being destroyed four separate times. Every lane went through the queue pair-review gate (agb-dev-codex), with the reaper, doctor, and daemon lanes additionally gated by the independent `patch` adversarial review; the review rounds caught real defects across the train ‚Äî a `#1709`-class env-assignment guard bypass, a path-traversal escape, a quarantine-bypass, an origin-spoofing hole, and several fail-safe-direction misses. **Every v0.16.x release is an LTS release** ‚Äî v0.16.9 supersedes v0.16.8 as the line head. Same `agb upgrade` path; all changes are fix-class or guard-tightening and degrade safe.

### Fixed

- **Idle dynamic-agent reaper no longer kills operator-created or `loop=1` dynamics (#1795).** `reap_idle_dynamic_agents` killed *any* detached + queue-empty + idle dynamic agent past `BRIDGE_DYNAMIC_IDLE_REAP_SECONDS` and deleted its roster entry ‚Äî correct GC for auto-spawned throwaways, but it also destroyed operator-created long-lived dynamics (live evidence: the same operator codex pair reaped 4√ó). The reap predicate now requires, on top of the existing idle checks, `ephemeral == "1"` (exact) **and** `loop != 1`: disposability is tagged at creation (wave-fixer dispatch + the `agent-bridge --ephemeral` flag), interactive operator spawns stay non-ephemeral, and a `loop=1` agent is unconditionally exempt (reaping it would void the relaunch contract). Migration fail-safe: a roster entry with no flag reads as non-ephemeral ‚áí never reaped (uniform across all five assignment sites). A one-line keep-audit shows when the reaper considered-and-kept an idle agent. `BRIDGE_DYNAMIC_IDLE_REAP_SECONDS` is now documented (OPERATIONS.md) and on the `config set-env` allowlist as a validated non-negative int.
- **Upgrade doc-migration no longer silently rolls back agent memory (#1781).** The upgrade-time migrate-agents pass synced the agent-home `MEMORY.md` over the live `workdir/MEMORY.md` whenever they differed ‚Äî rolling agent memory back to a stale copy on every upgrade (13 of 22 agents on one host). `MEMORY.md` / `users/<id>/MEMORY.md` are now treated as agent-written state: never overwritten when present, still first-materialized from the profile seed when absent, with each preservation audited and kept in the targeted backup set.
- **No-LLM picker auto-resolve stopped false-escalating every idle session (#1783).** Enabling the v0.16.8 picker stage flagged ordinary idle composer screens as `UNKNOWN`-stuck ‚Äî 19 of 22 sessions escalated within minutes, recurring every ~5 min. Both engines' idle composers are now cataloged `non_picker` (empty-composer-anchored, foreground-tail-scoped so a real picker sharing the capture still escalates), the prompt-like heuristic requires an actual option/affordance, and a per-pass storm fuse caps unknown escalations.
- **Post-upgrade `verify-tasks-db` works from the admin agent, and the "unable to open database file" mystery is root-caused (#1786).** The upgrade-complete checklist told the admin agent to run a command the v0.16.8 hook blocks; it now routes through a policy-blessed `agent-bridge doctor --detectors tasks-db`. The reported open failure was not the guard stack ‚Äî a WAL `mode=ro` open without the `-shm` sidecar raises `SQLITE_CANTOPEN` on a healthy db; fixed with an `immutable=1` fallback gated so an unreadable non-empty `-wal` reports `unverifiable`, never a stale `ok`. (Review bonus: the env-assignment prefix form `DB=<path> sqlite3 "$DB"` that could slip past the queue-DB guard is now also denied ‚Äî a #1709-class static-decode extension.)
- **`doctor` no longer guides quarantining a live agent, and stops false-flagging the intended settings layout (#1787, #1788).** On case-insensitive filesystems `orphan-agent-dir` + `agent retire` compared names case-sensitively, so a live agent's directory under alias casing was reported an unregistered orphan *with a quarantine recipe* ‚Äî executing it would dangle the agent's settings. Identity is now inode-based with a three-state contract (match / no-match / indeterminate‚Üíkeep). Separately, `settings-two-tree-drift` flagged the renderer's intended dual-render for every Claude agent and suggested a non-existent entry point; both detectors are now aligned to the renderer-canonical per-layout shape with working recipes.
- **Cron-dispatched child sessions are fenced to their dispatched job, and task-create origin is attributable (#1792).** A cron child inheriting full admin context would "helpfully" act on unrelated in-flight work (creating queue tasks under the admin's name). The dispatch prompt now carries a hard scope fence (control-char-sanitized so a job field can't inject a forged directive), and task-create records an `origin` (`cron:<run_id>` | `session:<id>`) that the server validates against the cron runtime's own trusted anchor ‚Äî a non-cron process can't spoof a cron origin.

### Operational notes

- The us‚Üîcm-prod production A2A pair was restored end-to-end on 2026-06-11 using v0.16.7's `trusted-routed` transport (root cause: Cloudflare WARP corporate forwarding blackhole) ‚Äî bidirectional delivery verified, existing mesh peers unaffected.
- Follow-ups filed from this train: #1797 (reaper keep-audit log volume + ci-select coverage + minor edges), plus the carried external-contrib PRs (#1770 MCP engine-log liveness, #1790 #1789 D1/D2 rotation) and #1789 D3‚ÄìD5 / #1784 picker-judge design, all for a later train.

## [0.16.8] ‚Äî 2026-06-11

**Session-resume + settings/CI reliability sweep, plus no-LLM picker auto-resolve** ‚Äî a patch on the **v0.16 LTS line** closing seven issues that all surfaced from live fleet operation: agent restarts silently discarding live sessions (two distinct mechanisms), the operator-global settings symlink turning self-referential, `agent update --model/--effort` being a silent no-op on static agents, iso v2 agents unable to read their own settings (a production startup-blocking picker), fresh agents rendering an abbreviated HUD, and a CI ratchet whose substring matching cost three false-red rounds in one day. Every lane went through the queue pair-review gate (agb-dev-codex), with the restart/iso/settings lanes additionally gated by the independent `patch` live/VM verification; the review rounds caught real defects in five of seven lanes ‚Äî including a path-traversal escape, a quarantine-bypass in the trusted-resume design, and a fail-open parser edge. **Every v0.16.x release is an LTS release** ‚Äî v0.16.8 supersedes v0.16.7 as the line head. Same `agb upgrade` path; A2A wire protocol unchanged; all changes are fix-class or guard-tightening and degrade safe.

### Added

- **No-LLM picker auto-resolve ‚Äî daemon fingerprint catalog + policy keystrokes + admin escalation skill (#1762).** The daemon now detects interactive pickers (codex update prompts, Claude resume/compact prompts, trust dialogs) by capturing tmux pane content and matching it against a shipped fingerprint catalog (`runtime-templates/shared/picker-catalog.json`), then resolves them with cataloged policy keystrokes ‚Äî no LLM sweep required. Three safety rails: post-resolve re-capture verification, anti-loop guard, and a destructive-option guard (any option containing ASCII `>` pointing at destructive verbs escalates instead). Unknown pickers escalate to the admin via the new `picker-resolve` skill with a verbatim capture for catalog promotion. The blocked agent returns to a state where it can resume its previous session and accept user prompts.
- **Operator plugin display config seeds into new agent homes (#1753).** Fresh Claude agents no longer render an abbreviated HUD: at agent create/start, allowlisted per-plugin display config (default exactly `claude-hud`, extendable via `BRIDGE_SEED_PLUGIN_CONFIG_ALLOWLIST`) seeds **if-absent** from the operator's `~/.claude/plugins/<plugin>/config.json` into the agent home ‚Äî never overwriting a diverged agent-local config, with plugin-id validation + resolved-path containment on both layers (a review round probe-proved and closed a `../../` traversal escape) and a Claude-only engine gate. On the shared/non-iso path the no-overwrite contract is exclusive-create race-closed (`open(‚Ä¶, "xb")`); on iso v2 the copy goes through the controller-publish pattern (`chgrp ab-agent-<a>` 0660) as seed-if-absent on the supported create/start flows, with a documented residual stat-then-publish window (an atomic `publish-if-absent` iso primitive is a noted follow-up, not claimed here).

### Fixed

- **Agent restart no longer silently discards a live session ‚Äî both mechanisms of #1769 closed.**
  - *Mechanism 1 ‚Äî post-kill resolver gate (#1774):* `run_restart`'s #981 session re-inject was defeated for fresh/idle sessions (live-session record only, no eligible transcript): the resolver's #827 shortcut needs an alive pid, so the just-killed id was wiped and the agent relaunched fresh. `run_restart` now validates the live session it is about to kill (record presence, pid alive, cwd match, **not quarantined**) and writes a one-shot trusted-resume marker (`state/agents/<a>/resume.trusted`, 0600, TTL 300s, rewritten per verify-retry kill, Claude-only); the resolver accepts that exact id once with a dead pid ‚Äî **after** quarantine checks, which the #827 live-session shortcut now also enforces (a quarantined id never resolves, live or not; the review caught this bypass in two successive rounds). Marker consume is decision-site-gated so hydration probes can't burn it; terminal clears at every restart exit path.
  - *Mechanism 2 ‚Äî setup-freshness gate (#1777):* `bridge-start.sh`'s `FORCE_FRESH_SESSION` discarded a resolvable resume id **silently** whenever any setup-freshness check tripped ‚Äî the actual cause of a live 18-session mass-fresh event (1.3‚Äì38.8 MB transcripts).
