Every fresh-forcing trip now logs one named line, and on the normal restart path a resolvable resume id plus only re-ensurable trips keeps `--continue` (the five hook-status checks re-ensure unconditionally before exec and die on failure ‚Äî no stale-hook resume path exists). First-boot, `--skip-project-skill`, explicit `--continue`, and Codex paths are byte-identical.
- **`agent update --model/--effort` now reaches static Claude agents (#1763).** The static launch builder never read the roster `BRIDGE_AGENT_MODEL`/`BRIDGE_AGENT_EFFORT` that `agent update`/`materialize-fields` wrote ‚Äî a silent no-op behind "changed: yes". The builder now emits `--model`/`--effort` with roster-wins / preserve-when-empty precedence, deduping both space-form and joined-form baked flags, consuming a follower token as the value slot only when it is a plain value (an option-shaped follower is preserved ‚Äî a review round caught the earlier rule eating an unrelated `--settings` flag). The documented `--set-launch-cmd` workaround keeps working byte-for-byte.
- **iso v2 agents can read their own `workdir/.claude/settings.json` (#1766).** The per-agent `settings.effective.json` rendered controller-owned 0600 under a 0700 parent, so every iso agent hit EACCES on its own project settings ‚Üí a blocking "Settings Error" picker on every (re)start that became load-bearing during a cm-prod restart storm. Render/prepare/reapply now publish the effective file `controller:ab-agent-<a>` **0640** (group-read only, never group-write, never `ab-shared`) with a 0750 traversable parent ‚Äî re-applied after every rerender so the fix can't flap; the `publish_content_tree` planted-redirect guard accepts exactly the canonical self-target symlink (realpath target-validated, dir_fd TOCTOU-safe) while all other symlinks stay refused; and the Settings Error picker is cataloged for auto-resolve (option 3) as a belt-and-braces mitigation. Verified on a real iso v2 VM with kernel-enforced UID boundary tests.
- **Operator-global settings self-reference loop broken (#1759).** On shared-admin layouts where the operator's `~/.claude/settings.json` is a symlink to an agent's own `settings.effective.json`, the #11901 base-read became self-referential for that one agent ‚Äî self-sustaining benign keys, surviving accidental hand-edits, and letting drift-apply rewrite the operator global wholesale. One detection function now classifies the operator-global target (realpath equality ‚Üí `os.path.samefile` inode equivalence for APFS case-variants ‚Üí output-shaped classification) with fail-safe direction: indeterminate output-shaped targets break the loop and degrade to the bridge base, while another agent's output reached one-directionally ‚Äî including the first-render case ‚Äî keeps the verified #11901 inherit. Render, drift-plan, and drift-apply all delegate to the one function; drift-apply additionally refuses to write *through* a symlink doubling as the operator global without `--force-operator-global` (naming the file); preserved user keys survive the loop-break via the #1756 pass.
- **CI iso-helper-ratchet substring false-positives eliminated (#1764).** The boundary-site pattern matched `\.env` inside `os.environ` and flagged test-fixture `settings.effective.json` paths ‚Äî three false-red CI rounds in one day, each costing a diagnose+noqa+push cycle. The pattern is now boundary-anchored (`(‚Ä¶)([^A-Za-z0-9_]|$)`, POSIX-safe, rg-portable), the baseline dropped 1561‚Üí871 across 125 files with a mechanically-audited no-per-file-increase guarantee, and a self-test smoke (live-pattern extraction, the three false-positive shapes as negatives, nine genuine positives, noqa + fail-closed behavior) locks the anchoring against regression.

### Operational notes

- The us‚Üîcm-prod production A2A pair was restored end-to-end on 2026-06-11 using v0.16.7's `trusted-routed` transport (root cause confirmed: Cloudflare WARP corporate forwarding blackhole) ‚Äî bidirectional delivery verified 6/6 with the existing mesh peers unaffected.
- Known cosmetic: `a2a net-status` displays a per-peer `trusted-routed` override under the node-level substrate label; the override applies at send time and functions correctly.
- Earlier deferrals carry forward (#1738, #1699 ‚Äî the daemon.sh cold-boot flake cost ~1 rerun per PR this train, #1711/#1722, #1709 brace numeric-range over-block). PR #1770 (external, MCP engine-log liveness) is in review for a later train.

## [0.16.7] ‚Äî 2026-06-10

**`trusted-routed` A2A transport + a hooks/settings reliability pair** ‚Äî a patch on the **v0.16 LTS line** whose marquee is an operator-directed capability-add (allowed on the LTS line per `docs/release-lines.md` when operator-directed; shipped here rather than a v0.17 beta so `lts`-channel production installs can actually receive it): a new A2A transport kind for corporate-routed trusted private networks, restoring a down production cross-bridge pair. Every lane went through the dual gate (agb-dev-codex + patch); the A2A lane converged over three rounds with each reviewer catching a distinct real blocker. **Every v0.16.x release is an LTS release** ‚Äî v0.16.7 supersedes v0.16.6 as the line head.

### Added

- **`transport.kind: trusted-routed` ‚Äî direct private-IP bind on a trusted routed network, no WARP/Tailscale required (#1758).** For hosts on a corporate-routed private network (the operator's trust call), the receiver's bind proof is "the configured `listen.address` is assigned on a real local interface" ‚Äî the WARP/Tailscale enrollment half is dropped for this kind ONLY. Everything else stays fail-closed: loopback/wildcard binds refused, an **explicit `listen.address` is required** (empty hard-fails `bind_unresolved` ‚Äî no Tailscale auto-select fallback), unknown kinds hard-fail, and HMAC / `inbound_allowlist` / source-addr check / dedupe are byte-untouched. **Per-peer transport override**: a peer entry may carry its own `transport.kind`, and the sender now picks the egress source per destination ‚Äî mesh peers keep the pinned mesh source, routed-marked peers get the OS-routed source (fixes the measured "Network is unreachable" when a mesh-pinned source can't reach a routed peer). A typo'd per-peer kind degrades that one row to retry (never halts the whole outbox runner); a typo'd node-level kind still fail-closes the receiver bind. Docs: `docs/a2a-cross-bridge.md` trust model + rollout recipe.

### Fixed

- **Dynamic-agent every-prompt "UserPromptSubmit hook error" eliminated (#1755).** Two-layer fix: (1) the bridge hook was registered in global + workdir scopes with different interpreter spellings, so Claude Code's exact-string dedupe ran both concurrently ‚Äî all hook ensure paths now record the pinned interpreter and converge stale-spelling duplicates in place on the next ensure pass; (2) `save_timestamp_state` (and same-class call sites) move to unique per-instance temp files with **no error swallow** ‚Äî all rename errors propagate to the existing #1205 iso-fail-open/controller-re-raise policy (a review round caught that swallowing rename errors after the unique-temp change would mask real write failures; a forced-failure regression pins it).
- **Operator-pinned session preferences survive settings re-renders (#1756).** `model`, `alwaysThinkingEnabled`, `agentPushNotifEnabled` join `PRESERVED_USER_KEYS` (the #1689 statusLine data-loss class ‚Äî live-confirmed: a pinned model silently reverted after an upgrade re-render). The `link-shared-settings` adoption step now folds existing user keys into the render input at symlink takeover, runs the launched-channel sticky-false repair in the same position as the render paths (a review round caught that the fold could otherwise re-disable a launched channel plugin, #1453 class), and a global `model` inherits through the #11901 safety filter.

### Known issues

- **#1759** ‚Äî on shared-admin layouts where the operator global is itself a render-output symlink, the #11901 base-read is self-referential for that one agent (loop guard + operator-input/output split designed, not yet implemented).
- **#1762** ‚Äî no-LLM picker auto-resolve (daemon fingerprint catalog + policy keystrokes + admin escalation skill) is in implementation; next train.
- Earlier v0.16.6 deferrals carry forward (#1738, #1699, #1711/#1722, #1709 brace numeric-range over-block).

## [0.16.6] ‚Äî 2026-06-10

**Mesh-robustness + read-guard hardening, plus an upgrade/settings/daemon reliability sweep** ‚Äî a patch on the **v0.16 LTS line** that makes the A2A mesh resilient to transient peer churn, gives admins a durable install env-override path, comprehensively closes the static-decodable bypass class in the shared-secret read-guard, and folds in a reliability batch (queue-gateway crash-loop fix, upgrade-time orphan reaper, settings-render conflict collapse, shared-global settings inheritance, A2A receiver-supervisor robustness, and a fresh-install admin-identity scaffold fix). Every lane was gated by per-PR + full-branch agb-dev-codex review; the security/settings/daemon-critical lanes (read-guard #1709, test-bind state-path #1728, shared-global settings #11901) additionally went through the independent `patch` write-gate, and the WARP bounce-gate (#1733) was verified on a real Cloudflare-WARP mesh host. The dual-gate review repeatedly earned its keep ‚Äî it caught a series of real read-guard bypasses on #1709 (converged over many rounds) and an all-denied-global silent-drop on #11901 that the per-lane review missed. **Every v0.16.x release is an LTS release** ‚Äî v0.16.6 supersedes v0.16.5 as the line head and does not revoke the prior LTS designation. Same `agb upgrade` path; A2A wire protocol unchanged (`PROTOCOL_VERSION` `a2a-enqueue-v1`); all changes are additive or guard-tightening and degrade safe.

### Added

- **Audited durable install env-override ‚Äî `config set-env` (#1734).** Admins now have a durable, write-gated path to set install-level environment overrides without hand-editing tracked files: a JSON-only `config set-env` surface behind the existing write-gate ‚à© config-set contract, so the override survives upgrades and never leaks into the source tree.
- **Shared static agents inherit the operator's global `~/.claude/settings.json` (#11901).** A shared (non-isolated) static Claude agent now inherits the operator's system-global `~/.claude/settings.json` as the bottom-most render layer, so operator-global changes (e.g. `agentPushNotifEnabled`) propagate to shared agents ‚Äî while per-class defaults (static `autoCompactWindow` stays 400k), bridge hooks, and per-agent keys still win on top. A safety denylist filters credential/machine-specific keys (`apiKeyHelper`, `env`, `permissions`, `statusLine`, `awsAuthRefresh`, credential-shaped names) before inheritance, emitting one `[info]` line naming any dropped key; a missing/unreadable/malformed global fail-safe-degrades to the existing bridge base. The operator home is resolved via the controller-home path (not the per-agent `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`), and isolated (iso-v2) agents are explicitly unaffected.

### Fixed

- **WARP bounce gate honors the transient-peer class ‚Äî cross-lane #1732 √ó #1733 (integration-review catch, #1742).** The full-branch integration review caught a cross-lane regression no per-lane review could see: Lane B (#1732) made transient peers a distinct quiet/no-alarm class, but Lane A's (#1733) WARP bounce classifier still treated every configured peer as bounce-relevant ‚Äî so a transient (expected-disconnect) peer going down could bounce the tunnel, and a transient peer's stale state could even suppress a real persistent-loss bounce. The bounce gate now builds its loss proof from bounce-relevant (`peer_alarm_on_unreachable`, i.e. persistent default + explicit override) peers only; transient peers are excluded from the proof.
- **A2A transient-peer resilience ‚Äî quiet-pend, no-drop, seamless resume (#1732).** A peer that briefly disappears (transient tailnet/WARP blip) is no longer hard-dropped from the mesh: the reconcile loop quiet-pends a transient-suspect peer, holds its roster entry, and resumes delivery seamlessly when it reappears ‚Äî instead of tearing the peer down and forcing a full re-join. Composes with the #1733 bounce-gate so a transient loss neither drops the peer nor bounces the tunnel.
- **WARP tunnel-bounce gated on real peer-loss, not handshake-idle (#1733).** The WARP tunnel-health auto-bounce was too aggressive ‚Äî it fired on handshake *idle* (no traffic on a healthy mesh), severing a live tunnel. A full bounce now requires actual peer-loss signal: stale + N‚â•2 consecutive streak + ‚â•1 fresh suspect/down peer + soft-refresh-first; all-fresh-up or stale-unknown is a hard no-bounce. The connection is threaded to tunnel-health which reads the prior-tick peer FSM. New env knob `BRIDGE_A2A_WARP_HANDSHAKE_STALE_SECONDS` tunes the stale threshold. Verified on a real Cloudflare-WARP mesh host (no idle self-bounce; recovers on genuine peer loss).
- **Shared-secret / peer-home read-guard ‚Äî static-decodable bypass class comprehensively closed (#1709).** `hooks/tool-policy.py`'s read-guard for `shared/secrets`, `shared/private`, and other agents' homes resolved a read's *spelling* but not the path Bash actually resolves, so a range of static spellings bypassed it. The guard now MODELS the resolved path across the full static-decodable surface and denies on resolved-containment: `cd`/`pushd` cwd grammar (positional, subshell, `pushd`/`popd`/`cd -`), `&&`/`||`/`;` conditional-execution chains (short-circuit precedence, a cd-OUT-fail "sticky" fail-branch, `!`-negated cd exit), reserved-word/quote-removal tokenization, `$BRIDGE_HOME`/`~`/`$HOME` prefix expansion, `..`/`//`/`.` folding, glob enumeration (`ls sec*ets`, `cat */token`), ANSI-C `$'‚Ä¶'` decode (incl. hex/octal/unicode), and bash brace expansion (`{secrets,wiki}`, ranges, nested/cartesian ‚Äî with a cap-truncation fail-close and quote-aware expansion so quoted/escaped braces stay literal). Runtime-only indirection (`eval`, command-substitution, `$var` read-prefix, symlink-through) is explicitly out of the static-analysis scope ‚Äî covered by filesystem permissions (iso v2) + #1738/#341 ‚Äî and this hook remains defense-in-depth over the static-path class. Closes the v0.16.5 read-bypass class.
- **Test-bind state-path guard ‚Äî no live `rooms.db` clobber (#1728).** A local test mesh that overrides only `BRIDGE_HOME` could write into the live `state/handoff/rooms.db` because `handoff_dir()` honors `BRIDGE_STATE_DIR` over `BRIDGE_HOME`. A `BRIDGE_A2A_ALLOW_TEST_BIND`-gated guard now refuses to bind when the resolved state path points outside the (test) home, preventing a test run from clobbering live mesh state. Production installs are unaffected (the flag is test-only). Resolves the v0.16.5 known issue.
- **Queue-gateway socket-listener crash-loop stopped (#1652).** The queue-gateway socket listener could enter a crash-loop: it unlinked a socket that still had a live pid (premature `clean_stale`), an unguarded `FileNotFoundError` in the socket group/mode refresh hard-failed on a transiently-missing socket path, and an empty connect-probe `recv` churned `invalid_payload`/`send_failed` log noise. The daemon now requires N consecutive probe failures before unlinking a socket whose pid is alive (`bridge_queue_gateway_socket_probe_persistently_dead`), narrowly catches the missing-socket `FileNotFoundError` to degrade instead of crash, and routes an empty probe through a quiet `_ProbeClose` path. Live socket-transport verified in an isolated `BRIDGE_HOME`.
- **Upgrade-time reaper for leaked codex-broker + queue-gateway orphans (#1567).** Upgrades now reap the accumulated backlog of already-leaked orphans ‚Äî `ppid==1` codex `app-server-broker` processes whose disposable worktree `--cwd` is gone, and stale `bridge-queue-gateway` procs whose `--bridge-home` no longer resolves. Conservative by default: a dry-run report + a high-priority admin cleanup task with the safe-kill recipe; nothing is killed inside the upgrade unless `AGENT_BRIDGE_REAP_CODEX_ORPHANS` is set. Guarded by a one-shot migration marker (runs once, skips new installs), an `lstart`-anchored PID-reuse guard, and positive disposable-worktree provenance so it can never match a live owned broker.
- **Upgrade no longer prompts on cosmetic settings.json differences (#1675/#1694).** A Homebrew `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3` interpreter path and rendered absolute `.agent-bridge/hooks` paths now canonicalize to the same form as the tracked template, so a purely cosmetic interpreter/path difference no longer triggers a settings.json merge-conflict prompt during upgrade. Real edits (different hook basename, bare/venv interpreter, unrelated hooks directory) still fall through as genuine conflicts.
- **Fresh-install admin workdir identity no longer clobbered by the codex-pair template (#1750).** On a managed-project install the admin's codex pair shares the admin's workdir (#1492); the start-time identity reconcile (#1417) carried neither create-time shared-workspace guard, so the pair's first start copied *its* home identity (SOUL/SESSION-TYPE/CLAUDE) over the admin's workdir ‚Äî and since the runtime resolves identity from the cwd copy, a fresh admin booted believing it was the codex pair-programmer, mis-applying onboarding. A roster-aware ownership guard now decides by the identity the workdir actually carries (whole-token canonical-position name match ‚Äî `patch` structurally cannot match `patch-dev`; longest-match tie-break; a stale-but-mine copy still refreshes from home per #1417; indeterminate ownership fail-safes to keeping per-agent identity in `agent_home`, never rendering a sibling template).
- **A2A receiver supervisor no longer floods the admin queue on macOS tailnet conditions (#1679, #1680).** On a macOS A2A host the receiver supervisor misclassified *environmental* tailnet conditions as receiver faults: a self-route flap (the host transiently can't reach its own tailnet IP) produced bursts of false `healthz_timeout` ‚Üí 51 bogus crash-loop tasks over 2 days (#1679), and a tailnet-IP loss (Tailscale down/wedged) crash-looped the fail-closed bind, permanently stopped auto-restart after 5 tries, and emitted ~100 alert tasks over a 53h outage with no auto-recovery (#1680). The supervisor now discriminates environment from fault: on a `/healthz` timeout it first probes self-reachability (an outbound connect to its own already-bind-proven address) ‚Äî a self-unreachable host HOLDS the healthy receiver (`healthz_unreachable_self`, no restart-budget burn, no crash-loop task) while a reachable host still restarts a genuinely-wedged serve loop (the #1405 path is preserved); a `bind_fail` from an absent tailnet IP is treated as a retryable environmental state (`env_bind_ip_absent`) that re-probes and auto-rebinds when the IP returns instead of burning the restart budget; and the per-cycle alert flood collapses to a single auto-clearing "receiver environmental incident" task. The fail-closed bind proof, HMAC, allowlist, and dedupe are untouched ‚Äî the new discriminators are read-only.

### Internal

- **CI: `lint-heredoc-ban` job timeout 5m ‚Üí 10m (#1747).** The heredoc-ban baseline ratchet runs a genuinely CPU-heavy audit (`audit-footgun-11.sh --tsv`, ~3.5min) that was being killed at the 5-minute wall on congested runners, cancelling otherwise-green PRs. Raised to 10m (no scan-logic change).
- **CI: `unit/static smoke` job timeout 20m ‚Üí 25m (#1752).** The required-smoke battery grows with every release wave and legitimately runs ~17-18m healthy by v0.16.6; the 20m cap cancelled green runs twice in one day under normal runner congestion (the same false-red mode as the original #1509 10m cap). The comment now carries a split-don't-bump guideline if the healthy baseline crosses ~20m.

### Known issues

- **#1709 brace numeric-range truncation** ‚Äî a brace whose *numeric range* overflows the expansion cap under a bridge cwd currently fail-closes (a safe-direction over-block, never a leak); precise exemption of pure-numeric-range truncation is deferred to a v0.16.x follow-up.
- **#1738** ‚Äî env-trust wrapper redesign for the class-(b) runtime-indirection tail of the read-guard (eval/`$()`/`$var`-cd/symlink); covered today by FS perms, deferred.
- **#1699** ‚Äî de-flake two chronic-slow required smokes (the `unit/static smoke` battery runs long); deferred.
- **#1711 / #1722** ‚Äî deferred follow-ups.

## [Unreleased]

## [0.16.5] ‚Äî 2026-06-09

**Zero-touch A2A Rooms mesh** ‚Äî a feature release on the **v0.16 LTS line** that turns multi-node A2A Rooms into a self-healing mesh: a k8s-controller-style reconcile loop runs on the handoff daemon tick and continuously converges each node's *actual* network state (stable address, tunnel health, peer reachability, applied roster epoch) toward the only human input ‚Äî *desired* room membership + transport config. New nodes join from a room token, a leader relays member‚Üîmember traffic, the roster anti-entropies itself, and `agb a2a net-status` becomes the control-loop's status window. The mesh is **transport-agnostic** (identical on `cloudflare-warp-mesh` and `tailscale`; only stable-address acquisition and tunnel-health probing live behind per-transport adapters) and preserves every receiver security invariant (fail-closed bind proof, HMAC, allowlist, dedupe, `remote_addr`, two-factor admission). Delivered as a 7-lane wave (L0 reconcile framework ‚Üí L1‚ÄìL3 self-heal adapters ‚Üí L4 token-join ‚Üí L5 leader-relay + roster ‚Üí L6 net-status v2), each lane gated by per-PR + full-branch agb-dev-codex review **and** the receiver lanes additionally by an independent adversarial skeptic sweep and a patch receiver review; the integration branch was VM-verified on a Linux iso v2 install (fresh `agb upgrade` + 7/7 lane smokes) and exercised by a real-daemon 5-node churn run that confirmed zero-touch convergence of peer-down‚Üírejoin and kick‚Üíconverge‚Üíretire. The full-branch review caught a real cross-lane config-path seam (fixed pre-cut). **Every v0.16.x release is an LTS release** ‚Äî v0.16.5 does not supersede the prior LTS designation. Same `agb upgrade` path; A2A wire protocol unchanged (`PROTOCOL_VERSION` `a2a-enqueue-v1`); all new mesh state is additive and degrades safe on installs with no rooms/mesh config.

### Added

- **Reconcile control-loop framework (#1705-#1708, L0).** A durable, idempotent `reconcile_once` sequence (`bridge_reconcile_common.py`) layered onto the existing handoff daemon tick: each step is eligibility-gated by a per-step durable backoff (cap + exponential + jitter in `reconcile.db`), event-driven AND periodic anti-entropy, and fail-safe (a raising/erroring step degrades to a no-op + audit, never crashes the tick). The store is created only by the daemon's own attempt path ‚Äî viewer/status reads never materialize it.
- **Self-heal adapters ‚Äî stable address, tunnel health, peer reachability (#1705/#1706/#1707, L1‚ÄìL3).** Per-transport adapters that (L1) detect and converge the node's stable listen address (Tailscale identity-keyed `tailscale ip`; WARP a hardened utun/Mesh address ‚Äî never a guessed CIDR) and rebind through the single `resolve_bind()` oracle on IP drift; (L2) probe tunnel handshake freshness and bounce a stale WARP tunnel within a bounded budget; (L3) advance a per-peer `UP‚ÜíSUSPECT‚ÜíDOWN‚Üí(recovery)‚ÜíUP` hysteretic state machine and record a desired LAN‚ÜíWARP rebind when the local listen address vanishes. All fail-closed and active-transport-only.
- **Token-bootstrap room join (#1695 P1, L4).** A new node joins a room from a room token: the wire carries only `sha256(token)`, the controller derives a per-pair key via HKDF domain separation (`a2a-room-pair-seed-v1` ‚Üí `a2a-room-pair-key-v1`) and persists the *derived* key, and auto-pair registers the unknown peer only **after** a valid token + `remote_addr` + HMAC (two-factor admission, all mutation deferred until post-HMAC). Auto-join is env-gated with the default unchanged.
- **Leader-relay + roster anti-entropy (#1695 P2, L5).** A room leader relays member‚Üîmember traffic by re-signing with the leader‚Üîtarget pair key (never reusing the origin signature), with a `relayed_via` loop-guard and a deterministic forward-id for dedupe; `relayed_from` provenance is trusted only after the receiver confirms the authed peer is the cached leader. The roster anti-entropies via a durable `room_roster_outbox` (kick/leave/deny broadcast + reconcile-tick re-broadcast), a monotonic epoch split-brain tiebreaker, and bounded retirement ‚Äî a kicked member loses access and the mesh converges without operator commands.
- **`agb a2a net-status` v2 ‚Äî control-loop status window (#1708, L6).** Additive, read-only enrichment of net-status so a human can confirm the daemon is already self-healing: own stable address, room leader + IP, allowed agents, room roster + epoch + last-sync, tunnel freshness, per-peer `UP/SUSPECT/DOWN`, last reconcile tick, auto-recovery counts, and roster-epoch convergence. Strictly additive to the #1697 v1 schema (every v1 field byte-identical), read-only/non-creating (never materializes reconcile.db), no secrets, active-transport-only.

### Fixed

- **Reconcile desired-config writers honor the active `serve --config <path>` (#11573, cross-lane).** The full-branch integration review caught a config-path seam: the stable-address reconcile writer persisted desired listen-address drift to the default config (`a2a.config_path()`) regardless of the custom config a `serve --config <path>` receiver had loaded, so a custom-config daemon drifted the wrong file forever and never converged. The active `config_path` is now threaded into `stable_local_addr` and `peer_reachability_step`, which write `config_path or a2a.config_path()`; default-path behavior is byte-identical.

### Known issues

- **#1722 ‚Äî relay reachability over-claim** (relay-resistance hardening of the leader-relay path); deferred to a v0.16.x follow-up.
- **#1728 ‚Äî test-bind state-path guard.** A local test mesh that overrides only `BRIDGE_HOME` can write into the live `state/handoff/rooms.db` because `handoff_dir()` honors `BRIDGE_STATE_DIR` over `BRIDGE_HOME`; a `BRIDGE_A2A_ALLOW_TEST_BIND`-gated guard (refuse-when-state-points-outside-home) + doc is deferred to v0.16.6. Production installs are unaffected (the flag is test-only); the workaround is to also override `BRIDGE_STATE_DIR` (and the rooms/reconcile/inbox/outbox DB paths) under the test home.
- **#1729 ‚Äî extend the mesh verify matrix** with S2 rebind-*success* and S4/S5 relay live legs on an orbstack registered-agent VM (the loopback churn harness proved detect+fail-closed but couldn't bind a second substrate address or terminate a relay at a real inbox); deferred to v0.16.6.

## [0.16.4] ‚Äî 2026-06-09

Guard over-block hardening patch over **0.16.3** on the **v0.16 LTS line** ‚Äî a wave that relaxed several false-deny friction points in the `hooks/tool-policy.py` containment/audit guard, fixed a WARP-mesh receiver crash-loop, and added A2A transport observability. Every lane was gated by per-PR + full-branch agb-dev-codex review **and** independent local adversarial review; the integration branch was VM-verified on a Linux iso v2 install. The dual-review gate caught real read-gate bypasses on the higher-risk lanes (see Known issues for the one lane that was descoped). v0.16.4 is a v0.16.x LTS-line patch ‚Äî it does **not** supersede the v0.16.3 LTS designation; **every v0.16.x release is an LTS release**. Same `agb upgrade` path; A2A wire protocol unchanged (`PROTOCOL_VERSION` `a2a-enqueue-v1`).

### Added

- **`agb a2a net-status` ‚Äî active-transport observability (#1697).** A new read-only verb (alias `agb a2a status`) reports the install's `bridge_id` / transport / listen address / receiver (pid + healthz) / substrate health / configured peers / room membership, so a co-resident agent can answer "what transport is this install actually on, and is it healthy?" without acting on a stale Tailscale assumption after a transport switch. **Active-transport-only**: it probes only the configured substrate (a `cloudflare-warp-mesh` install never shells `tailscale`). Zero state mutation, stable JSON shape (`--json`), no secrets in output. Receiver-liveness delegates to the same healthz path as the daemon (so it inherits the #1701 WARP fix below rather than re-reporting a healthy WARP receiver as down).

### Fixed

- **WARP-mesh receiver no longer crash-loops a healthy `/32`-bound receiver (#1701).** On a `cloudflare-warp-mesh` install the A2A receiver binds a point-to-point /32 WARP tunnel IP that cannot hairpin a TCP connection to itself, so the `GET /healthz` self-probe always timed out and the daemon supervisor crash-looped a receiver that was actually serving peers (bricking the node's A2A/rooms). `cmd_healthz` now falls back, **for that transport only**, to a dependency-free socket-held liveness check (a plain `bind()` of the same address:port returns `EADDRINUSE` ‚áí the listen socket is held ‚áí healthy). Tailscale/LAN keep full HTTP wedge-detection (their self-probe hairpins normally).
- **`state/tasks.db` is readable, writes stay denied, and the read-intent classifier is hardened fail-closed (#1690).** The queue DB gained a read-intent carve-out (a `cat`/`stat`/etc. read is allowed; the "use `agb` queue commands" contract still blocks all writes/redirects/mutations, with no admin bypass). Closing that carve-out exposed pre-existing fail-open gaps in the shared read-intent classifier, which were hardened: dynamic-loader/preprocessor env prefixes (`LD_PRELOAD`/`LD_AUDIT`/`GCONV_PATH`/`DYLD_*`/‚Ä¶) are treated as exec surface, Bash 5.3 `${ ‚Ä¶ }` function-substitution and other shell embeddings fail closed, `awk` is scanned only in its program word, `uniq`/`view`/`file`/`yq` write/exec flag surfaces are gated, and an unresolved path-expansion (`$VAR`/`${VAR}`/`~`/brace) blocks the protected-path carve-out so a variable-spelled forbidden sibling can't slip through. Sibling protected-path gates fire even when the same command also names `tasks.db`.
- **Admin Bash reads of a peer agent's home are no longer falsely denied (#1692).** An admin (`is_admin_agent`) read-intent Bash read of a peer-home file (`cat <peer>/MEMORY.md`) is now allowed, matching the non-Bash Read-tool behavior ‚Äî but **only** reads, **only** for admin, and **only** peer-home: admin writes/mutations stay denied, non-admin peer-home reads stay denied, the system-class carve-out is preserved, and the team-shared private/secret subtrees stay denied even for admin (least-privilege).
- **Read-only viewer commands are classified read-intent + a prose over-fire is fixed (#1693).** `strings`, `hexdump`, `comm`, `fold`, `expand`, `paste`, and `csvlook` are now read-intent so a benign `strings <roster>` / `hexdump -C <file>` is no longer false-denied by the roster/system-config/queue gates (a write/redirect through a viewer stays denied). The shlex-failure short-needle fallback was narrowed to a redirect/assignment/path **position** test, so a prose mention of `hooks/`/`state/cron/` inside an unbalanced-quote command body (e.g. a `git commit -m` with an apostrophe) is no longer treated as a protected-path reference ‚Äî while a real redirect/assignment to a protected path stays denied.

### Known issues

- **#1691 ‚Äî intent-aware credential/shared-alias *mention* gates (descoped from this release).** The attempt to let a non-admin command merely *list/search/mention* a credential filename or protected-path name (while still denying an actual file *open*) hit the same fundamental fragility as the substring-based protected-path gates: distinguishing "opens the file" from "mentions the name" via substring analysis is structurally vulnerable, and the dual-review gate found two distinct real bypasses in the candidate (a broadened peer-home gate and a `find -exec` credential read). It is deferred to be redone as an architecture-level change **bundled with #1709**. Today the affected gates stay conservatively over-blocking, which is the safe default.
- **#1709 ‚Äî substring protected-path gates miss brace/`$BRIDGE_HOME` path spellings** (pre-existing). To be addressed by the same suffix-matching + obfuscation-fail-close redesign as #1691.
- **#1711 ‚Äî non-Bash admin Read can access the shared secret/private subtrees** (over-permission vs the Bash least-privilege behavior added in #1692); to be tightened for parity.

## [0.16.3] ‚Äî 2026-06-08

Stabilization patch over **0.16.2** on the **v0.16 LTS line** ‚Äî nine fixes surfaced by the v0.16.2 fleet rollout plus an operator-directed `lts` upgrade channel, processed as a 9-lane wave-orchestration (per-lane internal codex + per-PR agb-dev-codex Phase-4 + a full-branch integration review) and VM-verified on a Linux iso v2 install. v0.16.3 is a v0.16.x LTS-line patch ‚Äî it does **not** supersede the v0.16.2 LTS designation. Same `agb upgrade` path; A2A wire protocol unchanged (`PROTOCOL_VERSION` `a2a-enqueue-v1`).

### Added

- **`lts` upgrade channel ‚Äî version-line pin for conservative installs (#1687).** `agb upgrade --channel lts` pins an install to the highest non-prerelease tag *within the LTS series* (root `LTS_SERIES` pointer, currently `0.16`) so it doesn't auto-jump off the LTS line when a higher minor ships. A dedicated sticky `state/upgrade/channel` records the choice (precedence: explicit CLI `--channel`/`--version`/`--ref` > special-cases > recorded sticky > legacy `stable`); `AGENT_BRIDGE_UPGRADE_CHANNEL` is transient and `--version`/`--ref` are one-shot (neither overwrites the sticky). The series resolver is full-match (not prefix), skips prereleases, and **fails closed** (clear remediation, non-zero rc) if `LTS_SERIES` is missing/malformed or the sticky write can't be verified ‚Äî never a silent fallback to `stable` that would slip an LTS install off-line. Default behavior is unchanged for non-`lts` installs.

### Fixed

- **`upgrade --apply` self-restart no longer reads as a failure to the invoking session (#1662).** An upgrade that restarts its own session was observed exiting 137 (SIGKILL of the parent during agent restart), which an invoking wrapper read as failure even though the upgrade had completed. An upgrade-complete marker is now written after the work and before the restart, promoted post-restart, and surfaced in `--json` (atomic tmp+rename, dry-run suppressed) ‚Äî the marker is the source of truth, so a self-restart exit is expected-success rather than a spurious error.
- **`agb upgrade`/`rollback` lock is held through the full critical section (#1667).** The daemon-control lock token was captured in a `$()` subshell whose fd closed immediately, releasing the lock early and re-opening the concurrent-upgrade race that #1661 was meant to close. The token now lives in a shell global (no subshell fd-close) and the refuse-fast path uses `flock -n` (not the invalid `flock -w 0`); a serialization smoke proves the fd is held across the critical section.
- **cron-runner no longer discards a whole child payload over a missing `summary_short` (#1677).** A `null`/empty/absent/over-long `summary_short` previously produced a fatal error-envelope that threw away the entire cron child result (silent data loss in `state/cron/runs/`). It now derives `summary_short` from the first non-empty line of `summary` with visible truncation (197 chars + `‚Ä¶`). An empty `summary` and a bad `forward_target` stay fatal; the error-envelope path was not generalized.
- **Settings rerender preserves an operator-added `statusLine` (HUD) (#1689).** The isolated-home settings rerender that runs on every upgrade/restart/relink carried forward only an allowlist of user keys, which did not include `statusLine` ‚Äî so an operator-configured statusLine (e.g. a claude-hud HUD) silently vanished after a bridge upgrade. `statusLine` is now in `PRESERVED_USER_KEYS`, so an operator-added (or `hud_usage_tap`-patched) statusLine survives every rerender; an unrelated user setting still does not leak through.
- **`rematerialize-agent-identity --dry-run` no longer blanks the agent in its JSON (#1670).** The helper ran `shift 5` before sourcing `bridge-lib.sh`, which re-execs Bash 3.2‚Üí4 and replayed the original `$@` ‚Äî blanking the agent argument on the stock-shebang path (dry-run reported `agent=""`). It now slices positionals via `"${@:6}"` and adds a Python-side agent-preserved guard.
- **`link-shared-settings` is idempotent on an iso-owned existing symlink (#1672).** A re-run hit `FileExistsError` when the target symlink already existed and was owned by an iso UID. The link step now catches the idempotent case (resolving the existing link's real path via a fail-closed `sudo readlink -f`); a wrong-target or non-symlink at the path still re-raises.
- **A2A receiver self-heals after an upgrade left it on stale code (#1685).** A pre-v0.16.1 ‚Üí v0.16.x first upgrade never restarted the tailnet receiver, so it kept running stale code and silently 429'd inbound A2A. A daemon-tick detector (receiver boot marker `state/handoff/receiver-boot.json` vs `last-upgrade.json.updated_at`) now performs a guarded one-shot auto-restart (preflight-before-stop, systemd-aware, fully fail-safe on a malformed marker). Ships with an operator upgrade-guide note for the manual receiver restart on a pre-v0.16.1 first upgrade.
- **Teams `server.ts` emits a clear `EADDRINUSE` diagnostic (#1671).** A port collision now reports `HOST:PORT` + the `TEAMS_WEBHOOK_PORT` knob instead of an opaque stack trace (diagnostic-only ‚Äî no kill primitive, since cross-UID provenance of the holder is unprovable; the watchdog still self-heals).
- **Deterministic `1659` multijob smoke fixture (#1683).** The cron cadence-health smoke used a wall-clock-relative fixture that failed inside a Sunday-22:00‚ÜíMonday-09:00 UTC window, intermittently red-failing the required static smoke on unrelated PRs. The fixture is now weekday-independent (fixed `hourly ‚àí 4d` offset), verified across 672 synthetic times/week with zero ordering failures, and still catches a latest-run-per-agent regression.

## [0.16.2] ‚Äî 2026-06-08

Stabilization patch over **0.16.1** ‚Äî four robustness/perf fixes surfaced by the v0.16.1 fleet rollout (macbook / SYRS mac-mini / cm-prod, the last a Linux iso v2 production host). Every fix was codex pair-reviewed (per-PR + a full-branch integration review) and the integration branch was VM-verified on a Linux iso v2 install with the **flock backend** ‚Äî the path that exposed two real production bugs the macOS (mkdir-backend) tests could not (see #1661). Same `agb upgrade` path; A2A wire protocol unchanged (`PROTOCOL_VERSION` `a2a-enqueue-v1`).

### Fixed

- **`agb status` is no longer O(cron-run-records √ó window-minutes) (#1659).** On a host with a large `state/cron/runs/` backlog the dashboard's cron cadence-health walk re-ran the cron matcher tens of millions of times (‚âà84s CPU on a 5,632-record host ‚Äî a timeout risk for any `cron`/monitor that shells `agb status`). The pure cron matcher is now memoized (`allowed_values(expr,min,max) ‚Üí frozenset` behind a bounded `lru_cache`; `field_matches` is a membership test), and `last_cron_run_by_agent` reduces to the latest run per distinct `(agent, job/schedule)` before the cadence check ‚Äî preserving the in-cadence latch + most-recent-timestamp aggregation bit-for-bit (a multi-job agent with an older-healthy + newer-stale schedule still classifies correctly). Render now scales with distinct schedules, not total historical run rows.
- **`upgrade --apply` preserves its intended exit code when stdout is closed (#1660).** A `BrokenPipeError` at a `migrate-agents` (or any other) JSON emit no longer surfaces as a misleading non-zero exit (observed: exit 144 on a *successful* migration when a concurrent run closed the capture pipe). A new `emit_json(payload, rc)` helper flushes inside `try`, redirects stdout to devnull on `BrokenPipeError`, and returns the command's intended rc ‚Äî applied to all JSON emit sites. No global `SIGPIPE` handler (a completed migration that only broke at emit exits 0, not 141). Commands that intentionally return non-zero (e.g. `cmd_verify_tasks_db`) keep their code.
- **`agb upgrade`/`rollback` now hold a singleton lock ‚Äî concurrent runs on the same install refuse fast (#1661).** Previously two `upgrade --apply` against one `BRIDGE_HOME` raced the daemon + agent restart (observed: a 5-process thrash on a real host). A new shared `lib/bridge-lock.sh` (flock-first, `mkdir` fallback) acquires `state/locks/upgrade.lock` for the mutating `upgrade --apply` and non-dry-run `rollback` paths only; the second concurrent run refuses fast with a clear `already running (pid ‚Ä¶, started ‚Ä¶)` diagnostic (opt-in bounded `--wait`). **This fix also closed two bugs that made the lock non-functional on every Linux (flock) host: `flock -w 0` is rejected by real `flock(1)` (the refuse-fast path now uses `flock -n`), and a bare `exec {fd}>>file 2>/dev/null` had permanently muted the shell's stderr.** Lock release is integrated into the existing upgrade exit-handler; child spawns (daemon/receiver/agent restart) drop the inherited lock fd.
- **An upgrade-conflict sidecar can no longer cascade-fail iso agent launches (#1663).** If an upgrade left a `*.upgrade-conflict` backup (mode 0600, owner-only) inside a dev-plugin source dir, the iso plugin-cache build hit `PermissionError` reading it and aborted the *entire* cache ‚Äî taking down every iso agent on that plugin (observed as a real outage on a cm-prod iso v2 host). The cache overlay now skips upgrade/VCS sidecars (`*.upgrade-conflict`, `*.orig`, `*.rej`, merge-tool sidecars, `.git/.hg/.svn`) by pattern, and guards each entry so a single unreadable/unknown file is skipped with a warning instead of aborting ‚Äî **except** required plugin-contract material (`plugin.json`, `server.ts`/`server.js`, `package.json`, `mcp.json`), which now fails loud (`install-failed`) rather than silently shipping a broken plugin.

## [0.16.1] ‚Äî 2026-06-08

Stabilization + hardening patch over **0.16.0**. A post-0.16.0 fix wave (handed via the SYRS mac-mini and local patches) plus an A2A audit follow-up, iso-upgrade robustness, an MS365 cross-plugin token-refresh path, and a complete `set -u` hardening of the roster-map accessors. Every PR was codex pair-reviewed (the gate caught real defects across the wave ‚Äî a cross-agent scaffold-write escape, a CLI no-upn exit-0, and the full nounset accessor set); the candidate was verified on a Linux VM (`agb upgrade --channel current` clean on an iso v2 install ‚Äî 12 agents incl an iso agent migrated with no abort, daemon healthy ‚Äî plus the per-fix Linux smoke battery). Same `agb upgrade` path; A2A wire protocol unchanged (`PROTOCOL_VERSION` `a2a-enqueue-v1`).

### Added

- **MS365 `get_valid_token` ‚Äî guaranteed-valid token for trusted in-fleet callers (#1650).** A new ms365 MCP tool **and** a `bun server.ts get-valid-token [upn]` CLI subcommand return a currently-valid Graph `access_token` (transparently refreshing via the stored `refresh_token` when expired/near-expiry, reusing the existing pre-call expiry check + `SingleFlight` coordination) so a sibling caller (e.g. the CRM proxy) that previously read the token file directly ‚Äî and so used a stale token ‚Äî gets a fresh one. Returns `{ upn, access_token, expires_at, expires_in_seconds }`; **never** returns the `refresh_token`; emits a redacted `ms365_token_issued` audit. Live-verified end-to-end on real M365 (expired token ‚Üí auto-refresh ‚Üí fresh token, no `Authentication required`).
- **Post-restart auto-wake for auto-restarted sessions (#1639).** After an upgrade/daemon/watchdog auto-restart, the fresh Claude session no longer sits idle: `bridge-start.sh` propagates `BRIDGE_AUTO_RESTART_WAKE=1` on non-attach launches and `bridge-run.sh` fires a first-turn inbox-bootstrap wake, scoped to non-cron claimed/blocked work (a cron-dispatch-only queue never spuriously wakes). An operator's interactive `--attach` launch never auto-wakes; a pending `NEXT-SESSION.md` handoff suppresses it; an empty queue on a long-lived agent injects nothing.
- **`agb urgent --from <sender>` sender-attribution override (#1640).** `agb urgent` accepts `--from` to set the sender on the urgent interrupt (wired through `bridge-send.sh`).
- **`agb list` marks controller-blind iso agents as `[iso]` (#1637).** An iso v2 agent whose workdir the controller cannot stat now renders as `[iso]` instead of a blank/error column.

### Fixed

- **iso-agent upgrades no longer abort on the backup scan (#1635).** The upgrade backup scan graceful-skips iso-owned files the controller cannot stat (per-UID isolated tree), instead of aborting the whole upgrade with a `PermissionError` traceback ‚Äî the v0.16.0 upgrader aborted here on an iso v2 install. The `remember()`/`exists()` probe and the copy path are both tolerant.
- **Agent `_template` scaffolding propagates to the workdir on upgrade (#1636).** `rematerialize-agent-identity.sh` now also add-missing-only copies the non-identity `_template` scaffolding (`.claude/commands/`, `raw/captures/*/.gitkeep`, `session-type-files/`, `codex/` except `AGENTS.md`) to the agent workdir ‚Äî previously only the identity files propagated, so existing agents silently missed new scaffolding across upgrades. Skip-existing (never clobbers a user-customized file), reuses the iso-UID write path, graceful-skips on PermissionError, and a per-agent `target_dir` containment guard refuses any symlink ancestor that would escape the agent's own workdir (cross-agent write protection).
- **Complete `set -u` hardening of roster-map accessors (#1627).** Every `BRIDGE_AGENT_*` associative-map access on the launch hot path ‚Äî per-agent reads, `[[ -v ‚Ä¶ ]]` predicates, key-iterations (`${!MAP[@]}`), and existence probes ‚Äî is now guarded by `bridge_var_is_assoc`, so an undeclared or scalar-clobbered map can no longer abort a fresh agent launch/restart under `nounset` (`<agent>: unbound variable`). Closes the partial #1626 fix (which covered only `source`/`provenance`/`precompact_notify`); now also `loop`, `continue`, `start_policy`, `model`, `effort`, `permission_mode`, the class/action key-iterations, and the `exists`/`session_id`/`created_at` probes (which previously used a bare `declare -p` / unanchored grep that false-positives on a scalar).
- **settings.json cosmetic-only diff no longer forces a spurious `.upgrade-conflict` (#1638).** An upgrade where `.claude/settings.json` differs only by hook-event ORDER or `python3` vs `/usr/bin/python3` is treated as semantically identical (preserve live, no conflict); a genuine hook-set change still conflicts. Tight interpreter allowlist (`python3`/`/usr/bin/python3` only).
- **A2A inbound backpressure fails OPEN on a count-read error (#1623).** A WAL/read error in the open-task count no longer fail-closes inbound A2A with a 503 (which silently blocked a peer); it fails open.
- **A2A sender deliver-loop per-row guard + outbox retry/backoff (#1628, #1618).** One malformed outbox row can no longer abort the whole deliver batch; a manual retry resets the attempt counter so backoff restarts.
- **A2A fresh-arrival nudge + read-only nudge DB (#1633, #1634).** An inbound handoff posts a fresh-arrival nudge marker so the daemon wakes the target; the daemon nudge helpers open the queue DB read-only (no empty-DB fabrication).
- **A2A receiver cycles on `--restart-daemon` (#1614); `GET /healthz` exempt from the request semaphore (#1645).**
- **Deferred pending-attention staleness ‚Äî one unified task-ref gate (#1619).** A deferred tmux inject for a task that has since been done is dropped at flush (the gate now covers arrival / `task #N` / completion headers, not just `[task-complete]`); read-failure/missing-task fail-safe to replay.
- **Daemon socket loop-restart CI flake hardened (#1644).** The `scripts/smoke/daemon.sh` loop-restart no longer aborts with a cryptic `first_pid: unbound variable` on a slow cold-boot ‚Äî `first_pid` is initialized and the initial-listener wait budget widened.
- **Runtime/cron/wiki:** shared-mode agents use the operator HOME (keep `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` per-agent) + upgrader credential consolidation (#1622); migrate-agents roster-restricted, skips non-roster orphans (#1611); cron capture-runner hotfixes folded in (#1625); upgrade-complete wiki report step + indented-code wikilink false-positives fixed (#1616).

## [0.16.0] ‚Äî 2026-06-07

Official **0.16.0 stable**. Built on the rc1-rc3 foundation (A2A Rooms P1-P4, the daemon-singleton supervision redesign, and A2A inbound backpressure + the B1-B8 receiver security audit ‚Äî see the rc sections below) plus a post-rc3 feature/fix wave. Every PR was codex pair-reviewed; the wave was verified on a Linux VM (`agb upgrade` dry-run clean + the full per-feature smoke battery green). Same `agb upgrade` path; A2A wire protocol unchanged (`PROTOCOL_VERSION` `a2a-enqueue-v1`).

### Added

- **Bounded AskUserQuestion for autonomous agents (#1569).** An autonomous bridge agent that calls the interactive multiple-choice `AskUserQuestion` tool no longer hangs on the picker: a PreToolUse intercept denies the tool and routes the question to the agent's channel with a bounded wait (`BRIDGE_ASKUSERQUESTION_WAIT_SECONDS`, default 30s), resolving a channel reply (numeric ‚Üí option label) or taking an autonomous fallback (reversible ‚Üí proceed-with-note, high-stakes ‚Üí blocked). `BRIDGE_ASKUSERQUESTION_BOUND=0` restores the raw picker. Live-verified in a real session: no picker renders, no hang, other tools unaffected.
- **A2A whole-room fan-out ‚Äî `agent-bridge a2a send --room` (#1594).** Send one message to every eligible member of an A2A room (aliases `room send`, `room talk --fanout`). Sender membership is OS-actor anchored from this node's roster cache / `rooms.db` (a non-member is refused before anything leaves either leg); same-node members deliver via the durable queue, remote members via the existing room-scoped A2A path (HMAC + room epoch); per-recipient partial-failure reporting. The receiver fail-closed `room_scoped_check` is untouched ‚Äî the sender-side gate is purely additive.
- **A2A Cloudflare One / WARP-Mesh transport (#1595).** A2A is now transport-pluggable: alongside the default Tailscale transport, a `cloudflare-warp-mesh` kind binds the receiver to a WARP Mesh device IP only after proving the IP is assigned to a real local interface AND WARP is connected + registered/enrolled (a nonzero `warp-cli` registration probe can only confirm a negative, never prove enrollment ‚Äî fail-closed on any uncertainty; CIDR shape is never proof). Tailscale + raw-IP back-compat preserved exactly; HMAC / dedupe / `remote_addr` / allowlist / `room_scoped_check` unchanged.
- **Agent Bridge operating-manual index skill (#1604).** A pointers-only "operating manual" index skill (situation ‚Üí SSOT routing table + absolute-rules checklist + SSOT document map) installed for all Claude session types, with a Codex project-`CLAUDE.md` pointer ‚Äî one entry point to the operating canon with zero doctrine duplication. SSOT pointers resolve across engines/session-types (the isolated-home renderer normalizes `~/.agent-bridge/` ‚Üí the absolute `BRIDGE_HOME`).

### Fixed

- **Stop-hook drain skips daemon-owned `[cron-dispatch]` work (#1596).** The Claude/Codex Stop-hook inbox-drain no longer re-enters the model for daemon-owned cron-dispatch rows the daemon itself closes (a wasted LLM turn). One shared actionable predicate filters them (title-prefix as SSOT; a `cron:`-actor fallback only for untitled rows; a `[cron-followup]` carve-out), iterates the queued/claimed lists so a real task sitting behind a cron row still blocks, and re-checks/fails-open as late as practical. Queued-first, claimed anti-abandonment, loop-guard, and fail-open invariants intact; a nonzero `find-open` exit fails open even when its stdout is parseable.
- **`agb upgrade conflicts adopt` guards against marker/unparseable sidecars (#1601).** `conflicts adopt` now scans for unresolved conflict markers and syntax-checks the `.upgrade-conflict` sidecar before overwriting the live file, and refuses (controlled nonzero, no traceback, sidecar preserved) when the prior-live `.pre-adopt` backup cannot be created ‚Äî so adopting a marker-laden or backup-less sidecar can no longer brick the upgrader's own backend. `--force` escape hatch retained.
- **`agb upgrade --ref <tag> --dry-run` previews the requested ref (#1602).** The dry-run preview now reads the requested ref's tree via `git ls-tree` / `git show` (no checkout, no working-tree mutation) instead of previewing against whatever ref the source checkout happened to sit on. The `--apply` path is unchanged (it still checks out the ref) and dry-run/apply parity is enforced.
- **migrate-agents re-materializes stale workdir identity copies (#1598).** `agb upgrade` migrate now re-materializes each agent's workdir identity copy (not just the home managed-block), through the iso safe-writer with a shared-workspace guard and a backup-before-overwrite, so a copy-model agent's workdir no longer drifts stale behind its home.

### Deferred ‚Äî live validation pending infra

- **2-node A2A rooms fan-out live test (#1594)** and **2-node WARP-Mesh transport live test (#1595)** are deferred until the multi-node / WARP-enrolled infrastructure exists. Both are smoke-verified (single-node + the full fail-closed battery) and adversarially reviewed; the rooms fan-out reuses the previously-validated room-scoped wire path. Only the cross-node / WARP *live* leg is pending ‚Äî the code is shipped and gated fail-closed.

## [0.16.0-rc3] ‚Äî 2026-06-06

Third release candidate for **0.16.0**. The marquee is **A2A inbound backpressure + security-receiver stabilization** ‚Äî a live incident where "A2A that used to work stopped working" root-caused to a backpressure ratchet bug, fixed alongside an 8-finding adversarial audit of the A2A receiver ‚Äî bundled with six accumulated fixes / hotpatch-upstreams. Each PR codex pair-reviewed; the A2A audit went through two adversarial NO-GO rounds (a `Retry-After: inf` remote-DoS among the catches) before GO, and the cross-PR `_schedule_retry` reconciliation was verified on both smokes. Same `agb upgrade` path; no wire-protocol/schema change vs rc2 (`PROTOCOL_VERSION` `a2a-enqueue-v1` unchanged).

### Added / Fixed

- **A2A inbound audit ‚Äî B1-B8 delivery defects (Track A, #1589).** A live `crm-dev ‚Üí patch` outage where inbound A2A silently stopped was root-caused to a **backpressure all-time-count bug**: the open-task count counted the entire dedupe history instead of currently-OPEN tasks, so any peer past the cumulative cap got **503-rejected forever** (cm-prod permanently blocked at 200). The fix counts only OPEN tasks via a separate read-only connection ‚Äî the original ran `ATTACH`/`DETACH` inside an open `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`, which fails deterministically on SQLite 3.51 and 503-rejected every capped peer, a remote-triggered DoS the CI smoke missed because it only exercised the autocommit path. An adversarial multi-agent audit surfaced seven more (B2-B8): large-body reaper corruption on a symlinked home (resolved-vs-unresolved path compare), crash-consistency (no task duplication on a mid-deliver crash), a stale-pending backpressure + room-gate bypass, a recover `created_by` mismatch, and a slow-loris pre-auth header DoS (absolute request deadline). The fail-closed receiver bind / HMAC / `remote_addr` / allowlist / dedupe boundary is untouched.
- **Two-cap Retry-After (Track A reconciliation with #1575-B).** A sender's untrusted peer `Retry-After` is honored as a hard delivery floor (respecting receiver backpressure) **bounded by a separate `delivery_max_retry_after_seconds` cap (default 600s)**, while the exponential backoff keeps its own `delivery_backoff_ceiling_seconds` cap (default 120s). The two caps coexist because they defend different threats ‚Äî backoff-ceiling protects us (DoS-by-sleep), the Retry-After cap protects the peer (backpressure honor). The opt-in trust gate requires an exact boolean `True` (a string `"false"` no longer fails open), a non-finite `Retry-After` (`inf`/`nan`/`1e400`) can no longer crash the deliver loop, and a trusted `Retry-After` is sanity-capped at 3600s.
- **A2A sender outbox backoff ceiling cap (#1575-B).** The outbox retry backoff default ceiling dropped 3600s ‚Üí 120s with a configurable `delivery_backoff_ceiling_seconds` (env `BRIDGE_A2A_BACKOFF_CEILING_SECONDS`), so a recovered peer's high-attempt retry rows no longer idle dormant for 16-60 minutes.
- **smoke-test LIVE-state isolation (Track C, #1588).** `scripts/smoke-test.sh` no longer leaks into the operator's live `~/.agent-bridge` state ‚Äî it clears smoke-owned controller path vars and exports temp-rooted state / daemon-log / task-db, asserting every controller path stays under a temp prefix (fixes the recurring `#365 / SCRIPTDIR_STARTUP_OK` false-failure on clean checkouts).
- **Daemon routine idle-nudge gates on real interaction, not bare attach (#1568).** The periodic queued-task idle-nudge previously skipped whenever any tmux client was attached, so under a persistent-client multiplexer (every tab attached) the proactive nudge never fired. It now skips only when the session is genuinely busy (`bridge_tmux_session_inject_busy`), matching the urgent path; an attached-but-idle session is nudged. (#1568 stays open for the fuller recent-keypress/focus design.)
- **`lib/bridge-core.sh` de-heredoc + `lib/bridge-path-utils.py` (#1466).** Upstreams a lost footgun-#11 fix ‚Äî the remaining heredoc-stdin / here-string sites in `bridge-core.sh` (a Bash 5.3.9 `read_comsub`/`heredoc_write` deadlock class) are converted to `python3 -c` one-liners / process-substitution / a new file-as-argv path helper.
- **agent-bridge dispatcher skips roster load for pure queue verbs (#848).** `show`/`claim`/`done`/`cancel`/`update`/`handoff`/`summary`/`create` no longer pay the `bridge_load_roster` cold-start cost ‚Äî they exec `bridge-task.sh`, which owns its own lazy roster loading (`handoff` target validation preserved).
- **PreToolUse hook hardening (#1574).** Fixes a Python 3.12+ `SyntaxWarning`, pins the hook interpreter, de-duplicates hook registration, and narrows a #341 config-path false-positive that over-blocked legitimate `shared/` report writes ‚Äî scoped to a single decidable inert quoted-heredoc-write shape (three adversarial bypass classes verified still denied; the credential guard is not weakened).

## [0.16.0-rc2] ‚Äî 2026-06-06

Second release candidate for **0.16.0**, landing the **#1563 daemon supervision redesign** ‚Äî a crash-only supervisor that self-aborts a genuinely wedged scheduler tick (and never a healthy-but-busy one), an admin-liveness escalation path that escalates instead of self-healing, and an A2A-receiver health gate that ends the ~9-minute bind-fail crash-loop ‚Äî plus a confirmed watchdog-scan daemon wedge, the slow-tick per-agent-pass cost, and A2A sender-side diagnosis/recovery hardening surfaced from a live tailnet incident. Eight PRs (PR-1..8), each codex pair-reviewed; the Phase-4 gate caught a real defect on nearly every code PR. Same `agb upgrade` path; no schema/contract change vs rc1.

### Added / Fixed

- **Daemon singleton hardening ‚Äî owner-record with start-time-proof eviction (PR-1, #1564).** The daemon singleton now records its owner with an absolute process start-time proof, so a stale lock from a dead PID (or a recycled PID) is evicted deterministically instead of either blocking a legitimate start or letting two daemons race. Preserves the #1388 fd-cloexec guard and the #1463 launchd-keepalive no-thrash invariant.
- **Daemon audit false-positive fix (PR-1b, #9882).** Two audit-shape bugs: (A) `self_check` escalation is now gated on a positive proof of a second live daemon rather than the absence of a negative, and (B) `daemon_started` is emitted exactly once per boot (single-emit invariant) ‚Äî so the audit trail no longer shows phantom double-starts or self-check escalations against a healthy singleton.
- **T1 runner-process self-abort + T0 OS-init crash-only restart (PR-2, #1572).** Each scheduler tick runs under a runner-process supervisor with a deadline of **max-step-budget + grace** (NOT nudge-latency); a genuinely wedged unbounded step self-aborts (the daemon exits non-zero and the OS init system restarts it crash-only), while a healthy long step under its own bounded timeout ‚Äî heartbeat-refreshed before AND after ‚Äî is never aborted (the flapping-monitor negative control). The cross-tick escalation counter is persisted to a per-key state file so it survives the supervised-child boundary instead of resetting every tick.
- **T2 admin-liveness escalation ‚Äî escalate, don't self-heal (PR-3, #1576, #9819 A/B).** When the daemon's mechanical checks detect a down admin/peer it enqueues a durable admin task instead of trying to fix it. The admin-liveness predicate is conservative: `alive` for ANY live session (working/starting/picker-blocked/idle), `down` only with no live session AND a file-backed daemon heartbeat stale past `BRIDGE_DAEMON_ADMIN_DOWN_STALE_SECS` (default 900s) ‚Äî a busy/long-turn admin is never classified down. A failed escalation task-create is now audited (`daemon_escalation_task_create_failed`) with retained retry state (no swallowed `|| true`, no hot-loop); the admin's codex pair (patch-dev) is the recipient only after the admin-down predicate holds; an MCP-liveness give-up escalates to an admin task, and an admin-self give-up routes to patch-dev (audit-only fallback when no pair exists).
- **A2A receiver health gate ‚Äî kill the ~9-minute bind-fail crash-loop (PR-4, #1573, #9813 + #9819 C).** The receiver supervisor now classifies the exit cause (transient bind/network availability vs auth/config) and applies a bounded backoff + a circuit breaker keyed by (peer/config, error_class), escalating once per cooldown; a successful bind resets the breaker, and a real exit after a previously-healthy bind restarts promptly. A loadable-but-empty-peer-secret config is HELD as `auth_config` (not thrashed through the transient backoff) ‚Äî the exit-cause override reuses the receiver's full `load_config` + `validate_config_peer_secrets` contract. The fail-closed tailnet bind proof / HMAC / remote_addr / allowlist / dedupe are byte-for-byte unchanged.
- **iso v2 urgent instant-wake for always-on isolated agents (#9981, via #1571).** `agent-bridge urgent <iso-agent>` previously failed to write the pending-attention instant-wake marker into the isolated agent's data dir (the controller is not a member of the `ab-agent-<a>` group), so urgent silently degraded to poll-latency (~1‚Äì2 min) for iso agents. The pending-attention spool is now anchored on the controller-owned `state/agents/<a>/` leaf (which the controller can write) and group-published readable for the iso agent to consume; the old 200-retry-then-give-up is replaced by a fast-fail one-warning. Durable queue delivery was always intact; this restores the instant-wake.
- **Daemon-supervision false-positive-control integration teeth + docs (PR-5, #1577).** A new `1563-pr5-fp-control-matrix.sh` integration smoke proves the combined supervisor never punishes a healthy daemon while still catching real wedges (healthy-long-step ‚Üí no abort, wedged-unbounded ‚Üí abort within max-step+grace, stale-timer disarmed between ticks, singleton race ‚Üí one survivor, escalation task-create-fail ‚Üí audit+retry, admin-self MCP-giveup ‚Üí patch-dev, A2A transient ‚Üí backoff vs real-crash ‚Üí bounded-restart). Docs: corrected the A2A Rooms P4 status (leader/invite/join/roster-broadcast IS wired and multi-node-verified) and documented the daemon supervision contract + the rc1 sealed-paste / spool-reaper / stop-drain features.
- **Watchdog drift-scan no longer wedges the daemon (PR-6, #1580).** The daemon's watchdog gate called `bridge-watchdog.sh scan` with no timeout before the 30s-bounded `--json` call, so a scan that hung in a directory-walk loop blocked the daemon main loop forever (confirmed live with `sample` ‚Äî killing the hung `bridge-watchdog.py` child resumed the tick). Both scans now route through a new `bridge_run_command_with_pgroup_timeout` (`BRIDGE_WATCHDOG_SCAN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`, default 30s) that process-group-kills the hung scan subtree (no orphan), and the new ceiling is coupled into the PR-2 self-abort deadline so a healthy operator-raised scan is never false-aborted. (The required-smoke CI suite, which grew across these daemon PRs, also had its `unit/static` timeout raised 15m ‚Üí 20m.)
- **Slow daemon ticks ‚Äî cadence-gate the expensive periodic per-agent passes (PR-7, #1581, closes #1579).** `cmd_sync_cycle` ran ~33 passes serially, several iterating every agent every 5s tick, so `tick_age` drifted to 30‚Äì45s on a real roster. The expensive periodic health/housekeeping passes (channel-health, plugin-liveness, memory-refresh, stall-reports, unclaimed-queue/marker sweeps, nudge-late-success sweep, context-pressure-scan, crash-reports) are now cadence-gated to ~30s (each env-overridable) via a new `bridge_daemon_pass_due` helper, while every time-critical delivery/escalation/liveness pass (queue-gateway, attention-flush, cron-dispatch, A2A ticks, nudge, admin-liveness escalation, MCP-liveness give-up recovery, heartbeats) stays every-tick. Each gated pass refreshes the PR-2 progress heartbeat before its due-check, so a skipped tick never false-aborts.
- **A2A diagnosis + recovery hardening (PR-8, #1582).** Surfaced from a live tailnet outage where the death was environmental but the bridge's diagnosis (both sides blamed each other's receiver) and recovery (exponential backoff kept messages dormant for tens of minutes after the peer recovered) prolonged it. Adds a directional leg classifier (`agb a2a diagnose-stuck` ‚Üí `peer_receiver_unreachable` / `local_tailnet_degraded` / `peer_tailnet_degraded` / `transport_dead_path_unknown`, from local healthz + peer-TCP + tailscale tx/rx); a **probe-gated, transition-gated backoff recovery reset** (when the stuck-scan sees a retry row and a TCP probe to that peer succeeds, it resets the peer's retry rows to deliver immediately ‚Äî still-unreachable peers keep their backoff + dead-letter, leased rows are never touched); an actionable stuck-alert body (`next_attempt_in_seconds` + last TCP-probe result + a "TCP healthy but backoff-waiting" line); and ack history preservation (the last error is no longer NULLed on ack). TCP `peer:port` is the health oracle (never `tailscale ping`); there is **no** automatic `tailscale up`/`down`; the fail-closed receiver bind/HMAC/allowlist/dedupe boundary is untouched.

## [0.16.0-rc1] ‚Äî 2026-06-05

Release candidate for **0.16.0**, capping the `0.16.0-beta1..4` line with a 6-item stabilization wave (each codex pair-reviewed). Same `agb upgrade` path; no schema/contract change vs beta4.

### Fixed / Added

- **#1455 ‚Äî settings single-tree drift doctor (#1556).** Read-only `agb doctor` detectors `settings-two-tree-drift` + `settings-multi-tree` (realpath-keyed dedup) flag a managed agent whose HOME vs workdir `.claude/settings.json` diverge; no writer/render change.
- **#1459 ‚Äî cron-dispatch backlog recovery + run/queue reconcile (#1557).** Daemon-wired backlog recovery with a separate `cron_dispatch_*` audit taxonomy (never the human-unclaimed/nudge path) + three run/queue reconcile cases guarded by worker-evidence so a live cron run is never false-positived.
- **#1417 ‚Äî lifecycle identity sync-on-start + `agb agent set-onboarding` (#1558).** Managed-project agents (workdir‚âÝhome) re-materialize the workdir identity copies FROM HOME on start ‚Äî identity files only, strictly-newer-HOME mtime guard, fail-closed, iso v2 controller-published ‚Äî so HOME edits / onboarding-state changes actually propagate to the copy the runtime reads.
- **#9773 ‚Äî surgical codex app-server subtree reap on teardown (#1560).** Reaps only the torn-down session's pane-subtree codex / Pencil-MCP children, verified by absolute start-time identity (`ps -o lstart=`), so a leaked codex app-server is cleaned without ever touching a live roster codex or in-progress review.
- **#9780 ‚Äî Stop/turn-end inbox auto-drain, claude + codex (#1562).** A turn-end hook drains a genuinely-claimable queued task instead of idling, behind a fail-open infinite-loop guard (id+status marker, atomic-persist-before-block) that preserves the #1199 queued-vs-claimed split.
- **#1367 ‚Äî auth sealed-paste: operator-terminal `agb auth claude-token receive` (#1559).** Echo-off `/dev/tty` read so the operator's Claude token is never read into an agent transcript / argv / env / queue / audit; fails closed with no controlling tty. The agent-context refusal + PreToolUse hook wrapper-detection are documented best-effort deterrents (the shared-UID residual ‚Äî where an agent can only store its own token ‚Äî is out of the threat model; on iso-v2 the registry's controller-UID ownership is the real FS boundary).

## [0.16.0-beta4] ‚Äî 2026-06-04

A **beta** on `0.16.0-beta3` landing the **create‚Üíisolate‚Üíonboard completeness** fixes for linux-user isolated agents ‚Äî surfaced from a live multi-user onboarding. Two create-path fixes: first-create credential seeding so an isolated Claude agent does not auto-start authless, and a **TOCTOU-safe root publish** of the six identity profile files (plus a metadata-aware watchdog classifier). Both went through codex pair-review (the profile publish converged over three rounds down to symlink-race safety and audit parity); same `agb upgrade` path, no schema/contract change vs beta3.

### Fixed

- **First `agent create --isolate` for a linux-user Claude agent could auto-start before its per-agent credential was seeded ‚Üí authless first session / "Channels not available" (#1520b, PR #1531).** The create path now seeds the per-agent credential during isolation prepare (after `bridge_linux_prepare_agent_isolation`, before the ~20s daemon auto-start window) so the agent's first managed session starts with working auth instead of racing the daemon. Linux-user isolation only; non-fatal (the agent is held from authless auto-start until the credential is present).
- **First `agent create --isolate` left the six identity profile files (SOUL/CLAUDE/SESSION-TYPE/MEMORY/MEMORY-SCHEMA/TOOLS.md) at `iso-uid:<controller-group> 0600` instead of `ab-agent-<a>:0660`, so the controller/watchdog could not read them (#1520c, PR #1532).** The controller's stale supplementary-group cache (KNOWN_ISSUES ¬ß28) blocks the #1506 recursive normalize from traversing the freshly-`chown -R`-ed `2770 ab-agent` workdir, so the profile files were missed on first create (the workdir dir was correct; only the files were stranded). A narrow, **root-forced** publish now sets exactly those six basenames to `ab-agent-<a>:0660` after prepare's `chown -R`, independent of the controller's group cache. The publish is **TOCTOU-safe**: a standalone root helper opens the workdir as an `O_DIRECTORY|O_NOFOLLOW` directory fd, `openat()`s each basename `O_NOFOLLOW` (a planted symlink is refused via `ELOOP`, never followed), `fstat`-verifies a regular file owned by the iso UID, then `fchown`/`fchmod`s the **open fd** ‚Äî no profile pathname is re-resolved after the check, closing the swap-between-check-and-mutation race. It deliberately excludes HEARTBEAT.md (controller-owned), CHANGE-POLICY.md (shared symlink), and the v3 channel-state `.env`; it is non-fatal (warn + `profile_publish_failed` audit row on any per-file failure, including a refused symlink). The watchdog's `classify_scan_error_category` now anchors the expected group to the **workdir directory's own group** (the setgid-inheritance contract) rather than string-swapping the iso username, so a long agent name (OS user truncated to 32 chars vs the hash-truncated group) is no longer misclassified as a publish gap. Because `isolate --reapply` shares `bridge_linux_prepare_agent_isolation`, it runs the same publish ‚Äî giving operators a native recovery path for an already-stranded agent.

## [0.16.0-beta3] ‚Äî 2026-06-04

A **beta** on `0.16.0-beta2` bundling a new-items wave: the marquee items are **per-agent Claude config isolation for shared-mode agents (#1520)** ‚Äî hardened across multiple adversarial review rounds down to the BASH_ENV/allexport process-startup boundary ‚Äî and a **generic plugin `requires` resolver (#1528)** so an agent created with one plugin channel auto-provisions the channels that plugin declares it needs. Also lands the multi-user **Teams/M365 OAuth callback demux (#1524)**, three isolation/upgrade fixes (#1516/#1517/#1518), and the hermetic-CI fix that ends the recurring `unit/static smoke` false-red (#1527/#1509). Every item is codex pair-reviewed and CI-green; same `agb upgrade` path, no schema/contract change vs beta2.

### Added

- **Generic plugin `requires` resolver on `agent create` (#1528).** A plugin's `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` may now declare an optional top-level `"requires"` array of canonical channel specs (`plugin:<name>@<marketplace>`); the create path transitively expands them into the resolved `--channels` set (BFS with ordered dedupe, cycle detection, depth cap, and warn-and-continue on an unresolvable dependency ‚Äî create is never blocked), emitting `[info] <plugin> requires <‚Ä¶> ‚Äî adding`. This fixes the half-provisioned-agent trap where forgetting a dependency channel yields an agent that looks created but can't authenticate or call its API. **Zero domain hardcoding in core** ‚Äî it forwards only whatever the manifest declares; a no-`requires` channel set is byte-identical to before. `--dry-run` shows the expansion. Introduces create-time plugin-manifest read (previously isolation-time only) via the existing `bridge_resolve_plugin_install_path`.
- **Multi-user Teams/M365 OAuth `/auth/callback` demux (#1524).** When a single Azure bot app fronts N per-agent listeners, the OAuth callback (which carries only `?code&state`, no agent identity) couldn't be routed. The ms365/teams plugins now embed the agent id in the OAuth `state` as `<BRIDGE_AGENT_ID>.<uuid>` (validator allows `.`, rejects `/`+backslash; `AGENT_TAG` strictly `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}`), so a front router can demux `/auth/callback` by the `state` prefix while single-listener installs keep a plain uuid (no regression). Shipped to the marketplace as teams + ms365 `0.1.1` (#1526). Live-validated on a production multi-user deployment.

### Fixed

- **Shared-mode `agent create` Claude agents launched without `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` ‚Üí per-agent config/MCP/credential isolation silently ignored (#1520).** A shared (non-linux-user) Claude agent's final launch never set `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`, so the launched `claude` read the operator-global `~/.claude.json` instead of its pre-seeded per-agent `<agent-home>/.claude`. The launch now exports `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` (and **only** that ‚Äî HOME stays the shared home; Codex agents excluded; the iso/v2 secret-env path untouched) for shared Claude launches. Auth-continuity is preserved across the switch: the per-agent credential is **seeded on demand** before the export (single-agent `claude-token sync`, working for static **and** dynamic agents), keychain-free (apiKeyHelper) auth is export-safe without a credential file gated on a **proven** keychain-free state (Darwin + validated settings/helper/registry ‚Äî never the platform-blind enable flag), and if no credential can be seeded the export is skipped (one-time warn, operator-global fallback) so a previously-authed agent is never stranded. Hardened across adversarial review down to the startup boundary: the keychain-free **proven flag** is `unset` at true process entry (drops any inherited export attribute before the first fork/re-exec), and an inherited `BASH_ENV` running `set -p -a` can no longer leak the ambient-secret captures or the proven flag ‚Äî `builtin set +a` clears inherited allexport unconditionally at entry, before STEP A, so a skipped (caller-spoofable, defense-in-depth) `bash -p` re-exec cannot reintroduce the leak. The residual `builtin`-keyword shadow is the pre-existing same-UID #1454 boundary, not a new regression.
- **`upgrade --apply` silently DOWNGRADED a beta install to the latest stable (#1516).** With no `--ref`, the `stable` channel skips pre-release tags, so a beta install would jump *backward* to the newest stable on a routine `upgrade --apply`. A semver downgrade guard (new `bridge-release.py compare` + `--allow-downgrade`) now aborts a backward move (exit 64 + JSON), scoped to the stable+`--ref` path, after capturing the installed version and before checkout/pull.
- **First `agb room create` on a fresh iso host failed closed (#1517).** With no `rooms.db` yet, the actor resolution had nothing to anchor the controller against and returned `actor_unresolved`. `agb room create`/`adopt-all` now controller-only auto-bootstrap the **canonical** `rooms.db` (derived from the task-DB home, never an env-redirectable path), idempotently, before actor resolution.
- **Dead `chmod 0755` on the legacy mirror clobbered by later normalization (#1518).** A #1513 follow-up: the layer-2 `chmod 0755` on the legacy mirror dir was overwritten by the later recursive isolation normalization (the mirror ends `2750`, still iso-traversable) ‚Äî dead code. The isolate path now applies the mirror leaf mode **last** (after the grant-matrix + install-tree passes) and asserts the final iso-traversable state, covering the split-root layout the data-root passes don't reach.

### Internal / CI

- **`unit/static smoke` recurring false-red fixed ‚Äî it was a chronic job timeout, not a content flake (#1527, closes #1509).** The required-smoke suite legitimately runs ~9.5‚Äì10m on `ubuntu-latest`, but the job capped at `timeout-minutes: 10`; normal runner variance (+20s) tipped it over and GitHub reported the `timeout-minutes` kill as conclusion `cancelled` ‚Äî read as the "kappa flake" but actually a boundary timeout that admin-blocked several otherwise-green PRs. Raised the cap to **15m** (matching `integration-smoke`). Secondary: the `beta5-2-kappa-state-audit-reconcile` static-introspection assertions now snapshot every source-under-test once via `git show HEAD:<path>` (object store, immune to working-tree mutation), closing the source-mutation-race class.

## [0.16.0-beta2] ‚Äî 2026-06-04

A **beta hotfix** on `0.16.0-beta1` ‚Äî fixes a launch-blocking isolation bug (#1513) surfaced during beta1 Linux validation, so a freshly-provisioned isolated agent with a `plugin:teams` channel starts correctly. Same `agb upgrade` path; no schema/contract change vs beta1.

### Fixed

- **Isolated `plugin:teams` agent aborts at launch on a 0700 legacy mirror (#1513, via #1514).** A sibling facet of #1506/#1508 on the tree that fix did not cover. `bridge-run.sh` runs the legacy Teams-MCP prune **as the iso user** against the legacy `$BRIDGE_AGENT_HOME_ROOT/<a>` mirror; on a freshly-created isolated agent that mirror dir was scaffolded `0700` (controller umask), so the iso user can't traverse it and `Path.is_file()` raises `EACCES` ‚Äî which Python 3.10's `is_file()` does **not** swallow ‚Äî crashing the prune with an uncaught traceback that `bridge-run.sh` hardened into `aborting launch: stale Teams MCP cleanup failed` (the webhook port never binds).
