- **CLI hot-path heredoc / here-string elimination ‚Äî Wave A of issue #815** (refs #815 Wave A). On a stale live macOS runtime the CLI hot path (`agb status`, `agb agent list --json`, `agb inbox <agent>`, `agb daemon start`) was hanging inside Bash `heredoc_write` while sourcing `lib/bridge-agent-update.sh` (two `$(cat <<'PY' ... PY)` source-time captures) and while iterating large tmux / queue / roster captures via `done <<<"$text"`. Wave A removes those four classes from the CLI startup path: (1) the two source-time Python captures in `lib/bridge-agent-update.sh::bridge_agent_update_apply_launch_cmd` / `‚Ä¶_apply_channels` move into `scripts/python-helpers/agent-update-apply-launch-cmd.py` + `agent-update-apply-channels.py` invoked via `python3 "$BRIDGE_SCRIPT_DIR/..."` (no source-time read remains); (2) the 8 `done <<<"$text"` sites in `lib/bridge-tmux.sh` (`bridge_tmux_session_has_prompt_from_text`, `bridge_tmux_session_has_pending_input_from_text`, `bridge_tmux_codex_post_paste_is_clean`, `bridge_tmux_codex_submit_landed`, `bridge_tmux_type_and_submit`, `bridge_tmux_pending_attention_flush`, `bridge_tmux_claude_last_prompt_is_ghost_text`, `bridge_tmux_codex_last_prompt_is_placeholder`) and the 2 sites in `lib/bridge-agents.sh` (`bridge_agent_channel_diagnostics_text`, `bridge_list_active_agents_numbered`) are routed through `mktemp + printf ‚Ä¶ > $tmp + done < $tmp` with a per-function `trap RETURN` cleanup, per the wave-orchestration Footgun 11 recipe; (3) the top-level `agent-bridge` dispatcher skips the eager pre-dispatch `bridge_load_roster` for `status` / `daemon` / `agent` / `task` / `inbox` ‚Äî each of those subcommands `exec`'s its own dispatch script which already loads the roster lazily on first use, so behavior is unchanged but startup wall time stays under 5s even when the roster is large and stale. Wave B (`bridge-daemon.sh` daemon-path heredoc-stdin sites), Wave C (`daemon_tick` freshness watchdog semantics), and Wave D (regression smoke fixture) stay pending ‚Äî issue #815 remains open until all four waves land.
- **`daemon_tick` freshness as a first-class health signal + auto-repair on silent-but-alive ‚Äî Wave C of issue #815** (refs #815 Wave C). Operator's live host had `state/daemon.heartbeat` stuck at `2026-05-13T07:30:05+09:00` for 18h while `agb daemon status` still reported a live pid ‚Äî symptom of the daemon being alive but silent. Wave A + Wave B fixed the *structural* wedge (Bash `heredoc_write` deadlocks in CLI / daemon paths); Wave C closes the *detection* gap so pid liveness is no longer the only health signal. Adds two helpers to `lib/bridge-state.sh`: `bridge_daemon_heartbeat_age_seconds` (reads `state/daemon.heartbeat`, parses both bare-epoch and ISO-string formats, returns age in seconds) and `bridge_daemon_health_signal` (emits `tick_age_seconds=` / `tick_fresh=` / `health=ok|silent|down` key=value lines derived from pid liveness √ó tick freshness with threshold `BRIDGE_DAEMON_TICK_FRESH_SECONDS` defaulting to 120s ‚Äî 2√ó the daemon's 60s tick cadence, so stale after 2 missed ticks). `agb daemon status` consumes the helper and adds an additive single-line `health: ...` summary alongside the existing grep-grammar fields (existing parsers keying off `running pid=` / `stopped` are unaffected). `agb daemon start` detects pid-alive-but-tick-stale and runs an auto-repair sequence (`kill -TERM` with 5s grace then `kill -KILL`, stop silence watchdog cleanly, remove stale pid + heartbeat, fall through to fresh start) instead of the prior "already running" no-op. The repair path is audit-logged (`daemon_start_repair_silent` + `daemon_start_repair_silent_killed`) and prints an explicit `[daemon] silent daemon detected (pid=N, tick stale by Ns) ‚Äî repair sequence: kill + restart` line to stderr. New daemon emits one initial `daemon_tick` + writes `state/daemon.heartbeat` immediately at the top of `cmd_run` before the first sync cycle, so a `daemon stop && daemon start && daemon status` triple shows `health: ok` within 1s instead of waiting up to 60s for the first periodic boundary. The existing silence-watchdog active-repair branch (`bridge-watchdog-silence.py::attempt_restart` with cooldown via `read_cooldown` / `write_cooldown`) already satisfies the watchdog half of the acceptance criterion ‚Äî no changes needed there. Wave D (destructive regression smoke fixture exercising stale dynamic envs + active patch / patch-dev + dynamic agents + large tmux captures) stays pending ‚Äî issue #815 remains open until Wave D lands.
- **Daemon hot-path heredoc / here-string elimination ‚Äî Wave B of issue #815** (refs #815 Wave B). Same deadlock class as Wave A, applied to `bridge-daemon.sh` so a fresh `agb daemon start` and every subsequent sync cycle never wedge in Bash `heredoc_write` while parsing analyzer captures, queue summaries, parsed-marker TSVs, or shell-formatted helper output. Wave B routes the 11 `while ‚Ä¶ done <<<"$text"` loops in `bridge-daemon.sh` (alert_rows + rotation_rows in `process_usage_monitor`, summary_output in `process_stall_reports` + `process_context_pressure_reports` + `process_on_demand_agents`, expired_rows in `process_permission_task_timeout_fanout`, parsed/pending_parsed in `_bridge_precompact_handle_started`/`_handle_completed`, ready_rows in `start_cron_dispatch_workers`, orphans_tsv in `process_memory_daily_orphan_sweep`, nudge_output in `cmd_sync_cycle`) through tempfiles via the Footgun 11 recipe (`mktemp + printf ‚Ä¶ > $tmp + done < $tmp` with `trap ‚Ä¶ RETURN` cleanup combined per scope ‚Äî Bash allows only one RETURN trap per function). Replaces the 8 `source /dev/stdin <<<"$shell_output"` sites in the same file (analysis_shell in stall/context-pressure analyzers, open_task_shell in `nudge_agent_session`, route/render/send shell in the two precompact handlers) with `printf > $tmp + source "$tmp"` plus `# shellcheck disable=SC1090`. Routes the 2 `bridge_with_timeout ‚Ä¶ python3 ‚Ä¶ <<<"$capture"` analyzer invocations (`bridge-stall.py analyze`, `bridge-context-pressure.py analyze`) through `< file` to keep the multi-KB tmux-capture text off the here-string path. Converts the daily-backup hot-path state writer (`bridge_daily_backup_compose_state`, was `cat <<EOF`) to grouped `printf '%s\n'` so the cooldown-pinning success/failure/warn paths stop emitting a heredoc on every backup tick. Subprocess-timeout audit: confirms the analyzer + release-monitor + dashboard-post + cron-sync hot paths already pass through `bridge_with_timeout`; wraps two previously unwrapped daemon hot-path subprocesses ‚Äî `bridge-sync.sh` (30s, called from `cmd_sync_cycle::bridge_sync` step) and `agent-bridge upgrade --check` (30s, called from `process_release_monitor` after a release alert resolves) ‚Äî under `bridge_with_timeout` so a stuck child cannot freeze the loop. No behavior change: every site already absorbed empty / failure output (`|| true`, empty-string guards), so a tempfile-routed empty read takes the same path. Wave C (`daemon_tick` freshness watchdog semantics) and Wave D (regression smoke fixture) stay pending ‚Äî issue #815 remains open until all four waves land.

## [0.11.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-14

### Highlight ‚Äî onboarding split (server vs dev), essential-default flip, wave-orchestration as admin default

Post-v0.10.0 follow-up wave (PR #809 / #811 / #812 / #813). Four PRs that together reshape the **operator experience after `bridge-init.sh`**: dev laptops and server hosts now diverge cleanly at the first init question, the two essentials operators kept forgetting to opt into (picker-sweep, prompt-guard) flip to default-enabled on hosted installs, and the admin agent's default workflow is now the parallel wave-orchestration pattern instead of sequential one-PR-at-a-time work. No CLI breakage, no `BRIDGE_HOME` migration step ‚Äî every existing `state/install/host-profile.json` value is honored, and explicit env overrides (`BRIDGE_*_ENABLED=0`) still win in both directions.

### Changed

- **`memory-daily-<agent>` cron added to the profile=dev gated set** (refs Issue #713 follow-up). Until now the host-profile helper deliberately preserved `memory-daily-*` on every host on the rationale that long-term context harvesting is universally useful. Operator decision 2026-05-13 reverses that for dev hosts: Claude Code's own session-memory system (`~/.claude/projects/<repo>/memory/`) already covers the long-term-context use case on a developer laptop, so running the daily harvester is duplicative there. `BRIDGE_HOST_PROFILE_PRODUCTION_CRON_PREFIXES=("memory-daily-")` was added alongside the existing exact-name list, and `bridge_host_profile_list_production_crons` now matches both. Re-enable any time with `agb cron update memory-daily-<agent> --enable`. Server profile unchanged ‚Äî memory-daily stays on hosted installs.
- **`wave-orchestration` skill is now the documented default workflow for admin agents** (refs PR #706). The bundled wave-orchestration skill already auto-distributes to every admin / dynamic agent's `.claude/skills/` via `bridge_bootstrap_claude_shared_skills`. The admin-pair-programming SOP managed block (`lib/bridge-admin-pair.sh:bridge_admin_pair_managed_block` + `bridge-upgrade.py:render_admin_pair_block`, byte-identical) now adds a "Default workflow ‚Äî `wave-orchestration`" section after the existing 6-step plan/review loop. When the operator asks for a feature / fix / multi-issue ship, the admin agent defaults to parallel `wave` (2-4 issue-fixer dispatches into isolated worktrees with codex-pair review via queue or `codex:codex-rescue` subagent fallback) instead of sequential one-PR-at-a-time work. Single-track work still uses the original pair-programming protocol unchanged. `bridge-upgrade.py inject-admin-pair-block` re-renders on the next `agent-bridge upgrade --apply`, so existing installs converge automatically.
- **`bridge-init.sh` host_profile question moved before channel bootstrap; `dev` answer now also short-circuits channel setup and emits the heavy-feature advisory in one block** (refs Issue #713 follow-up). Previously `bridge_host_profile_run` fired after `Discord/Telegram/Teams/Mattermost` setup, so a developer-laptop install was forced to walk through (or `--skip-channel-setup` past) the channel branches before being told it could skip them. The question now runs immediately after admin-agent + `<admin>-dev` codex-pair materialization and immediately before the channel setup blocks; on `host_profile=dev` the call site flips `skip_channel_setup=1` and appends an init warning when the operator-passed `--channels` is non-empty (so the explicit channel csv is acknowledged rather than silently dropped). `bridge_host_profile_emit_dev_advisories` (new helper in `lib/bridge-host-profile.sh`) prints a unified "this is what dev profile skips" block: external-channel bootstrap, multi-tenant v2 isolation migration (stays opt-in via `agent-bridge migrate isolation-v2`), librarian / wiki-* maintenance crons (already disabled by the existing offer), and the operator's `--admin <name>` + `<name>-dev` static-pair-only floor (codex r2 round threaded `admin_agent` through the helper so the advisory renders the real pair names rather than the literal `patch`/`patch-dev` defaults; no extra static roles get auto-created ‚Äî operators add them in `agent-roster.local.sh` when they upgrade to server). Server / CI / `--json` paths are unchanged: non-interactive still defaults to `server`, and `--reconfigure` still re-prompts. Idempotent against re-runs (the existing `[host-profile] already=‚Ä¶` sentinel still wins).
- **`BRIDGE_PICKER_SWEEP_ENABLED` and `BRIDGE_PROMPT_GUARD_ENABLED` defaults flipped to `1`; `host_profile=dev` opts both back out** (Track D follow-up to refs #713 / refs #809). Fresh hosted installs (`host_profile=server` or non-interactive default-server) now run picker-sweep and prompt-guard by default ‚Äî they were essential-class features previously gated behind unwritten opt-in env vars. Explicit env overrides are unchanged: operators who set `BRIDGE_PICKER_SWEEP_ENABLED=0` / `BRIDGE_PROMPT_GUARD_ENABLED=0` still win, and operators on a dev host who want either feature can set the env to `=1` to override. Dev hosts (`host_profile=dev`) remain quiet by default ‚Äî `bridge_host_profile_is_dev` (new helper in `lib/bridge-host-profile.sh`) gates the default-on at the runtime read site in `scripts/picker-sweep.sh`, `lib/bridge-guard.sh::bridge_prompt_guard_enabled_default`, and `bridge_guard_common.py::prompt_guard_enabled`. `bridge-init.sh` now auto-registers the `picker-sweep` bridge-native cron (`*/10 * * * *`, payload routed through the `<admin>-dev` codex pair per OPERATIONS.md ¬ßB to avoid the picker-via-Claude loop) on `host_profile=server` installs when no job titled `picker-sweep` is already present; idempotent on re-run. The new registration fires on fresh init / `agb init --reconfigure` only ‚Äî existing installs without a `state/install/host-profile.json` do NOT get retroactive cron registration; operators must re-run init or set the env override manually. `bridge_host_profile_emit_dev_advisories` lists the two skipped essentials so dev operators see why their install is quiet by default. New helper file `lib/bridge-init-default-crons.sh` houses the auto-register so additional server-default crons stay scoped to one place.

### Internal ‚Äî wave orchestration metadata

- 4 PRs landed in <6 hours of operator-clock-time, all squash-merged with `implement-ok` notes and codex-rescue pair-review (r1/r2/r3 chains as needed). Three of the four hit `needs-more` at r1 and converged at r2 (PR #809 admin_agent threading in advisory, PR #813 heredoc-stdin survival in registrar + outdated opt-in docs); PR #812 reached r3 on a docstring/code drift catch.
- `wave-orchestration` skill `references/footguns.md` gained a new entry ‚Äî **Footgun 11: Bash heredoc / here-string deadlock against a slow consumer** (PR #811). Codifies the #800 `python3 - <<'PY'` root cause + the 2026-05-13 `<<<"$var"` recurrence into a single brief-template recipe (`mktemp + < file`). Skill documentation only; no runtime change. Already paid off in this same cycle ‚Äî PR #813 r1 needs-more cited the new footgun entry verbatim when the registrar still piped its python script via heredoc-stdin.
- Issue #810 filed ‚Äî `credential helper: close the same-UID FS readability residual from #799 (Path A')`. Design seed for the residual `KNOWN_ISSUES.md ¬ß25` defense-in-depth limit (deliberate `Path.home()` enumeration in agent-controllable interpreter code). Brainstorm-pending; not an implementation issue.

## [0.10.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-13

### Highlight ‚Äî credential file delivery + daemon hang fixes + worktree doctor

This release cycle (PR #799/#801/#802 + #803/#804/#805/#806/#807) landed 8 PRs across credential isolation, daemon timeout hardening, and operational surface. Headline: PR #799 closed the env-injection credential leak class (Path A architecture, 5-round codex review chain ending at AST-audited exhaustive enumeration of remaining final-path operations). PR #801 + #806 structurally eliminated the `$(python3 - <<'PY')` heredoc-write deadlock class across the daemon main loop (#800 root cause + parallel-PR regression). PR #807 ships `agent-bridge worktree doctor` for safe stale-worktree cleanup with repo-level stash-safety guard.

### Added ‚Äî new user-facing surface

- **`agent-bridge auth claude-token` subcommand** (PR #799): per-agent Claude OAuth token registry with rotation, quota recovery, and file-based credential delivery. Token written to per-agent `~/.claude/.credentials.json` (NOT env-injected); rotation atomic via `write_private_file_atomic` with chown-before-replace + `_ensure_claude_dir_safe` parent symlink check. `fcntl.flock` `registry_lock` makes concurrent rotate/recover/check safe across daemon + manual CLI. New fcntl-locked `claude-oauth-tokens.json` registry under `$BRIDGE_RUNTIME_SECRETS_DIR`.
- **Watchdog canonical install** (PR #802): `scripts/install-watchdog-silence-launchagent.sh` (macOS, KeepAlive=true + RunAtLoad=true) + `scripts/install-watchdog-silence-systemd.sh` (Linux, Type=simple Restart=always). Both wired into `bridge-bootstrap.sh` alongside daemon-liveness install. `DAEMON_SCRIPT` default now resolves via env override ‚Üí `$BRIDGE_HOME/bridge-daemon.sh` ‚Üí `~/.agent-bridge/bridge-daemon.sh` ‚Üí `SCRIPT_DIR` (last-resort dev fallback with explicit warning). `state/silence-watchdog.pidlock` (fcntl) blocks concurrent run.
- **`agent-bridge watchdog cleanup-orphans`** (PR #802): reaps watchdog instances with ppid=1 + script path outside canonical allow-list. Validated against 10 orphan instances on operator host.
- **`agent-bridge worktree doctor [--dry-run|--apply|--max-age-days|--target-branch|--include-stale|--prune-branches|--repo]`** (PR #807): stale worktree cleanup with repo-level stash-safety guard. Default `--dry-run`. Stash present anywhere in repo blocks all removals (load-bearing ‚Äî `feedback_worktree_stash_shared_git_dir.md`).
- **`bridge-daemon-helpers.py`** (PR #801, expanded PR #806): 13 subcommands corresponding to the daemon's parsing/lookup callsites. Replaces `$(python3 - <<'PY')` heredoc-stdin pattern that was the root cause of the 34h silent daemon hang in #800.

### Fixed ‚Äî daemon hang / heredoc deadlock class

- **34-hour silent daemon hang root cause** (PR #801, refs #800 Track A, refs #265 Proposal A): 9 unwrapped `$(python3 - <<'PY')` callsites in `bridge-daemon.sh` moved out of heredoc-stdin into `bridge-daemon-helpers.py` subcommands. Each wrapped in `bridge_with_timeout <secs> <label>` with per-site budget (5s JSON-only / 15s nudge_live_state hot path / 60s daily-backup). Highest-impact site (`nudge_live_state`) has explicit per-agent `skip_this_tick` audit-only fallback ‚Äî sibling agents continue. Smoke fixture (`scripts/smoke/daemon-heredoc-timeout.sh`) exercises real subcommand bodies via FIFO + sqlite EXCLUSIVE lock stall vectors (the actual #800 hang class).
- **PR #799 introduced 4 NEW heredoc regression sites** (PR #806): PR #799's cron auth + token rotation/recovery paths were developed in parallel with PR #801 and missed the #800 wrapping convention. PR #806 wrapped the four new sites (`usage_rotation_candidates_parse`, `rotation_status_parse`, `recovery_status_parse`, `sync_status_parse`) using same Pattern A helper subcommand. Plus 2 library sites in `lib/bridge-core.sh` (`core_match`) and `lib/bridge-skills.sh` (`skills_resolve_target`) wrapped with Pattern B (`python3 -c "$SCRIPT"` here-string variable). Registry now 13 entries.

### Fixed ‚Äî Claude OAuth credential isolation (Path A)

- **`CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` env-injection leak class** (PR #799 r1-r5 chain, refs `feedback_credential_env_injection_anti_pattern.md`): credential delivery moved from `launch-secrets.env` env injection to per-agent `~/.claude/.credentials.json` file. Path B (hook-deny defense-in-depth) ESCALATE recommendation from codex r3 led to architecture pivot. Now: tool execution UID can still read its own credential file (Finding 1 same-UID FS readability residual ‚Äî documented in `KNOWN_ISSUES.md ¬ß25` with credential-helper pattern noted as planned future enhancement), but no interpreter `os.environ` / `%ENV` / `ENV[]` leak. Hook deny rules from r2-r3 retained as belt-and-suspenders.
- **Symlink-attack hardening** (PR #799 r2): bash `bridge_auth_claude_credentials_file_for_agent` + Python `_ensure_claude_dir_safe` reject symlinked `.claude/` dirs. `cd -P` real-path verification requires resolved path to stay under isolated user home. macOS `/var` ‚Üî `/private/var` prefix mismatch handled.
- **Rotation atomicity** (PR #799 r2): `write_private_file_atomic` chowns tempfile to isolated UID BEFORE `os.replace` (NOT after ‚Äî that was the r1 race). Removed post-replace chmod (r5 redundancy elimination ‚Äî `rename(2)` preserves mode bits via inode rename).
- **`bridge-cron-runner.py` 401 on claude -p** (PR #799): runner now resolves per-agent `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` from `launch-secrets.env`. Previously fell back to controller default `~/.claude` and hit 401 even while interactive sessions worked.

### Fixed ‚Äî operational hardening

- **`bridge_with_timeout` self-defeat on bare macOS** (PR #804, refs PR #802 r2 caveat): wrapper relied on `timeout(1)`/`gtimeout(1)`; bare macOS without GNU coreutils ran wrapped command unwrapped. Added tier-2 Python `subprocess.run(timeout=)` fallback using `python3 -c "$SCRIPT"` here-string pattern (NOT heredoc-stdin). 3-tier chain: timeout(1) ‚Üí python3 ‚Üí unwrapped exec (last resort). Tier-3 audit wrapped in subshell `(...) || true` to contain `bridge_require_python` exit when python3 is also missing.
- **Channel runtime readiness false-negative** (PR #803, refs #779): `bridge_agent_channel_runtime_ready_for_item` now does LISTEN port probe after file/env-key checks. `bridge_port_is_listening` helper (Linux `ss` + macOS `lsof` portable, fail-open on minimal hosts). Teams branch only ‚Äî discord/telegram outbound, ms365 shares teams listener. Legacy roster (no `TEAMS_WEBHOOK_PORT`) preserved via empty-port short-circuit.
- **`agent-bridge show --json alive=null`** when tmux session absent (PR #805, refs #780): multi-signal OR ‚Äî tmux session OR `agents/<X>/runtime/agent.pid` alive OR channel LISTEN. Existing `active` field preserved unchanged for backwards compat; new `alive` + `alive_signals` fields surface in `--json`.
- **CI smoke v0.8.0 isolation-v2 marker noise** (PR #799 Wave 1, indirect): `scripts/smoke/lib.sh:smoke_setup_bridge_home` writes `$BRIDGE_STATE_DIR/layout-marker.sh` with `BRIDGE_LAYOUT=v2 + BRIDGE_DATA_ROOT`. Resolved the markerless(existing-install) abort that was masking real regressions across PR #799 r1-era CI runs.

### Process ‚Äî codex pair-review chains this cycle

- **PR #799 ‚Äî 5-round Path A cycle** (after 3-round Path B abandonment): r1 same-UID accepted residual + symlink + chown race ‚Üí r2 symlink/atomic fix ‚Üí r3 drop bash legacy helper ‚Üí r4 drop Python unchanged-content fast paths ‚Üí r5 drop redundant post-replace chmod. r5 codex AST-walker independent audit matched fixer's enumeration exactly (6 final-path chmod/chown calls all in allow-listed context: 3 in save_registry controller-owned, 1 in probe_claude_token sandboxed tempdir, 2 in write_private_file_atomic tempfile pre-replace). Convergent-pattern technical-override of 4-round cap policy ‚Äî documented in `project_pr799_path_a_shipped.md`.
- **PR #801 ‚Äî r1 smoke-fixture-realism finding**: codex r1 audit confirmed production code correctness (path resolution, no-stdin contract, arg order, per-agent nudge skip) but flagged smoke harness used synthetic helpers. r2 extended smoke with real subcommand stall vectors (FIFO no-writer for `mcp_orphan_cleanup_parse` blocking `Path.read_text()`, sqlite `BEGIN EXCLUSIVE` for `nudge_live_state` blocking `sqlite3.connect.execute`). Smoke now legitimately catches the production hang class.
- **PR #802 ‚Äî r2 SCRIPT_DIR silent-fallback BLOCKING**: codex r1 caught that fallback chain's SCRIPT_DIR last-resort returned without warning ‚Äî exactly the failure mode #800 Track C was fixing. r2 added explicit warning before SCRIPT_DIR fallback return. Documented caveat about `bridge_with_timeout` PATH dependency carried over to PR #804.
- **PR #806 catches regression class**: PR #799 + PR #801 developed in parallel against same base; PR #799 added 4 NEW heredoc-stdin sites that PR #801's audit had no visibility into. PR #806 was the audit follow-up. Pattern worth flagging: **parallel PR development on same surface needs cross-PR audit before merge or immediate follow-up after merge**.

### Internal ‚Äî wave orchestration metadata

- 8 PRs landed across this release cycle in <5 hours of operator-clock-time, all squash-merged with structured `implement-ok` notes and codex-rescue pair-review.
- New fixer footgun discovered: **Edit/Write tool CWD pollution**. Track F fixer's first round of edits landed in operator's primary checkout instead of own fixer worktree (Edit tool with parent-process CWD on operator path). Worktree-relative absolute paths now mandatory in fixer briefs.
- New runtime footgun discovered: **Bash 5.3.9 here-string heredoc-write deadlock**. `<<<"$porcelain"` into multi-record read loop deadlocks in `heredoc_write` ‚Äî same #800 class. Workaround: `mktemp + < file`.

### Carry-over to next cycle

- Finding 1 (same-UID FS readability) ‚Äî `KNOWN_ISSUES.md ¬ß25` notes credential-helper / separate Claude launcher identity as planned future enhancement.
- One-shot script heredoc sites (`bridge-intake.sh`, `bridge-bootstrap.sh`, `bridge-bundle.sh`, `scripts/wiki-daily-ingest.sh`, `scripts/repair-task-db.sh`, `scripts/export-public-snapshot.sh`) still use heredoc-stdin pattern. Lower priority than daemon main-loop sites; tracked for future audit pass.
- Worktree doctor stash-safety policy refuses all removal when ANY stash present ‚Äî operator's current checkout has 5 in-flight stashes blocking cleanup of 156 stale fixer worktrees. Resolve stashes (or use git stash drop for known-stale ones) to enable cleanup.

## [0.9.9] ‚Äî 2026-05-11

### Highlight ‚Äî every-upgrade isolated-UID breakage fixed (#792 CRITICAL hotfix)

`scripts/deploy-live-install.sh` was using `cp -p` which preserved source-tree perms (0700 for shell scripts, 0600 for libs/hooks). Every `agent-bridge upgrade --apply` broke isolated-UID agents on next restart with a cascading 6-symptom failure list (bridge-run.sh EACCES, lib/hook unreadable, marketplace.json PermissionError, plugins/ms365/bun.lock install-failed, .credentials.json ACL mask cancellation, .env mask cancellation). Operators were applying a 6-point manual chmod+setfacl recovery after every upgrade. Fixed in PR #796 via per-file-class mode contract + post-deploy critical-perm verification.

### Fixed

- **`scripts/deploy-live-install.sh` strips perms on every upgrade** (PR #796, refs #792): replaced bare `cp -p` with `cp -p` + per-file-class chmod (`deploy_live_install_set_mode` helper). Added post-deploy `deploy_live_install_verify_critical_perms` sanity sweep with cross-platform stat (GNU `%a` + BSD `%Lp`). Non-fatal warning preserves operator recovery path. Per-class matrix: bridge-*.sh/agent-bridge/agb/hooks 0755, lib/marketplace.json/plugins-*.json 0644. ACL mask restore for `~/.claude/.credentials.json` and per-agent `.env` is documented out-of-scope (those live outside `$TARGET_ROOT`, handled in `lib/bridge-isolation-v2.sh` grant matrix).
- **`bridge-upgrade.py:create_daily_backup_archive` aborts whole archive on isolated-UID EACCES** (PR #794, refs #785): per-member catch on `os.lstat` and `archive.add` for both `PermissionError` and raw `OSError(errno=EACCES)`. `skipped_isolated` list (bounded 50 + truncation marker + last 10 when len > 60) propagates to `result["skipped_isolated_count"]` AND `cmd_daily_backup_live` CLI JSON payload. Snapshot loop got the same defensive guard. Pattern mirrors existing `agent_migration` precedent.
- **Orphan `memory-daily-<agent>` cron jobs never garbage-collected** (PR #797, refs #791): new `process_memory_daily_orphan_sweep` helper in `bridge-daemon.sh`. Each sync pass enumerates `family=memory-daily` cron jobs, cross-checks source agent against `BRIDGE_AGENT_IDS`, emits ONE `[health]` task per UTC day per orphan group with body listing `agent-bridge cron delete <job-id>` suggestions. Marker file at `$BRIDGE_STATE_DIR/memory-daily-orphan-sweep/<UTC-day>.surfaced` suppresses re-emit. Idempotent ‚Äî does NOT auto-disable jobs (operator's explicit Option A preference). Mock unit test at `scripts/test-memory-daily-orphan-sweep.sh` (282 LOC, 16 assertions √ó 4 scenarios).
- **MS365 plugin state-dir not canonicalized on launch** (PR #790, refs #786): `bridge_claude_launch_with_channel_state_dirs` (`lib/bridge-state.sh`) now canonicalizes `MS365_STATE_DIR` alongside existing Discord/Telegram/Teams. Frozen-roster `launch_cmd` with stale pre-v2 `MS365_STATE_DIR=‚Ä¶/agents/<X>/.ms365` (under 2750 SetGID parent) gets rewritten to canonical `agents/<X>/workdir/.ms365` (under 2770 ab-agent-X group). Plus 3-round codex catch: duplicate-vector dedupe (multiple stale STATE_DIR spans collapse to one canonical) + surgical separator strip at drop site (preserves quoted multi-space content in non-target env values like `OTHER="a  b"`). Symmetric across all 4 channel state VARs.
- **Controller watcher times out before Claude foreground** (PR #793): split `bridge_start_schedule_dev_channels_accept` budget into foreground gate (`BRIDGE_START_DEV_CHANNELS_CLAUDE_FOREGROUND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`, default 600s ‚Äî waits for tmux pane foreground to become `claude`) and existing 60s accept window. On cold-plugin-cache restarts where `bridge-run.sh` runs `bridge_run_sync_dev_plugin_cache` for 3-5min before `exec claude`, the prior single 60s budget burned down before Claude even started. Plus in-loop `bridge_tmux_session_exists` check inside `bridge_tmux_wait_for_claude_foreground` so a hard plugin-cache failure that exits the tmux session mid-wait returns rc=1 promptly instead of polling the full budget. New runtime smoke `tests/tmux-wait-foreground-liveness/smoke.sh` covers the session-liveness path.

### Added

- **`scripts/picker-sweep.sh` ‚Äî opt-in auto-unstick utility for stuck Claude Code pickers** (PR #664, SYRS-AI fork contribution): scans every tmux session, detects rate-limit / resume-from-summary pickers via closed-pattern allow-list, sends Enter on default option. Default disabled (`BRIDGE_PICKER_SWEEP_ENABLED=0`); operators opt in via OS crontab or bridge-native cron. Strict line-anchored regex requires both `_PICKER_OPTION_LINE_RE` AND (optionally for log diagnostic) `_PICKER_TAIL_RE` to match ‚Äî defends against false-positives from PR bodies / docs / logs that quote picker text. Test seams allow `scripts/smoke/picker-sweep.sh` to exercise with mock tmux + mock queue. See `OPERATIONS.md` "picker-sweep utility" for registration paths.

### Hygiene

- **SC2088 false-positive in `bridge_expand_user_path`** (PR #795 Track D): replaced `'~/'*) printf '%s%s' "$HOME" "${raw#\~}"` with `\~/*) printf '%s%s' "$HOME" "${raw:1}"` ‚Äî byte-equivalent across 8 inputs, shellcheck-clean. The case pattern `'~/'*` (tilde inside quotes) was triggering SC2088 since v0.9.6 push, blocking all gated CI checks (integration smoke, legacy-full-smoke, live/tmux/daemon smoke, unit/static smoke) for 4 consecutive release pushes. v0.9.9 is the first release with green lint since v0.9.5.

### Process ‚Äî codex-rescue caught 3 BLOCKING vectors this cycle

- **PR #790 r1**: duplicate STATE_DIR spans ‚Üí 2 canonical entries. Inherited from a latent main-branch bug; PR #790 just exposed it for MS365. r2 dedupe.
- **PR #790 r2**: global `re.sub(r" {2,}", " ", env_prefix)` cleanup mangled quoted multi-space env values. r3 surgical drop-site strip.
- **PR #797 r1**: call-site wiring `|| true` silent-swallow instead of `|| daemon_warn`. r2 wiring fix + tracked unit test.

All 3 vectors were quote/substitution semantic issues that fresh-install probes would have missed. Pattern matches the v0.9.5-v0.9.8 "operator-host artifact reproducer required" lesson ‚Äî now baked into review brief template.

### Internal

- Wave orchestration pattern (per `~/.claude/skills/wave-orchestration/`): 4 fixers + 3 codex reviews dispatched in parallel; 5 squash-merges + 2 codex iteration cycles + 1 fork-side merge conflict resolution + 1 cross-account auth dance (SYRS-AI fork PR), all without operator interaction.

### Fixed (#787 ‚Äî RC6 ms365 leak, refs #786 Finding 1)

- `bridge-dev-plugin-cache.py:overlay_source_to_cache` + `_overlay_dir` ‚Äî symlink-to-outside-marketplace detect + skip + WARN. operator host Ïùò v0.7‚Üív0.8 isolation migration leftover (`source/ms365/node_modules ‚Üí /home/ec2-user/.claude/plugins/cache/...`) Í∞Ä isolated UID Í∂åÌïúÏúºÎ°ú read ÏãúÎèÑ ‚Üí `PermissionError: [Errno 13]` ‚Üí ms365 sync `install-failed`. v0.9.7 Ïù¥ Îã§Î•∏ 3 plugin (teams, cosmax-crm, cosmax-ep-approval) Îäî ÏûëÎèôÌñàÏßÄÎßå ms365 Îßå silent fail.
- 2-round codex destructive-probe: r1 catch ‚Äî `source_root = source_path.resolve()` boundary Í∞Ä ÎÑàÎ¨¥ narrow (plugin-specific path), intra-marketplace `node_modules ‚Üí ../dist` Î•º outside-source Î°ú ÏûòÎ™ª Î∂ÑÎ•ò. r2 fix ‚Äî caller chain ÏúºÎ°ú marketplace root ÏÝÑÎã¨, marketplace boundary ÏÇ¨Ïö©.

### Fixed (#788 ‚Äî memory-daily aggregate matrix vs harvester mismatch, refs #786 Finding 2)

- `scripts/memory-daily-harvest.sh` ‚Äî isolated UID Ïãú `--shared-aggregate-dir` Ïïà ÎÑòÍπÄ (per-agent fragment Îßå Ïì∞ÎèÑÎ°ù). Ïù¥ÏÝÑÏóî isolated UID Í∞Ä controller-only `aggregate/` Ïóê ÏßÅÏÝë write ÏãúÎèÑ ‚Üí EACCES.
- `scripts/memory-daily-reduce.sh` (NEW) ‚Äî controller-side reducer. agents/<X>/runtime/memory-daily/*.json fragment ‚Üí admin-aggregate-<agent>-<date>.json. operator Í∞Ä controller crontab Ïóê wire (v0.9.9 ÏóêÏÑú ÏûêÎèô Îì±Î°ù Í≤ÄÌÜÝ).
- `lib/bridge-isolation-v2.sh:bridge_isolation_v2_memory_daily_shared_aggregate_dir` ‚Äî self-contradicting comment fix. Îß§Ìä∏Î¶≠Ïä§ row Í∞Ä design A (r-x for isolated UIDs) binding Ïù¥ÎùºÍ≥Ý Î™ÖÌôï.
- 4-round codex destructive-probe (security-heavy):
  - r1: cp -p preserved fragment mode (0660) vs aggregate contract (0640) ‚Üí drop -p + explicit chmod 0640
  - r2 BLOCKING: symlink fragment Í∞Ä controller secret ÏúºÎ°úÏùò path-traversal exfiltration vector ‚Üí 3 defense layer (reject symlink agent_dir, reject symlink fragment, cp -P TOCTOU defense)
  - r3 BLOCKING: TOCTOU race ÌõÑ Layer 2 [[ -L ]] check ÏÇ¨Ïù¥ swap Í∞ÄÎä• ‚Üí post-cp [[ -f && ! -L ]] re-check + JSON parse gate
  - r4 implement-ok: 8/8 probes PASS (TOCTOU swap blocked, malformed JSON rejected, valid JSON published, mode 0640, group ab-shared Î≥¥Ï°¥)

### Verified (operator-host artifact + canonical state)

- operator's exact ms365 leftover artifact (`source/ms365/node_modules ‚Üí /controller/cache/...`) ‚Üí cache materializes without node_modules + WARN
- intra-marketplace symlink (e.g. `node_modules ‚Üí ../dist`) ‚Üí cache recurses + materializes
- agent-controlled symlink fragment exfiltration vector ‚Üí blocked at Layer 2
- TOCTOU race attack on symlink check ‚Üí blocked at Layer 4 (post-cp)
- malformed JSON fragment ‚Üí rejected at Layer 5 (JSON parse gate)
- aggregate file mode 0640 + ab-shared group inheritance from setgid parent
- chmod 2770 Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê workaround state coexists with new harvester (no regression)

### Known carry-over (v0.9.9 Ìä∏Îûô ÌõÑÎ≥¥)

- `installed_plugins.json` rewrite ordering (#786 suggested fix item 3 ‚Äî Ïù¥Î≤à PR Îäî cache materialization Îßå fix; pre-v2 installPath Í∞Ä cache fail Ïãú Ïïà Í≥ÝÏ≥êÏßÄÎäî Í≤ÉÏùÄ Î≥ÑÎèÑ)
- v1-isolation cleanup helper ‚Äî `agent-bridge upgrade --apply` Í∞Ä `source/<plugin>/node_modules ‚Üí /controller/...` symlink ÏùÑ ÏûêÎèô detect + remove (currently operator manual `rm`)
- memory-daily reducer Ïùò cron ÏûêÎèô Îì±Î°ù (`bridge-cron.sh` Ïóê controller-side cron job)
- design v2 ¬ß"Per-Agent Cache Tradeoffs" content-addressed package store proposal

## [0.9.7] ‚Äî 2026-05-10

### Highlight ‚Äî v0.9.6 verification surfaced 6 RCs ‚Üí unified isolation grant matrix

Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûêÏùò v0.9.6 Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÌõÑ ÌõÑÏÜçÏúºÎ°ú RC1-RC6 (#781) Î∞úÍ≤¨. Î™®Îëê Í∞ôÏùÄ architectural gap ‚Äî isolated agent's required-access matrix Í∞Ä single contract Í∞Ä ÏïÑÎãàÏñ¥ÏÑú path ÎßàÎã§ ad-hoc grant Í∞Ä Îã§Î•∏ mechanism (group, named ACL, mode) ÏúºÎ°ú Ìù©Ïñ¥ÏÝ∏ ÏûàÏóàÏùå. v0.9.7 ÏùÄ Ïù¥Î•º single isolation-grant-matrix Î°ú ÌÜµÌï©:

| # | Symptom | Mechanism (v0.9.7) |
|---|---|---|
| RC1 | `state/agents/<X>/` group `ab-controller` (v2 contract violation) | matrix row enforces `ab-agent-<X>` + setgid 2770 |
| RC2 | `idle-since` controller-owned 0600, isolated hook unlink fail | new `bridge_isolation_v2_write_agent_state_marker` route, group-aware writer |
| RC3 | `~/.claude/.credentials.json` ACL `mask::---` wipe ‚Üí `/login` infinite loop | single named-user ACL exception (Anthropic credential), path guard prevents v0.9.5/v0.9.6 mask-break recurrence, multi-agent roster-aware grant + strip |
| RC4 | `runtime/` not traversable | matrix row `agent-runtime` covers |
| RC5 | `logs/` not traversable | matrix row `agent-logs` covers |
| RC6 | `dev-plugin-cache` "linked OK" Í±∞Ïßì success log + ms365 cache dir Î∂ÄÏû¨ | `bridge-dev-plugin-cache.py` pre-link assert + post-link verify under isolated UID + per-agent isolated cache (real directory copy, NOT symlink to shared source) + criticality split (channel-required block / optional warn-and-continue) |

Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê binding principle (per design v2): Í∞Å isolated agent Í∞Ä **ÏûêÍ∏∞ plugin ÏûêÍ∏∞ install ÏûêÍ∏∞ login** ‚Äî cross-agent Í∞ÑÏÑ≠ Î∞©ÏßÄ. shared cache ÏûêÏ≤¥Í∞Ä Í∞ÑÏÑ≠ Îß§Í∞úÎùº per-agent isolated cache ÌïÑÏàò. disk overhead 100-300MB / agent Í∞êÏàò (4 agents = 400MB-2GB).

### Process ‚Äî codex destructive-probe 22 round (operator-authorized round-cap waiver)

Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê ÏûêÏú® ÏúÑÏûÑ: "Ï∫° Í∞ôÏùÄÍ±∞ ÌïÑÏöî ÏóÜÏñ¥ ÎêÝÎñÑÍπåÏßÄ ÏôÑÏÝÑÌïòÍ≤å ÏôÑÎ£åÌï¥" + "ÎÇ¥Í∞Ä ÏûêÍ≥Ý ÏùºÏñ¥ÎÇòÎ©¥ Îã§ ÎêòÏñ¥ ÏûàÏñ¥ÏïºÌï¥". CLAUDE.md Ïùò 3-round cap Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅ waiver. PR #782 16 round + PR #783 6 round = Ï¥ù 22 round. Í∞Å round Í∞Ä ÏÉà vector catch:

**PR #782 (matrix foundation + RC1-RC5, 16 round):**
- r1-r6: RC3 helper deep-dive (mode ‚Üí owner+group ‚Üí mask ‚Üí named-user ‚Üí ancestor ‚Üí other ‚Üí file_mode ‚Üí apply/check sym ‚Üí Linux stat group_class quirk)
- r7: 1 BLOCK + 2 WARN (strict r--, stale strip, effective annotation parse)
- r8-r10: helper hard-fail + reapply wrapper exit propagation + 2 other matrix-apply callers `|| true` swallow
- r11: RC3 multi-agent + write_marker + hook stderr
- r12-r13: load reapply.sh + (g) all-roster check + drop daemon writer fallbacks (5 writers total) + r12 path bug
- r14-r15: webhook ensure + chmod 0660 + daemon audit visibility + synthetic marker rc + scrub guard order + legacy `--help`
- r16: next-session.sha path divergence (Python hook write vs bash reader)

**PR #783 (RC6 + per-agent cache + criticality, 6 round):**
- r1: symlink-to-source = cross-agent collision (operator binding principle violation)
- r2: real directory copy + 4-branch unification (3 BUG)
- r3: self-contained per-agent (node_modules Ìè¨Ìï®) + chmod 0700 + derive node_modules_status from cache
- r4: symlink-to-dir handling (is_dir() first) + parent chmod 0700
- r5-r6: channel-required wins both-lists + _is_required_channel decision tree fix

Í∞Å round Ïùò fix Í∞Ä v0.9.5/v0.9.6 false-positive Í∞Ä Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê host ÏóêÏÑú Ïû¨Î∞úÌïÝ Ïàò ÏûàÎäî vector. Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê ÏûêÏú®ÏúÑÏûÑ ROI: 22 round Îäî release ÌõÑ hotfix cycle Î≥¥Îã§ Ìõ®Ïî¨ Ïã∏Îã§.

### Fixed (#782 + #783 ‚Äî refs #781)

- **`lib/bridge-isolation-v2.sh`** ‚Äî new `bridge_isolation_v2_matrix_rows_for_agent` enumerator (18 rows from design v2 + 4 plugin rows = 22 total), `bridge_isolation_v2_apply_grant_matrix_for_agent` (apply/check), `bridge_isolation_v2_apply_row` per-row dispatcher, `bridge_isolation_v2_ensure_matrix_path` for daemon writers, `bridge_isolation_v2_write_agent_state_marker` atomic write, `bridge_isolation_v2_apply_controller_credentials_read_grant` + `bridge_isolation_v2_check_controller_credentials_read_grant` (RC3 single exception, multi-agent roster-aware, all 5 ACL conditions enforced + (f)(g) roster checks). Path guard in `bridge_isolation_v2_acl_scrub` (RC3 recurrence prevention) + scrub file-path refusal.
- **`lib/bridge-isolation-v2-migrate.sh`** ‚Äî `bridge_isolation_v2_migrate_normalize_layout` propagates matrix-apply failure (was `|| true`).
- **`lib/bridge-isolation-v2-reapply.sh`** ‚Äî `bridge_isolation_v2_reapply_one_agent` consumes matrix rows; dispatch wrapper accumulates per-agent errors ‚Üí non-zero exit. action label `error:matrix_apply_failed` (was `warn:matrix_partial`).
- **`lib/bridge-agents.sh`** ‚Äî `bridge_linux_prepare_agent_isolation` propagates matrix apply failure.
- **`lib/bridge-state.sh`** ‚Äî daemon writers (`mark_idle_now`, `mark_manual_stop`, `note_prompt_ready`) route through matrix-aware writer + drop direct-write fallback. `bridge_agent_next_session_marker_file` aligned with Python hook path (state/agents/<X>/, was runtime/).
- **`lib/bridge-channels.sh`** ‚Äî webhook-port + missing-marker retry writers + ensure_matrix_path now hard-fail. synthetic marker call propagates rc.
- **`lib/bridge-notify.sh`** ‚Äî `bridge_claude_session_try_mark_prompt_ready` propagates mark_idle_now rc.
- **`bridge-daemon.sh`** ‚Äî nudge_scan step emits `daemon_step_warning` audit on idle-ready writer failure.
- **`bridge-dev-plugin-cache.py`** ‚Äî RC6 pre-link assertion + post-link verify under isolated UID, per-agent real directory cache (no shared symlink), node_modules included in copy, chmod 0700 on every cache + parent dir, criticality split (channel-required block / optional warn).
- **`bridge-run.sh`** ‚Äî Python exit code propagation for criticality split.
- **`agent-bridge`** ‚Äî new `isolation verify --agent <X> [--json] [--strict-optional]` subcommand. Pre-source bypass for `isolation verify --help` (operator can read syntax on legacy/markerless installs).
- **`hooks/bridge_hook_common.py`** ‚Äî next-session.sha writer emits stderr WARN on OSError (no longer silent return None) + uses bridge_active_agent_dir() (matches bash reader after r16).
- **`bridge-lib.sh`** ‚Äî sources `bridge-isolation-v2-reapply.sh` so `reapply_eligible_agents` is available to all v2 helpers (not just bridge-migrate.sh).

### Verified (codex destructive-probe + operator-state reproducer)

- Î™®ÎìÝ 6 RC Í∞Ä operator's RC1-5 workaround state ÏóêÏÑú single `migrate isolation v2 --apply` Ìïú Î≤àÏúºÎ°ú canonical state Î°ú converge.
- `agent-bridge isolation verify --agent <X>` Í∞Ä Î™®ÎìÝ 22 row Ïóê ÎåÄÌï¥ pass/mismatch/not-applicable ÏÝïÌôïÌûà Î∂ÑÎ•ò.
- multi-agent (4 agent) ÏãúÎÇòÎ¶¨Ïò§: Í∞Å agent Í∞Ä own plugin ÏûêÍ∏∞ install + own login + own credential, cross-agent interference 0.
- per-agent cache disk overhead measured ~200-400MB/agent for typical bridge plugin set.

### Process lessons (carry forward to v0.9.8+)

1. **release-PR codex review brief Ïóê operator's current production state reproducer ÏùòÎ¨¥Ìôî** (v0.9.6 lesson ÏÝÅÏö©). orbstack Í≤ÄÏ¶ùÏù¥ fresh-setup Îßå Îã§Î£®Î©¥ frozen-roster Í∞ôÏùÄ actual state ÎÜìÏπ®.
2. **Í∞Å round Ïùò catch Í∞Ä ÏÉà vector Î©¥ 5+ round ÏÝïÎãπÌôî**. ÎèôÏùº vector Î∞òÎ≥µÏù¥Î©¥ cap ÏÝÅÏö©. PR #782 16 round, PR #783 6 round Î™®Îëê each catch new vector ÏòÄÏùå.
3. **apply/check symmetry Í∞Ä architectural concern**. Ìïú path ÏóêÏÑú hard-fail propagation Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌïòÎ©¥ Î™®ÎìÝ caller chain ÏóêÏÑú swallow Ïïà ÎêòÎèÑÎ°ù sweep ÌïÑÏöî. PR #782 r9-r15 Í∞Ä ÏÝïÌôïÌûà Ïù¥ sweep.

### Known carry-over (v0.9.8 Ìä∏Îûô ÌõÑÎ≥¥)

- `bridge-upgrade.sh` Ïùò path guard / convergence ÏÝïÎ∞Ä Í≤ÄÏ¶ù (PR 3 deferred ‚Äî operator host Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÌõÑ hotfix Í∞ÄÎä•)
- `scripts/oss-preflight.sh` orbstack reproducer with operator-actual production state
- `docs/agent-runtime/v2-isolation-migration.md` 22-row matrix Î¨∏ÏÑúÌôî
- `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` v0.9.5/v0.9.6 false-positive Ìå®ÌÑ¥ lessons
- design v2 ¬ß"Per-Agent Cache Tradeoffs" Ïùò future content-addressed package store proposal (cross-agent dedup without shared mutable state)

## [0.9.6] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

### Highlight ‚Äî REAL #771 fix (v0.9.5 was a false-positive on frozen-roster)

`v0.9.5` Ïùò `bridge_write_linux_agent_env_file` regen call ÏùÄ Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûêÏùò R5 ÏßÑÎã®ÏúºÎ°ú **false-positive** ÌôïÏù∏. Linux ÏÑúÎ≤ÑÏóêÏÑú `migrate isolation v2 --apply` ÌõÑ `cat agents/<X>/runtime/agent-env.sh | grep TEAMS_STATE_DIR` Í∞Ä Ïó¨ÏÝÑÌûà stale `‚Ä¶/agents/<X>/.teams` (pre-v2 path) Î∞òÌôò. log Îäî `agent_env_regen ok` ÎùºÍ≥Ý ÌñàÏßÄÎßå Ïã§ÏÝú ÌååÏùº ÎÇ¥Ïö©ÏùÄ Ïïà Î∞îÎÄú.

ÏßÑÎã® Í≤∞Í≥º root cause Îäî `lib/bridge-state.sh` Ïùò `bridge_claude_launch_with_channel_state_dirs` helper. Ïù¥ Ìï®ÏàòÎäî launch ÏãúÏÝêÏóê cached `BRIDGE_AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD[$agent]` Ïùò env-prefix Ïóê canonical `TEAMS_STATE_DIR` / `MS365_STATE_DIR` / `DISCORD_STATE_DIR` / `TELEGRAM_STATE_DIR` Î•º Ï£ºÏûÖÌïòÎäî Í≤ÉÏù¥ Ï±ÖÏûÑÏù¥ÏßÄÎßå, Í∏∞Ï°¥ loop Í∞Ä:

```python
for name, value in assignments:
    if f"{name}=" in env_prefix:
        continue       # ‚Üê BUG: name Îßå Î≥¥Í≥Ý skip ‚Üí stale value Î≥¥Ï°¥
    env_prefix += f"{name}={shlex.quote(value)} "
```

Ï≤òÎüº `<NAME>=` substring Îßå Í≤ÄÏÇ¨ÌïòÍ≥Ý SKIP. fresh-setup agent (env_prefix ÎπÑÏñ¥ ÏûàÏùå) Îäî append branch Í∞Ä fire Ìï¥ÏÑú ÏÝïÏÉÅÏù¥ÏßÄÎßå, frozen-roster (v0.7‚Üív0.8 migrate ÏãúÏÝêÏóê cached Îêú stale `TEAMS_STATE_DIR=‚Ä¶/agents/<X>/.teams`) Îäî `name` Ïù¥ Ïù¥ÎØ∏ Ï°¥Ïû¨Ìï¥ÏÑú skip ‚Üí Îß§ restart ÎßàÎã§ stale path Í∞Ä Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú ÌÜµÍ≥º ‚Üí bun teams server.ts silent-exit before bind. v0.9.5 Ïùò regen ÏùÄ cached file Î•º ÏÉàÎ°ú Ïì∞ÏßÄÎßå **ÏÉàÎ°ú Ïì¥ ÎÇ¥Ïö© ÏûêÏ≤¥Í∞Ä Í∞ôÏùÄ helper Î•º ÌÜµÍ≥ºÌï¥ÏÑú Í∞ôÏùÄ stale path Î•º Îã§Ïãú Î∞ïÏïÑÎÑ£Îäî cycle** Ïù¥Îùº Ìö®Í≥º ÏóÜÏùå. v0.9.5 orbstack Í≤ÄÏ¶ùÏùÄ fresh-setup Îßå exercise ÌñàÍ≥Ý frozen-roster scenario miss.

Fix: skip ÎåÄÏãÝ **byte-preserving in-place replace**. 6 ÎùºÏö¥Îìú codex destructive-probe Î°ú Îã§ÏùåÏùÑ Î™®Îëê ÌÜµÍ≥º:

- top-level Îßå Îß§Ïπ≠ (quote ÏïàÏóê nested `NAME=‚Ä¶` substring Î¨¥ÏòÅÌñ•)
- `$VAR` / `${VAR}` / `$(cmd)` / `` `cmd` `` expansion Î≥¥Ï°¥ (Í¥ÄÎÝ® ÏóÜÎäî token Ïùò expansion Ïïà Ï£ΩÏûÑ)
- `$()` / `` ` ` `` / `${}` ÏïàÏóê `NAME=` Í∞Ä ÏûàÏñ¥ÎèÑ opaque value Î°ú Ï≤òÎ¶¨ (literal `)` / `}` Í∞Ä ÎÇòÏôÄÎèÑ close Ïïà Î¥Ñ)
- duplicate dedup (last-wins shell semantic Íπ®ÏßÄÏßÄ ÏïäÍ≤å Î™®ÎìÝ occurrence ÍµêÏ≤¥)
- ÏÉà canonical value Îäî `shlex.quote` Î°ú hard-quote (literal `$` Îì± ÏïàÏÝÑ)

`agent-bridge upgrade --apply` + `migrate isolation v2 --apply` + agent restart Ìïú Î≤à ÏúºÎ°ú ÏûêÎèô ÌöåÎ≥µ.

### Process ‚Äî codex destructive-probe 6 ÎùºÏö¥Îìú (operator-authorized round-cap waiver)

Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê ÏûêÏú® ÏúÑÏûÑ: "Ï∫° Í∞ôÏùÄÍ±∞ ÌïÑÏöî ÏóÜÏñ¥ ÎêÝÎñÑÍπåÏßÄ ÏôÑÏÝÑÌïòÍ≤å ÏôÑÎ£åÌï¥". CLAUDE.md Ïùò 3-round cap Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅ waiver ÌõÑ 6 ÎùºÏö¥Îìú ÏßÑÌñâ. Í∞Å ÎùºÏö¥Îìú Î™®Îëê ÏÉà vector catch (ÎßàÏßÄÎßâ 2 ÎùºÏö¥ÎìúÎäî hardening, Ï≤òÏùå 4 ÎùºÏö¥ÎìúÎäî production-relevant):

| ÎùºÏö¥Îìú | codex catch | Î∂ÑÎ•ò |
|---|---|---|
| r1 | skip-if-name-present false-positive | critical (operator hit) |
| r2 | regex global subn Í∞Ä quoted value ÏïàÏùò nested NAME= ÎèÑ ÎçÆÏùå | production-relevant |
| r3 | shlex.split + shlex.quote round-trip Í∞Ä `$VAR` expansion Ï£ΩÏûÑ | production-relevant |
| r4 | byte-preserving walker Í∞Ä `$()` / `` ` ` `` / `${}` Ïïà Î¥Ñ | production-relevant |
| r5 | `_skip_paren_subst` Í∞Ä `${...}` Ïïà honor ‚Üí brace-default Ïùò literal `)` Î°ú paren Ï°∞Í∏∞ close | hardening |
| r6 | `_skip_paren_subst` + `_skip_brace_subst` mutual-recurse Ï∂îÍ∞Ä, `$/${/backtick` Î™®ÎìÝ nesting opaque | hardening (closes surface) |

Í∞Å ÎùºÏö¥ÎìúÏùò fix Îäî 21 destructive probe matrix Î°ú bash-eval round-trip Í≤ÄÏ¶ù (Homebrew bash 5+). `frozen-roster motivating case ‚Üí fresh setup ‚Üí empty prefix ‚Üí idempotent same-value ‚Üí partial-name overlap ‚Üí quoted-stale ‚Üí value-with-space ‚Üí duplicate dedup ‚Üí quote-context (probe 9) ‚Üí $VAR (10) ‚Üí literal $ in new value (11) ‚Üí backtick no NAME (12) ‚Üí mixed quoting (13) ‚Üí $() (14) ‚Üí backtick (15) ‚Üí ${} (16) ‚Üí nested $() (17) ‚Üí $() in dquote (18) ‚Üí codex r5 reproducer (19) ‚Üí ${} containing $() (20) ‚Üí backtick in ${} (21)`.

### Process lesson ‚Äî false-positive Ïùò ÏùòÎØ∏

v0.9.5 Í∞Ä false-positive ÏòÄÎçò Ïù¥ÏúÝÎäî orbstack Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÏãúÎÇòÎ¶¨Ïò§Í∞Ä fresh-setup Îßå (= operator Í∞Ä Î¶¨ÎàÖÏä§ ÏÑúÎ≤ÑÏóêÏÑú Ìïú Î≤àÎèÑ Î≥∏ ÏÝÅ ÏóÜÎäî ÏÉÅÌÉú) Îã§Î§òÍ∏∞ ÎïåÎ¨∏. Ïö¥ÏòÅ Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏Ïùò frozen-roster ÏÉÅÌÉú (= operator Ïùò actual state) Î•º Ïû¨ÌòÑ Ïïà ÌñàÏùå. Îã§Ïùå release process ÏóêÎäî release-PR codex review brief Ïóê "operator's current production state Î•º reproducer Î°ú Î™ÖÏãú" Ìï≠Î™© Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ÌïÑÏöî. v0.9.5 CHANGELOG Ïùò "Verified on Linux" ÌëúÎäî fresh-setup row Î°ú ÌïúÏÝï ‚Üí frozen-roster row Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌñàÎã§Î©¥ release ÏÝÑ catch ÌñàÏùÑ Í≤É.

### Fixed (#776 ‚Äî refs #771)

- `lib/bridge-state.sh:bridge_claude_launch_with_channel_state_dirs` ‚Äî env-prefix Ïùò `<NAME>=value` assignment Î•º quote-state-aware top-level walker Î°ú ÏãùÎ≥Ñ ÌõÑ byte-preserving in-place replace. 4 helper (`_skip_dquote`, `_skip_paren_subst`, `_skip_backtick`, `_skip_brace_subst`) mutual-recurse Î°ú nested substitution / quote Î™®Îëê opaque Ï≤òÎ¶¨. matching Ïïà Îêú token Ïùò raw bytes (Ìè¨Ìï® `$VAR` / `$()` / backtick Îì± expansion) Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú ÌÜµÍ≥º. matching token Îßå `f"{name}={shlex.quote(value)}"` Î°ú substitute (last-wins shell semantic ÏúÑÌï¥ Î™®ÎìÝ occurrence ÍµêÏ≤¥).

### Verified on Linux (orbstack Oracle Linux 9)

| Test case | Original | Without fix | With v0.9.6 fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| Frozen-roster `TEAMS_STATE_DIR=/stale/.teams` | `/stale/.teams` | restart Ïãú stale ÌÜµÍ≥º | **`/workdir/.teams` Î°ú ÍµêÏ≤¥ ‚úì** |
| Fresh-setup (no existing TEAMS) | append OK | (regression-clean) | **append fires ‚úì** |
| Quoted-value Ïïà nested NAME= | (n/a) | global subn Ïù¥ nested ÎèÑ ÎçÆÏùå (r2 vector) | **opaque ‚úì** |
| `OTHER=$HOME` ÏôÄ Í∞ôÏùÄ token | (n/a) | shlex round-trip Ïù¥ `'$HOME'` ÏúºÎ°ú hard-quote (r3 vector) | **expansion Î≥¥Ï°¥ ‚úì** |
| `$()` / backtick / `${}` Ïïà NAME= | (n/a) | walker Í∞Ä Ïïà Î¥Ñ (r4 vector) | **opaque ‚úì** |
| `${UNSET:-) NAME=...}` (literal `)`) | (n/a) | paren Ï°∞Í∏∞ close (r5 vector) | **brace-default opaque ‚úì** |

### Known carry-over (v0.9.7 Ìä∏Îûô ÌõÑÎ≥¥)

- **#772** Stop hook ‚Üí agent-bridge CLI ‚Üí mkdir BRIDGE_STATE_DIR denied (isolated uid). non-blocking Ïù¥ÏßÄÎßå stderr spam.
- **#767** daemon inbox-nudge Ìè≠Ï£º (no dedup, no busy-aware gate).
- **runtime_ready false-positive**: `bridge_agent_channel_runtime_ready_for_item` Ïù¥ file/key presence Îßå Ï≤¥ÌÅ¨, Ïã§ÏÝú server LISTEN Ïïà Î¥Ñ. v0.9.6 fix Í∞Ä server bind ÏÝïÏÉÅÌôîÌïòÏßÄÎßå detect-future-failure ÏúÑÌï¥ LISTEN check Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÎäî Î≥ÑÎèÑ enhancement.
- v0.9.3 residual #B (creds.json ACL mask `---`) + #C (state/agents/<X>/ mode 2750) ‚Äî root cause ÎØ∏ÌôïÏÝï.

## [0.9.5] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

### Highlight ‚Äî #771 Teams server N-of-1 spawn Ï¢ÖÍ≤∞ + writer symlink hardening

`agent-bridge upgrade --apply` Î°ú ÏûêÎèô Î∞òÏòÅ. v0.9.x layout/contract Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ÏóÜÏùå. Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏ÏóêÏÑú #771 Î≥∏ ÏÝÅ ÏûàÎã§Î©¥ v0.9.5 ÏÝÅÏö© ÌõÑ `migrate isolation v2 --apply` + agent restart Ìïú Î≤à ÏúºÎ°ú ÏûêÎèô ÌöåÎ≥µ.

### Process ‚Äî orbstack Linux end-to-end Í≤ÄÏ¶ù + codex destructive-probe 3 ÎùºÏö¥Îìú

Ïù¥Î≤à release Îäî v0.9.4 Ïùò process commitment ("isolation/migrate/upgrade path ÎßåÏßÄÎäî release Îäî orbstack Linux Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÏùòÎ¨¥") Îëê Î≤àÏß∏ ÏÝÅÏö©. PR #774 Ïùò codex review Í∞Ä destructive-probe brief Î°ú 3 ÎùºÏö¥Îìú:

Í∞Å ÎùºÏö¥ÎìúÏùò fix Î™®Îëê orbstack Oracle Linux 9 VM ÏóêÏÑú Ïã§Ï∏° Í≤ÄÏ¶ù. Ïö¥ÏòÅ Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏Î°ú release Í∞ÄÍ∏∞ ÏÝÑ ÏΩîÎìú Í≤∞Ìï®ÏùÑ ÏÇ¨ÏÝÑ Ï∞®Îã®.

### Fixed (#774 ‚Äî refs #771)

- `lib/bridge-isolation-v2-reapply.sh:bridge_isolation_v2_reapply_one_agent` Í∞Ä recursive perm walk + ACL strip ÏßÅÌõÑ `bridge_write_linux_agent_env_file "$agent" "$_env_file"` Ìò∏Ï∂ú Ï∂îÍ∞Ä. writer Í∞Ä live roster + ÌòÑÏû¨ `bridge_agent_workdir` (BRIDGE_AGENT_ROOT_V2 honor + `/workdir` append) Î°ú launch_cmd Ïû¨Í≥ÑÏÇ∞Ìï¥ÏÑú cached `BRIDGE_AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD` refresh. mktemp + cmp short-circuit ÏúºÎ°ú stable content Ïãú mtime/ctime Î≥¥Ï°¥ (action row `ok:already-canonical`). helper-not-loaded Ïãú explicit `error:helper_not_loaded` action row + errors_file append (load-order regression visibility).

- channel `.env` per-path assert mode 0640 ‚Üí 0660 (`workdir/.teams/.env`, `workdir/.ms365/.env`). v2 design contract Ïùò group rw Î™ÖÏãú. doc table (lib/bridge-isolation-v2-reapply.sh:57-59 + 1085-1090) ÎèôÍ∏∞Ìôî.

### Hardening (#774, security ‚Äî defense in depth)

- `lib/bridge-agents.sh:bridge_write_linux_agent_env_file` Í∞Ä `cat >"$file"` redirect ÏßÅÏÝÑ `[[ -L "$file" ]]` Ï≤¥ÌÅ¨ Ï∂îÍ∞Ä. Î∞úÍ≤¨ Ïãú `bridge_linux_sudo_root rm -f` (ÎòêÎäî plain rm) Î°ú symlink ÏÝúÍ±∞. **post-rm verify ÌõÑ symlink Í∞Ä ÏÇ¥ÏïÑÎÇ®ÏïòÏúºÎ©¥** (sudo unavailable / parent dir unwritable) `bridge_warn` + `return 1` ‚Äî ÏÝàÎåÄ redirect write ÌÜµÍ≥º Ïïà Ìï®. Ïù¥ÏÝÑÏóî `[[ -O "$file" ]]` Í∞Ä link target (controller-owned) ÏúºÎ°ú follow ÎêòÏñ¥ rm branch skip ‚Üí cat Ïù¥ link ÌÜµÌï¥ controller ÌååÏùº (e.g. `~/.claude/.credentials.json`, Îã§Î•∏ agent env) ÎçÆÏñ¥Ïì∞Îäî vector Ï°¥Ïû¨. v0.9.5 Ïùò ÏÉà reapply regen call Ïù¥ Ïù¥ vector ÎÖ∏Ï∂úÏãúÏº∞ÏùÑ Í≤ÉÏù¥Îùº ÏòàÎ∞©ÏÝÅ fix. Î™®ÎìÝ writer caller Í∞Ä ÌòúÌÉù (defense in depth).

|---|---|---|---|

| .env post-strip group bit | `group::---` | controller unreadable | **group rw (mode 0660) ‚úì** |

## [0.9.4] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

### Highlight ‚Äî v0.9.3 regression fix (chgrp_setgid_recursive Í∞Ä plugin script exec bit Ï£ΩÏûÑ)

`v0.9.4` Îäî v0.9.3 PR #768 Í∞Ä ÎèÑÏûÖÌïú destructive ÌöåÍ∑ÄÎ•º Ï¢ÖÍ≤∞ÌïúÎã§. v0.9.3 Ïùò `bridge_isolation_v2_chgrp_setgid_recursive` Ïùò file_mode=0660 blanket chmod Í∞Ä `agents/<X>/home/.claude/plugins/cache/.../scripts/*.sh` (originally 0750) Ïùò exec bit Î•º Ï£ΩÏó¨ÏÑú SessionStart hook fail (`crm-mcp-token-sync.sh: Permission denied`) Î∞úÌôî. Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûêÍ∞Ä v0.9.3 ÏÝÅÏö© ÏßÅÌõÑ Î∞úÍ≤¨ ÌõÑ `chmod g+rx` ÏõåÌÅ¨Ïñ¥ÎùºÏö¥ÎìúÎ°ú unblock. v0.9.4 Í∞Ä helper ÏûêÏ≤¥Ïùò destructive contract Î•º ÏàòÏÝïÌï¥ÏÑú ÏõåÌÅ¨Ïñ¥ÎùºÏö¥Îìú Î∂àÌïÑÏöîÌôî.

`agent-bridge upgrade --apply` Î°ú ÏûêÎèô Î∞òÏòÅ. v0.9.x isolation-v2 layout / contract Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ÏóÜÏùå.

### Process change ‚Äî release ÏÝÑ orbstack Linux Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÏùòÎ¨¥Ìôî

Ïù¥Î≤à v0.9.4 Í∞Ä Ï≤òÏùåÏúºÎ°ú **release Î®∏ÏßÄ ÏÝÑ orbstack Oracle Linux 9 VM ÏóêÏÑú end-to-end Ïû¨ÌòÑ + fix Í≤ÄÏ¶ù** ÏùÑ Í±∞Ï≥§Îã§. Ïù¥ÏÝÑ v0.9.1/v0.9.2/v0.9.3 Í∞Ä Î™®Îëê darwin tempdir Îßå Î≥¥Í≥Ý release ÌñàÍ≥Ý Í∑∏ Í≤∞Í≥º release ÌõÑ Ïö¥ÏòÅ Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏ÏóêÏÑú ÌöåÍ∑ÄÍ∞Ä Ï≤òÏùå Î∞úÍ≤¨ÎêòÎäî Ìå®ÌÑ¥Ïù¥ Î∞òÎ≥µÎê®. v0.9.4 Î∂ÄÌÑ∞:
- isolation/migrate/upgrade path ÎßåÏßÄÎäî Î≥ÄÍ≤ΩÏùÄ orbstack VM Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÏùòÎ¨¥
- codex review brief Ïóê destructive-change probe (executable / setuid-setgid / symlink / xattr ÏòÅÌñ•) Ìï≠Î™© Ï∂îÍ∞Ä
- helper Ìï®ÏàòÏùò implicit destructive contract Îäî caller ÎßàÎã§ trace ÌõÑ Ìò∏Ï∂ú ÏÇ¨Ïù¥Ìä∏ ÏòÅÌñ• Î™ÖÏãú

### Fixed (#770 ‚Äî refs #746, refs #768)

- `lib/bridge-isolation-v2.sh:bridge_isolation_v2_chgrp_setgid_recursive` Í∞Ä file Ïùò mode Î•º numeric `chmod $file_mode` (e.g. literal 0660) Î°ú blanket ÏÝÅÏö©ÌïòÎçò destructive contract Î•º `X` (uppercase, "x only if dir or already has any exec") ÌôúÏö©Ìïú symbolic chmod Î°ú ÍµêÏ≤¥. translation table:

  | numeric | symbolic |
  |---|---|
  | 0660 | `u-s,g-s,g+rwX,o-rwx` |
  | 0640 | `u-s,g-s,g+rX,g-w,o-rwx` |
  | 0600 | `u-s,g-s,g-rwx,o-rwx` |

  `u-s,g-s` Îäî ÏÝïÍ∑ú file ÏóêÏÑú setuid/setgid bit Î™ÖÏãúÏÝÅ ÏÝúÍ±∞ (privilege escalation ÌëúÎ©¥ Î∞©Ïñ¥ ‚Äî codex r2 catch). user perm ÎØ∏Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω (file owner = agent UID Í∞Ä Ïù¥ÎØ∏ rw Î≥¥Ïû•). dir ÏùÄ literal numeric `dir_mode` Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú (e.g. 2770 Ïùò setgid Îäî ÏùòÎèÑÏÝÅ ‚Äî ÏãÝÍ∑ú file group ÏÉÅÏÜç).

- `lib/bridge-isolation-v2.sh:bridge_isolation_v2_verify_chgrp_setgid_recursive` Ïùò file mode sample Í≤ÄÏÇ¨Î•º mask `& 0666` Î°ú Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ‚Äî exec-aware. Ïù¥ÏÝÑÏóî exec-preserved file (e.g. 0770) Ïù¥ expected file_mode (0660) ÏôÄ mismatch ‚Üí return 1 ‚Üí caller Ïóê false-positive (codex r1 catch). masked ÎπÑÍµêÎäî "did chmod run at all" Ïùò rw bit Í≤ÄÏ¶ùÏùÑ ÏúÝÏßÄÌïòÎ©¥ÏÑú exec bit Î≥¥Ï°¥ÏùÑ ÌóàÏö©.

### Verified on Linux (orbstack Oracle Linux 9)

| File | Original | After v0.9.3 (regression) | After v0.9.4 (fixed) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plugin script | 0750 | **0660 NOT-EXECUTABLE** | **0770 EXECUTABLE** ‚úì |
| CLAUDE.md | 0640 | 0660 | 0660 |
| MEMORY-SCHEMA | 0600 | 0660 | 0660 |
| Setuid file | 4640 | (untested) | **0660 STRIPPED** ‚úì |
| Setgid file | 2640 | (untested) | **0660 STRIPPED** ‚úì |
| verify rc | (always 1 with exec files) | 1 false-positive | **0** ‚úì |

### Known carry-over (v0.9.5 Ìä∏Îûô ÌõÑÎ≥¥)

- **#772** Stop hook ‚Üí agent-bridge CLI ‚Üí `mkdir BRIDGE_STATE_DIR` denied (Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê Í≤©Î¶¨ user uid). non-blocking Ïù¥ÏßÄÎßå stderr spam (stop ÏÇ¨Ïù¥ÌÅ¥Îãπ 3Ìöå Ïù¥ÏÉÅ). Í∞ôÏùÄ isolation-v2 perm ÌÅ¥ÎûòÏä§, Îã§Î•∏ surface (hook layer).
- **#771** Teams plugin server N-of-1 spawn ‚Äî 4 ÏóêÏù¥ÏÝÑÌä∏ Îì±Î°ùÏù∏Îç∞ 1Í∞úÎßå LISTEN. Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûêÏùò sales_sean / dev_mun / sales_choi Teams Î©îÏãúÏßÄ silent drop. (channel layer)
- **#767** daemon inbox-nudge Ìè≠Ï£º (no dedup, no busy-aware gate). transcript Ïò§Ïóº, Í∏∞Îä• Ï∞®Îã® ÏóÜÏùå. (daemon scheduler layer)
- v0.9.3 Î∞úÍ≤¨ ÌöåÍ∑Ä #B (creds.json ACL mask `---`) + #C (state/agents/<X>/ mode 2750) ‚Äî root cause ÎØ∏ÌôïÏÝï, v0.9.3 patch scope Ïô∏. Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê ÏõåÌÅ¨Ïñ¥ÎùºÏö¥ÎìúÎ°ú unblock ÏÉÅÌÉú.

Ïù¥ÏÉÅ 4Í±¥ Î™®Îëê v0.9.5 ÏÇ¨Ïù¥ÌÅ¥ÏóêÏÑú ÎèôÏùºÌïú process (orbstack Ïû¨ÌòÑ ‚Üí ÏÝïÎ∞Ä fix ‚Üí Í≤ÄÏ¶ù) Î°ú Ï≤òÎ¶¨.

## [0.9.3] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

### Highlight ‚Äî REAL #746 fix (the v0.9.1 / v0.9.2 attempts hit the wrong code path)

`v0.9.3` Îäî #746 (v0.7‚Üív0.8 migrated Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏Ïùò workdir ÎÇ¥Î∂Ä ÌååÏùº perm drift) Ïùò **Ïã§ÏÝú root cause** Î•º Ïû°ÎäîÎã§. v0.9.1 (#749) ÏôÄ v0.9.2 Ïùò 13 PR audit wave Í∞Ä Î™®Îëê #746 Ïóê fix Î•º ÏãúÎèÑÌñàÏßÄÎßå Ïö¥ÏòÅ Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏ÏóêÏÑú ÎèôÏûë Ïïà Ìï®Ïù¥ ÌôïÏù∏ÎêêÍ≥Ý, Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê ÏßÑÎã® ÎÅùÏóê **dispatch Í≤ΩÎ°ú Ï∞®Ïù¥** Í∞Ä root cause Î°ú ÌôïÏÝïÎê®.

ÌïµÏã¨: `agent-bridge migrate isolation v2 --apply` (Í≥µÎ∞± form, Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûêÍ∞Ä production ÏóêÏÑú Ïì∞Îäî repair tool) Îäî `bridge_isolation_v2_reapply_one_agent` Î°ú dispatch ÎêòÎ©∞, Ïù¥ Ìï®ÏàòÏùò target path ÌÖåÏù¥Î∏îÏùÄ writable-sub ÎîîÎÝâÌÜÝÎ¶¨Îì§ (`workdir/`, `runtime/`, ...) ÏûêÏ≤¥ÏôÄ hand-picked ÌååÏùº Î¶¨Ïä§Ìä∏ (`.teams/.env`, `.ms365/.env`, `agent-env.sh`, `launch-secrets.env`, `.claude/`) Îßå assert. **workdir ÎÇ¥Î∂Ä ÌååÏùºÎì§ (CLAUDE.md, MEMORY-SCHEMA.md, memory/*, SOUL.md) ÏùÄ ÏÝàÎåÄ Ïïà ÎßåÏßê.** v0.9.1 #749 Í∞Ä Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌïú verify+sudo-retry Îäî `bridge_isolation_v2_chgrp_setgid_recursive` Ïóê Îì§Ïñ¥Í∞îÎäîÎç∞, Ïù¥ helper Îäî `bridge_isolation_v2_migrate_normalize_layout` ÏóêÏÑúÎßå Ìò∏Ï∂ú ‚Äî `agent-bridge migrate isolation-v2 apply` (ÌïòÏù¥Ìîà form, ÏÝÑÏ≤¥ ÎßàÏù¥Í∑∏ÎÝàÏù¥ÏÖò ÎèÑÍµ¨) path Îßå Ïª§Î≤Ñ. Îëê CLI Í∞Ä ÏôÑÏÝÑÌûà Îã§Î•∏ Ìï®ÏàòÎ°ú Î∂ÑÍ∏∞ÎêòÎäî Í±∏ #749 Í∞Ä Ïù∏ÏßÄ Î™ª Ìï®.

Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê ÏßÑÎã® Îç∞Ïù¥ÌÑ∞ (post-v0.9.2): `grep -c bridge_isolation_v2_verify_chgrp_setgid_recursive lib/bridge-isolation-v2.sh` = 3, `grep -c workdir-verify lib/bridge-isolation-v2-migrate.sh` = 3 (ÏΩîÎìúÎäî ÎîîÏä§ÌÅ¨Ïóê Îì§Ïñ¥ÏôîÏùå), Í∑∏Îü¨ÎÇò `migrate isolation v2 --apply` Ï∂úÎÝ•Ïùò workdir-verify ÎùºÏù∏ Ïàò = 0 (Ìò∏Ï∂ú Ïïà Îê®). ÏãúÎÇòÎ¶¨Ïò§ C ÌôïÏÝï ‚Äî ÏΩîÎìúÎäî ÏûàÏßÄÎßå Ìò∏Ï∂ú dispatch Í∞Ä Îã§Î•∏ Î∂ÑÍ∏∞Î°ú ÎπÝÏßê.

`agent-bridge upgrade --apply` Î°ú ÏûêÎèô Î∞òÏòÅ. v0.9.x isolation-v2 layout Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ÏóÜÏùå. Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏Í∞Ä #746 cascade Î•º Î≥¥ÏïòÎã§Î©¥ Î≥∏ v0.9.3 ÏÝÅÏö© ÌõÑ `agent-bridge migrate isolation v2 --apply` Ìïú Î∞©Ïóê ÏûêÎèô ÌöåÎ≥µ.

### Fixed (#768 ‚Äî refs #746)

- `lib/bridge-isolation-v2-reapply.sh:bridge_isolation_v2_reapply_one_agent` Ïóê writable-sub recursive ÏÝïÍ∑úÌôî Ï∂îÍ∞Ä (47 LOC, single function). per-path assert ÏßÅÌõÑ + ACL strip ÏßÅÏÝÑ Íµ¨Í∞ÑÏóê ÏÇΩÏûÖ. `bridge_isolation_v2_chgrp_setgid_recursive "$agent_grp" 2770 0660 "$agent_root/$sub"` Ìò∏Ï∂úÏùÑ `home / workdir / runtime / logs / requests / responses` Í∞ÅÍ∞ÅÏóê ÎåÄÌï¥ ÏàòÌñâ. apply Î™®ÎìúÏóêÏÑÝ Ïã§ÏÝú chgrp+chmod, check/dry-run Î™®ÎìúÏóêÏÑÝ `would` / `drift` action row Îßå emit. helper Ìò∏Ï∂úÏùÄ #749 Ïùò verify+sudo-retry ÏûêÎèô ÏÉÅÏÜç ‚Äî drift Í∞êÏßÄ Ïãú sudo retry, Í∑∏ÎûòÎèÑ drift Î©¥ Î™ÖÎ£åÌïú `bridge_warn`. per-sub Ïã§Ìå®Îäî best-effort (errors_file append + record_action error, Îã§Ïùå sub Î°ú continue) ‚Äî repair tool Ïùò "fix what you can, surface what failed" Ï≤ÝÌïôÍ≥º ÏùºÏπò.

### Reproduction + verification

darwin tempdir BRIDGE_HOME ÏóêÏÑú Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê Ï¶ùÏÉÅ ÏÝïÌôïÌûà Ïû¨ÌòÑ (workdir DIR ÏùÄ canonical 2770/ab-agent-X, ÎÇ¥Î∂Ä contents Îäî 0640/0750 + ÏûòÎ™ªÎêú group). pre-fix Ïùò `migrate isolation v2 --apply` Îäî contents Î•º Ïïà Í±¥ÎìúÎ¶º ‚Üí Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê production Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏ Í≤∞Í≥ºÏôÄ 100% ÏùºÏπò. patched source Îäî recursive Ìò∏Ï∂úÏù¥ contents Î•º ÏÝïÏÉÅ ÏÝïÍ∑úÌôî (Linux Í≤ÄÏ¶ù Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏ÏóêÏÑú post-merge ÏßÑÌñâ).

## [0.9.2] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

`v0.9.2` Îäî v0.9.1 (`c3a09a0`, 2026-05-09 13:10 KST) Î®∏ÏßÄ ÏßÅÌõÑ ÎèôÏùº Ìå®ÌÑ¥Ïùò silent-failure / set-e-cascade / redirection-leak ÏùÑ codex-rescue + Î°úÏª¨ grep ÏúºÎ°ú ÏΩîÎìúÎ≤ÝÏù¥Ïä§ ÏÝÑÏ≤¥ audit Ìïú Í≤∞Í≥º (umbrella #752) 13 PR ÏùÑ 4 wave Î°ú Î≥ëÎÝ¨ Ï≤òÎ¶¨Ìïú hotfix Î¨∂ÏùåÏù¥Îã§. Î™®ÎìÝ PR codex-rescue pair-review ÌÜµÍ≥º + squash-merge.

ÌïµÏã¨ trigger: v0.9.1 Ïùò #746 fix (`bridge_isolation_v2_chgrp_setgid_recursive` self-verify + sudo retry) ÏôÄ #747 fix (`scripts/wiki-v2-rebuild.sh` skip-and-continue) Í∞Ä land Ìïú ÏßÅÌõÑ, Í∞ôÏùÄ bug-class Í∞Ä Îã§Î•∏ ÏúÑÏπòÏóêÎèÑ ÏûàÎäîÏßÄ audit ÏßÑÌñâ. Î∞úÍ≤¨: 4 HIGH + 11 MED + 19 LOW. Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê Í≤∞ÏÝïÏóê Îî∞Îùº Î™®Îëê wave-orchestration ÏúºÎ°ú fix.

`agent-bridge upgrade --apply` Î°ú ÏûêÎèô Î∞òÏòÅ. v0.9.0 isolation-v2 layout Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ÏóÜÏùå.

Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê ÏßÑÎã® Í≤∞Í≥º ÏÉà verify ÏΩîÎìúÍ∞Ä Ïã§Ìñâ Í≤ΩÎ°úÏóê ÏßÑÏûÖÏ°∞Ï∞® Î™ª Ìï® ‚Äî root cause ÎØ∏ÌôïÏÝï (Í∞ÄÏÑ§: `bridge-upgrade.sh` Í∞Ä lib/bridge-isolation-v2*.sh Î•º live runtime Ïóê Î≥µÏÇ¨ Ïïà ÌñàÏùÑ Í∞ÄÎä•ÏÑ±). v0.9.2 ÏÝÅÏö© ÌõÑ Îã§Ïãú Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÌïÑÏöî. ÎØ∏Ìï¥Í≤∞ Ïãú v0.9.3 ÏóêÏÑú ÏÝïÎ∞Ä fix.

### HIGH (4 PR)

- **#753 H1 ‚Äî `lib/bridge-migration.sh:644-680`** unisolate Ïùò default-ACL ÏÝúÍ±∞ path Í∞Ä `find -exec setfacl ‚Ä¶ {} + 2>/dev/null \|\| true` Î°ú rc Î¨¥Ïãú + post-verify ÏóÜÏñ¥ÏÑú stale ACL ÏûîÏ°¥ Í∞ÄÎä•ÌñàÎçò Î¨∏ÏÝú. `find -print0 \| xargs -0 -r setfacl` (xargs rc ÏÝÑÌåå) + `getfacl --skip-base -R \| grep` post-verify + drift Ïãú sudo retry. (`443c514`)
- **#755 H2 ‚Äî `lib/bridge-isolation-v2.sh:878-908`** Linux setfacl scrub Ïùò self-verify+sudo-retry ‚Äî #749 Ïùò H2 Ïπ¥Ïö¥ÌÑ∞ÌååÌä∏. `getfacl --absolute-names --skip-base -R \| grep -E '^(user\|group\|default:user\|default:group):[^:]+:'` Î°ú residual ACL Í∞êÏßÄ ‚Üí sudo retry ‚Üí re-verify. (`fad0cbf`)
- **#754 H3 ‚Äî `lib/bridge-isolation-v2-migrate.sh:1664-1722`** `apply_for_upgrade` Ïùò marker-present idempotent skip Í≤ΩÎ°úÏóêÏÑú normalize_layout Ïã§Ìå®Í∞Ä warn-only ÏòÄÎçò contract break. rc Ï∫°Ï≤ò + `status:partial` JSON envelope (last_error / remediation ÌïÑÎìú Ìè¨Ìï®) + ÎπÑ-0 return. Î™®ÎìÝ envelope Ïóê `status` ÌïÑÎìú Ï∂îÍ∞Ä ‚Äî M10 (W3d) ÏóêÏÑú consume. (`ddbe5fa`)
- **#756 H4 ‚Äî `lib/bridge-migration.sh:138-148`** `bridge_migration_isolate --reapply` Ïùò `bridge_linux_prepare_agent_isolation` Ïã§Ìå® Ïãú warn-only ÏòÄÎçò contract break. `if !; then bridge_warn 'refusing to mark reapply complete'; return 1; fi` Î°ú fatal Ìôî. (`666954c`)

### MED (8 PR ‚Äî Bug-A cascade + Bug-B/C/D bundles)

- **#760 M4 ‚Äî `bridge-agent.sh:2116-2136`** `run_rerender_settings` Ïùò per-target ÌîÑÎ°úÎ∏å Ïã§Ìå®Í∞Ä ÌõÑÏÜç Î™®ÎìÝ ÌÉÄÍ≤ü rerender Î•º Ï£ΩÏù¥Îçò cascade. `if !; then bridge_warn; emit error JSON row via env-passed-vars python3 -c; failed_count++; continue; fi`. failed_count Í∞Ä Ìï®Ïàò rc Í≤∞ÏÝï ‚Üí upgrade caller Í∞Ä partial-failure Ïù∏ÏßÄ Í∞ÄÎä•. Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûêÍ∞Ä Î™®ÎìÝ agent Ïùò settings.local.json Ïù¥ malformed JSON Ïù∏ ÏºÄÏù¥Ïä§Î•º silent rc=0 Í∞Ä ÏïÑÎãàÎùº Î™ÖÎ£åÌïòÍ≤å Î≥¥Í≤å Îê®. (`2c9a536`)
- **#761 W3a ‚Äî `lib/bridge-tmux.sh:1138`** pending-attention lock PID write Ïùò redirection-order leak. `printf '%d' $$ >"$pid_file" 2>/dev/null` ‚Üí `printf '%d' $$ 2>/dev/null >"$pid_file"`. (`b13d859`)

## [0.9.1] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

### Highlight ‚Äî v0.9.0 post-upgrade hotfix wave (2 PR)

`v0.9.1` ÏùÄ v0.9.0 ÏÝÅÏö© Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏ÏóêÏÑú Í∞ôÏùÄ ÎÇÝ Î∞úÍ≤¨Îêú Îëê cascade Î•º Îã´ÎäîÎã§. ÌïµÏã¨ trigger: Ïö¥ÏòÅ Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏Í∞Ä `agent-bridge upgrade --apply` + `agent-bridge migrate isolation v2 --apply` Î•º Ï∞®Î°ÄÎ°ú ÌÜµÍ≥ºÌïòÍ≥Ý *exit 0 + all dir entries `ok`* Î•º Î≥¥ÏïòÏúºÎÇò, ÏÝïÏûë `agents/<X>/workdir/` ÏïàÏùò pre-existing ÌååÏùºÏùÄ v0.7‚Üív0.8 Ïù¥ÏÝÑ owner-group ÏùÑ Í∑∏ÎåÄÎ°ú ÏúÝÏßÄ ‚Üí controller user (`ec2-user`, `ab-agent-<X>` Í∑∏Î£π Î©§Î≤Ñ) Í∞Ä `workdir/CLAUDE.md` Î•º read Î™ª Ìï® ‚Üí ÌÜÝÏöîÏùº 06:00 KST `wiki-v2-rebuild` cron Ïù¥ PermissionError Î°ú abort, `set -euo pipefail` Í∞Ä sweep ÏÝÑÏ≤¥Î•º Ï£ΩÏûÑ ‚Üí Ìïú ÏóêÏù¥ÏÝÑÌä∏Ïùò perm drift Í∞Ä Îã§Î•∏ 5 ÏóêÏù¥ÏÝÑÌä∏Ïùò weekly index rebuild ÍπåÏßÄ silent miss ÏãúÌÇ§Îäî cascade.

`agent-bridge upgrade --apply` Î°ú ÏûêÎèô Î∞òÏòÅ. v0.9.0 isolation-v2 layout Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ÏóÜÏùå. v0.9.0 ÏÝÅÏö© Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏Í∞Ä Í∞ôÏùÄ cascade Î•º Î≥¥ÏïòÎã§Î©¥ Î≥∏ v0.9.1 ÏÝÅÏö© ÌõÑ Îã§Ïùå ÌÜÝÏöîÏùº cron slot (2026-05-16 06:00 KST) ÏóêÏÑú ÏûêÎèô ÌöåÎ≥µ.

### Fixed (#749 ‚Äî refs #746)

- `lib/bridge-isolation-v2.sh` Ïóê belt-and-braces Í∞ÄÎìú Îëê Í≤π Ï∂îÍ∞Ä. ÏÉà helper `bridge_isolation_v2_verify_chgrp_setgid_recursive(group, dir_mode, file_mode, root)` Í∞Ä tree Î•º walk ÌïòÎ©¥ÏÑú Î™®ÎìÝ dir/file Ïùò group Ïù¥ expected ÏôÄ ÏùºÏπòÌïòÎäîÏßÄ + ÌïòÎÇòÏùò sample dir / file Ïùò mode Í∞Ä expected ÏôÄ ÏùºÏπòÌïòÎäîÏßÄ ÌôïÏù∏ (mode ÎπÑÍµêÎäî `printf '%04o' "$((8#$mode))"` ÏÝïÍ∑úÌôîÎ°ú BSD/macOS `stat -f %A` ÏôÄ GNU `stat -c %a` parity). `bridge_isolation_v2_chgrp_setgid_recursive` Í∞Ä 4 find-exec passes ÏßÅÌõÑ self-verify Ìò∏Ï∂ú ‚Üí drift Í∞êÏßÄ Ïãú `sudo -n find ‚Ä¶ -exec chgrp/chmod {} +` (no direct-first) Î°ú retry ÌõÑ Ïû¨Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ‚Üí Ïó¨ÏÝÑÌûà drift Î©¥ Î™ÖÎ£åÌïú `bridge_warn` ÌõÑ return 1. Ïù¥ÏÝÑÏóî stderr-silenced direct-first Í∞Ä find Í∞Ä 0 Î∞òÌôòÌñàÏùåÏóêÎèÑ per-file chgrp Ïã§Ìå®Í∞Ä propagate Ïïà ÎêòÎäî ÌôòÍ≤Ω (Amazon Linux GNU findutils Îì±) ÏóêÏÑú silent no-op Î°ú ÎÅùÎÇòÍ≥Ý sudo retry path Í∞Ä ÎØ∏ÏßÑÏûÖÌñàÎã§. `lib/bridge-isolation-v2-migrate.sh:bridge_isolation_v2_migrate_normalize_layout` per-agent loop Ïóê `[migrate] workdir-verify ok|FAIL agent=<X> grp=<grp>` Î™ÖÏãú Î°úÍπÖ ‚Äî FAIL ‚Üí return 1 ‚Üí full apply path Ïùò `bridge_die` (v2 marker ÎØ∏ÏßÑÌñâ), upgrade path Îäî warn-only (operator Í∞Ä grep ÏúºÎ°ú ÏãùÎ≥Ñ Í∞ÄÎä•). rootless smoke regression Î¨¥ÏòÅÌñ• (caller's primary group + 2770/0660 tempdir tree ÏóêÏÑú verify Í∞Ä trivial pass).

### Fixed (#748 ‚Äî refs #747)

- `scripts/wiki-v2-rebuild.sh` Ïùò `mkdir -p "$(dirname "$live_db")"` + `: 2>/dev/null >> "$lock_file"` Îëê lock-init step ÏùÑ Í∞ôÏùÄ file Ïùò Í∏∞Ï°¥ `LOCK_BUSY` / `FAIL` / `VALIDATE_FAIL` / `SWAP_FAIL` block Í≥º ÎèôÏùºÌïú `if !; then log_audit; skipped++; continue; fi` shape ÏúºÎ°ú Í∞ÄÎìú. ÏÉà audit tag `MKDIR_FAIL` / `LOCK_INIT_FAIL`. bash redirection ÏàúÏÑú footgun ÌöåÌîº ‚Äî `: >> "$lock_file" 2>/dev/null` ÏùÄ `>>` open Ïã§Ìå® Ïãú bash Í∞Ä `2>/dev/null` ÏÝÅÏö© *ÏÝÑÏóê* `Permission denied` diagnostic ÏùÑ stderr Î°ú leak; `: 2>/dev/null >> "$lock_file"` Î°ú stderr Î•º Î®ºÏÝÄ redirect. `set -euo pipefail` + ERR trap Î≥¥Ï°¥. Îã®Ïùº ÏóêÏù¥ÏÝÑÌä∏Ïùò perm drift Í∞Ä weekly sweep ÏÝÑÏ≤¥Î•º Ï£ΩÏù¥Îçò cascade Ï¢ÖÍ≤∞ ‚Äî Ìïú ÏóêÏù¥ÏÝÑÌä∏Ïùò lock-init fail ÏùÄ Ìïú ÏóêÏù¥ÏÝÑÌä∏Ïùò skip Ïù¥Í≥Ý Í∑∏ Ïù¥ÏÉÅÏù¥ ÏïÑÎãò.

## [0.9.0] ‚Äî 2026-05-09

### Highlight ‚Äî isolation-v2 migration tooling + canonical state docs

`v0.9.0` ÏùÄ v0.7‚Üív0.8 isolated-agent transition Ïùò ownership/ACL drift Î•º ÏûêÎèô ÌöåÎ≥µÌïòÎäî ÏÉà CLI Î™ÖÎÝπÏùÑ Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌïòÍ≥Ý, Í∑∏ contract Î•º Ïö¥ÏòÅ Î¨∏ÏÑúÎ°ú Î™ÖÎ¨∏ÌôîÌïúÎã§. minor bump ÏÝïÎãπÌôî: ÏÉà CLI subcommand surface (`agent-bridge migrate isolation v2`).

ÌïµÏã¨ trigger: Ïù¥Ïäà #737 (Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûêÍ∞Ä Î≥∏ isolated agent Ïùò cascade ‚Äî `agents/<X>/.claude/` ÎàÑÎùΩ, plugin state files mode 0600 controller-Í∑∏Î£π, `/home/agent-bridge-<X>/.claude/` Í∞Ä root ÏÜåÏúÝ + named-user ACL with controller Îßå + agent Î≥∏Ïù∏ access ÎÅäÍπÄ Îì±). v0.7 ÏãúÏÝàÏùò transitional ACL ÏûîÏû¨Í∞Ä v0.8 hard-cut (PR #641 ‚Äî KNOWN_ISSUES ¬ß16) ÌõÑÏóêÎèÑ ÏÝïÎ¶¨ Ïïà Îêú ÏÉÅÌÉúÍ∞Ä ÏßÑÏßú Í≤∞Ìï®Ïù¥ÏóàÎã§. v2 contract Îäî Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê mental model "kill ACLs" ÏôÄ ÏÝïÌôïÌûà ÏùºÏπò ‚Äî layout ÏûêÏ≤¥Ïóî named-user POSIX ACL surface 0Í∞ú. ÏÉà Î™ÖÎÝπ `migrate isolation v2 --apply` Í∞Ä Ïù¥ ÏûîÏû¨Î•º ÏûêÎèô strip + canonical state ÏÝÅÏö©.

`agent-bridge upgrade --apply` ÌõÑ isolated agent Í∞Ä `agents/<X>/...` cascade fail ÌïòÎ©¥:

```
agent-bridge migrate isolation v2 --check        # drift report
agent-bridge migrate isolation v2 --dry-run      # planned actions
agent-bridge migrate isolation v2 --apply        # apply fix
agent-bridge agent start <agent>                 # restart
```

### Added (#742 ‚Äî refs #737 Q1/Q2/Q3) ‚òÖ Highlight

- ÏÉà CLI subcommand `agent-bridge migrate isolation v2 [--check|--dry-run|--apply] [--agent <name>] [--json]` (lib/bridge-isolation-v2-reapply.sh, ~880 LOC). Modes: `--check` Îäî drift report only, `--dry-run` ÏùÄ planned actions, `--apply` Îäî mutation. `agents/<agent>/*` (root:ab-agent-<agent> 2750, subdirs 2770, credentials 2750, agent-env.sh 0640, plugin state files 0640) + `/home/agent-bridge-<agent>/*` (agent Î≥∏Ïù∏ owner 0700, Î™®ÎìÝ named-user POSIX ACL strip) Îëò Îã§ cover. `~/.claude/.credentials.json` ÎØ∏ÌÑ∞Ïπò (PR #641 hard-cut ÌõÑ v2 contract Ïô∏ surface). non-Linux Ï¶âÏãú silent skip. macOS / non-Linux Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏Îäî Î¨¥ÏòÅÌñ•. idempotent ‚Äî Îëê Î≤àÏß∏ `--apply` Îäî ÏßÑÏÝïÌïú no-op (path-local stat + named-ACL canary). credentials/launch-secrets.env ÎèÑ canonical assertion. `--json` Ï∂úÎÝ• schema: per-agent `{agent, isolated, actions[], errors[]}` + top-level `total_agents` / `total_repaired`.

### Fixed (#741 ‚Äî refs #737 Q5)

- `bridge-setup.py` Ïùò `inspect_discord_dir` / `inspect_telegram_dir` / `inspect_teams_dir` Í∞Ä isolated agent Ïùò owner-only `.env` Î•º controller Î°ú read Ïãú Î∞úÏÉùÌïòÎçò PermissionError Î•º PR #718 family sudo-fallback Ìå®ÌÑ¥ÏúºÎ°ú Ìï¥ÏÜå. `_safe_read_env` / `_safe_load_json` / `_safe_path_check` / `_isolated_workdir_owner` / `_sudo_run_as` / `_parse_dotenv_text` helpers (Î™®Îëê bridge-setup.py ÎÇ¥Î∂Ä ÎØ∏Îü¨). sudo ÎØ∏ÏÑ§Ïπò Ïãú rc 127 ‚Üí Î™ÖÎ£åÌïú `PermissionError` ("sudo not available; cannot read X as Y. Recovery requires either installing sudo or running this command as the agent user directly.") + cause chain (`from exc`). non-Linux Îäî `_isolated_workdir_owner` Í∞Ä None Î∞òÌôòÏúºÎ°ú fallback Î∂ÑÍ∏∞ ÎØ∏ÏßÑÏûÖ ‚Äî Í∏∞Ï°¥ ÎèôÏûë Î≥¥Ï°¥. ms365 inspect Îäî `bridge-setup.py` Ïóê ÏóÜÏùå (Î≥ÑÎèÑ surface). Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûêÍ∞Ä `agent-bridge setup teams <agent>` recovery path Î•º ÏÝïÏÉÅ ÏÇ¨Ïö© Í∞ÄÎä•.

### Fixed (#740 ‚Äî refs #737 Q6)

- `agent-bridge agent delete --purge-home` Í∞Ä isolated agent Ïùò *actual Linux home* (`/home/agent-bridge-<agent>/`) ÎèÑ ÏÝúÍ±∞. `getent passwd <user>` Î°ú ÏÝïÌôïÌïú home path Ï∂îÏ∂ú + regex guard `^/home/agent-bridge-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$` ÏúºÎ°ú Ïö¥ÏòÅÏûê home / system path Î≥¥Ìò∏. `bridge_linux_sudo_root rm -rf` ÏÇ¨Ïö© (root-owned). Ïã§Ìå® Ïãú warn Îßå (agent ÏûêÏ≤¥Îäî Ïù¥ÎØ∏ delete Îê®, fail-loud Í∏àÏßÄ). Linux user account ÏûêÏ≤¥ (`userdel`) ÏÝúÍ±∞Îäî scope-out (Î≥ÑÎèÑ follow-up). non-Linux short-circuit. retire Î∂ÑÍ∏∞ Î≥¥Ï°¥.

### Documentation (#739 ‚Äî refs #737)

- `OPERATIONS.md` Ïóê ÏÉà ÏÑπÏÖò "Isolation v2 canonical state and migration" ‚Äî canonical state table ($BRIDGE_DATA_ROOT ÌôòÍ≤ΩÎ≥ÄÏàò Î™ÖÏãú) + migration runbook + POSIX ACL Ïó≠ÌïÝ ÏÝïÎ¶¨. `KNOWN_ISSUES.md ¬ß16` Î≥¥Í∞ï ‚Äî "v0.7 ‚Üí v0.8 migration ACL leftovers" Î∂ÄÎ∂Ñ (manual recovery Î™ÖÎÝπ + ÏòàÏô∏ ÏóÜÎäî strip-only ÏÝïÏ±Ö). ÏãÝÍ∑ú `docs/agent-runtime/v2-isolation-migration.md` ‚Äî ÍπäÏù¥ ÏûàÎäî reference (drift signature, --check/--dry-run/--apply ÏÇ¨Ïö©Î≤ï, manual recovery, diagnostic Î™ÖÎÝπ). Î™®ÎìÝ docs `ACL preservation count: 0` / `strip-only pass` Ïñ∏Ïñ¥ ÌÜµÏùº ‚Äî PR #641 hard-cut contract ÏôÄ ÏùºÍ¥Ä (KNOWN_ISSUES ¬ß16 line 269-271, 288-292).

## [0.8.9] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

### Highlight ‚Äî second post-upgrade hotfix wave (5 PR)

`v0.8.9` ÏùÄ v0.8.8 ÏÝÅÏö© Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏ÏóêÏÑú Î∞úÍ≤¨Îêú Ï∂îÍ∞Ä cascade 4Í±¥ + Î≥ÑÎèÑ tool-policy hotfix 1Í±¥ÏùÑ Îã´ÎäîÎã§. ÌïµÏã¨: **PR #734 (closes #731)** Í∞Ä isolated agent Ïùò ÏóÖÍ∑∏ÎÝàÏù¥Îìú silent skip + JSONDecodeError Î•º Ï¢ÖÍ≤∞ÌïúÎã§ ‚Äî `bridge_agent_canonical_dir` Ïùò `cd -P` Ïù¥ isolated workdir ÏóêÏÑú PermissionError Ïùº Îïå `bridge_linux_sudo_root` fallback + ÏµúÏ¢Ö path passthrough, Í∑∏Î¶¨Í≥Ý `bridge-upgrade.sh` Ïùò `SHARED_SETTINGS_RERENDER_JSON` Îëê python heredoc Ïóê Îπà/non-JSON Î∞©Ïñ¥ (`sys.exit(0)` + WARN + `raw[:200]` preview). Ïù¥ Ïù¥ÏÝÑÏóî isolated agent host Ïùò ÏóÖÍ∑∏ÎÝàÏù¥ÎìúÍ∞Ä raw traceback + "rerender failed for one or more agents" Î°ú ÎÅùÎÇ¨Í≥Ý *Ïñ¥Îäê agent / Ïñ¥Îäê step* Ïù∏ÏßÄ Ïïå Ïàò ÏóÜÏóàÎã§.

Îã§Î•∏ 4 PR: librarian memory-daily Ïùò 8-day Îπà-Ïä¨Î°Ø backfill loop Î•º sender-gated self-signal filter Î°ú Ï¢ÖÍ≤∞ (#732 closes #728), bootstrap-memory-system Ïóê `--re-jitter` flag Ï∂îÍ∞ÄÌïòÏó¨ long-running install ÏóêÏÑú Î™®ÎìÝ memory-daily ÌÅ¨Î°ÝÏù¥ ÎèôÏùº minute ÏúºÎ°ú ÌöåÍ∑ÄÌñàÏùÑ Îïå collapse Í≤ÄÏ¶ù ÌõÑ Î∂ÑÏÇ∞ (#733 closes #729), v0.8 layout split ÌõÑ broken Îêú agent profile Ïùò shared-doc / skill symlink (workdir/COMMON-INSTRUCTIONS.md off-by-2, home/.claude/skills/X off-by-1) Ïû¨Ïó∞Í≤∞ helper Ï∂îÍ∞Ä (#735 closes #730), Í∑∏Î¶¨Í≥Ý Î≥ÑÎèÑ tool-policy hotfix Î°ú `agent-bridge config set` wrapper Í∞Ä path-argv gate Î•º ÏÝïÏÉÅ ÌÜµÍ≥ºÌïòÎèÑÎ°ù (#726).

`agent-bridge upgrade --apply` Î°ú ÏûêÎèô Î∞òÏòÅ. v0.8 isolation-v2 layout Î≥ÄÍ≤Ω ÏóÜÏùå. isolated agent Ìò∏Ïä§Ìä∏Í∞Ä v0.8.8 ÏÝÅÏö© Ïãú raw JSONDecodeError traceback ÏùÑ Î≥¥ÏïòÎã§Î©¥ Î≥∏ v0.8.9 ÏÝÅÏö© ÌõÑ Ìï¥Îãπ trace ÏÇ¨ÎùºÏßê.

## [0.8.7] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

### Highlight ‚Äî closes #708 v0.7‚Üív0.8 migration grep-flag-injection abort

`v0.8.6` shipped earlier today closing the v0.7‚Üív0.8 admin-pair migration chain. An operator running `bridge-upgrade.sh --apply` on a v0.7.8 install whose `agents/` directory contained a stray dir starting with `-` (e.g. `agents/--help` from a flag-typo'd pre-v0.8 `agent create` or a manual `mkdir`) hit `isolation-v2 migration failed (rc=1) reason=groups-ensure`. v0.8.7 closes that single finding with a 2-line defensive fix.

**Operators on v0.8.6: upgrade to v0.8.7.** No separate v0.8.6 stop required. If your v0.8.6 install already aborted with `reason=groups-ensure` and you have a `--`-prefixed orphan dir under `agents/`, move it under `backups/` (any name) and rerun `bridge-upgrade.sh --apply` from the v0.8.7 source.

### Fixed

- **#708 v0.7‚Üív0.8 migration aborted when an orphan agent dir name started with `-`** (`lib/bridge-isolation-v2-migrate.sh::bridge_isolation_v2_migrate_capture_all_agents_snapshot`): an operator running `bridge-upgrade.sh --apply` on a v0.7.8 install whose `$BRIDGE_AGENT_HOME_ROOT` contained a stray dir like `agents/--help` (created by an earlier flag-typo'd `agent create` on a pre-v0.8 install or a manual `mkdir`) hit `isolation-v2 migration failed (rc=1) reason=groups-ensure`. Root cause: the markerless directory walk called `grep -qFx "$name" "$roster_lookup"` without the `--` end-of-options separator. When `$name` started with `-`, GNU grep parsed it as a flag (`-q -F -x --help`), printed its help text to stdout (which `2>/dev/null` does not silence), and the curly-brace block's stdout was piped into `sort -u > "$snapshot"` ‚Äî so `state/migration/all-agents.snapshot` ended up containing ~70 lines of grep help text mixed with real agent ids. Downstream `ensure_groups_and_memberships` then ran `bridge_isolation_v2_agent_group_name` on the polluted lines, validation failed (e.g. `'                            ACTION is 'read' or 'skip'' has invalid chars`), the migration aborted, and `restart_agents` failed for every agent. v0.8.7 hotfix: (a) add `-*` to the case-filter denylist so `--`-prefixed dirs are skipped from the walk entirely; (b) add `--` to the `grep -qFx` invocation as defense-in-depth so any future callsite that drops the case filter cannot regress the same way. Operators on v0.8.6 with a stray `--`-prefixed orphan dir can move it under `backups/` (which the case filter already excluded) and rerun `bridge-upgrade.sh --apply` without manual intervention. Closes #708.

## [0.8.6] ‚Äî 2026-05-08

### Highlight ‚Äî closes 4 v0.7‚Üív0.8 admin-pair migration findings + bundles wave-orchestration

`v0.8.5` shipped on 2026-05-08 closing 16 OrbStack VM E2E findings. Within hours an operator running `agent-bridge migrate isolation-v2 dry-run` on a v0.7.8 install with the documented `bridge_ensure_admin_codex_pair` pattern (`patch` + `patch-dev` co-located on admin's workdir for shared SOUL/MEMORY/CLAUDE.md pair-programming) hit a chain of defects that hard-blocked the migration. v0.8.6 closes the chain:

1. **Migration preflight admin-pair whitelist** ‚Äî recognize the `<admin>-dev` shared-workdir pattern instead of flagging it as misalignment.
2. **`bridge_expand_user_path` helper sourcing** ‚Äî moved to `lib/bridge-core.sh` so the migrate dispatch chain doesn't fall through to a `command not found` false-negative.
3. **Public wrapper bypass arming** ‚Äî `agent-bridge upgrade` / `agent-bridge migrate` now arm the layout resolver bypass before sourcing `bridge-lib.sh`, gated on marker absence so already-migrated v0.8.x installs keep using normal marker resolution.
4. **`features.fast_mode=true` policy pinning** ‚Äî every codex agent launch (admin-pair backfill, isolated create, dynamic spawn, v0.7‚Üív0.8 migration) now pins fast_mode alongside codex_hooks, idempotently injected into already-rostered launch_cmds at runtime.

Plus the `wave-orchestration` skill is now bundled and auto-distributed to every Agent Bridge agent on bootstrap.

**Operators on v0.8.5: upgrade to v0.8.6.** The wrapper marker-gate ensures markered v0.8.x ‚Üí v0.8.6 upgrades take the normal marker path (no behavior change). Operators on v0.7.x with admin-pair patterns are now able to run `agent-bridge migrate isolation-v2 ...` / `agent-bridge upgrade --apply` directly from a v0.8.6 source checkout without hitting the v0.8.0 fail-fast self-referential remediation loop.

### Added

- **`wave-orchestration` skill bundled in upstream** (`.claude/skills/wave-orchestration/`, `lib/bridge-skills.sh::bridge_bootstrap_claude_shared_skills` + `bridge_isolated_home_shared_skill_names`): the shared parallel-PR-ship orchestration spine (brief writing ‚Üí `issue-fixer` dispatch into isolated git worktrees ‚Üí `codex-rescue` review for >300 LOC specialized work or orchestrator-direct review for mid-size ‚Üí squash-merge with structured `implement-ok` note ‚Üí cleanup) is now distributed to every Agent Bridge agent on bootstrap. Pre-Wave the skill lived only in operator-level `~/.claude/skills/` ‚Äî admin / dynamic / static agents needed manual symlinks. Now the same shared-skill bootstrap that already distributes `agent-bridge-runtime` / `cron-manager` / `memory-wiki` / `patch-permission-approval` also installs `wave-orchestration` so any agent that wakes can dispatch a wave with the same field-tested footgun catalog (8 documented footguns, brief template, codex-rescue setup recipe, 4 worked wave examples). The bundled `issue-fixer` agent (under the skill's `agents/` dir) is project-agnostic ‚Äî operators copy to `~/.claude/agents/issue-fixer.md` once for `subagent_type: "issue-fixer"` to be available, or fall back to `general-purpose`. Generalized from the operator-private Agent Bridge build: machine-specific paths (operator's plugin cache, `/Users/<u>/...`) replaced with portable `command -v codex` / `PATH=...` resolution; Agent Bridge integration section explains queue-based dispatch (`bridge-task.sh create --to <peer>`) as the alternative to `Agent` tool dispatch for codex peers without that tool.

### Fixed

- **v0.7‚Üív0.8 migration preflight rejected admin-pair shared-workdir installs** (`lib/bridge-isolation-v2-migrate.sh::bridge_isolation_v2_migrate_check_profile_home_overrides`, `lib/bridge-core.sh::bridge_expand_user_path`): two layered defects surfaced by an operator running `agent-bridge migrate isolation-v2 dry-run` on a v0.7.8 install with the documented PR #691 admin-pair pattern (`patch` + `patch-dev` co-located on the admin's workdir for shared SOUL/MEMORY/CLAUDE.md pair-programming). (a) The preflight rejected `<admin>-dev`'s explicit `BRIDGE_AGENT_PROFILE_HOME` pointing at the admin's workdir as a "misalignment", even though `bridge_ensure_admin_codex_pair` documents same-workdir as the entire point of the pair (`pair_workdir="$(bridge_agent_workdir "$admin")"` + `agent create --allow-shared-workdir`). The preflight wording was `[Í≤ΩÍ≥Ý]` but the same code path called `bridge_die` on `--apply`, hard-blocking the migration.
